title: Best Private MBBS Colleges in Uttar Pradesh (UP) 2026 – Fees, Ranking, Admission & Cutoff
content:
Best Private MBBS Colleges in Uttar Pradesh (UP) 2026 – Fees, Ranking, Admission & Cutoff

Uttar Pradesh has become one of India’s largest medical education hubs. With over 85 medical colleges and approximately 13,000 MBBS seats available, both government and private sectors contribute significantly. Consequently, thousands of students apply for admission every year.

The MBBS course duration is 5.5 years (including internship), and all colleges follow the guidelines of the National Medical Commission (NMC). The main gateway for admission is NEET 2026, a national-level entrance exam. Based on the scores in this exam, students are allotted colleges through the All India Quota and State Quota.

The role of private colleges is very important because it provides a huge part of total MBBS seats. There are limited seats in Government colleges and competition is very high, Therefore, private institutions become a practical option for many students.

Due to this reason choosing Best Private MBBS Colleges in Uttar Pradesh is a crucial decision. A good college can offer students a quality education, experienced faculty, modern infrastructure and strong clinical exposure. Due to the high patient flow in UP hospitals students get a good exposure to  real-life practical learning. 

Choosing the right private MBBS college will build your future career on a strong foundation. Therefore, it’s crucial to carefully evaluate a college’s ranking, fees, hospital facilities, and internship opportunities before enrolling.

Top Private MBBS Colleges in UP 2026 

Top private MBBS colleges in Uttar Pradesh for 2026 include SRMS Bareilly, Sharda University (Greater Noida), and Era’s Lucknow Medical College, offering high-quality clinical training and modern infrastructure. In colleges, annual fees generally range between ₹12–17 lakhs, while total course fees in top institutions can exceed ₹1 crore. Admission is based on NEET-UG score, and counselling is conducted through DGME UP.Below are the college’s list with fees:

Eligibility Criteria for MBBS in UP 

To be eligible for the MBBS in Uttar Pradesh (UP) 2026 candidates must qualify 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotechnology and English. With minimum 50% marks in PCB for (General), 40% (SC/ST/OBC)  and 45% (PwD), along with qualifying NEET UG 2026 is mandatory. Candidates must be at least 17 years of age as on 31 December 2026 (DOB on or before 31 December 2009). Registration on upneet.gov.in is also mandatory for counselling.

Key Eligibility Criteria for MBBS in UP 2026:

    • NEET 2026 Qualification: Mandatory, with minimum required percentile for the state merit list.
  • Minimum Marks Criteria (10+2):
    • General/UR: 50% aggregate in Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotech.
    • OBC/SC/ST: 40% aggregate in Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotech.
    • PwD: 45% aggregate in Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotech.
  • Age Requirement: Must be 17 years old by 31st December 2026 (born on or before 31st December 2009). There is no upper age limit.

Domicile Rules:

  • Government Seats: UP domicile is required.
  • Private/Private Minority Colleges: Open State Policy applies; candidates from other states can also apply for private medical seats.
  • Nationality: Indian citizens, NRIs, OCIs, PIOs, and foreign nationals are eligible.

Admission Process for Private MBBS Colleges in UP

  1. Register: Register and pay fees on upneet.gov.in.
  2. Documents: Online verification of documents by DGMET.
  3. Choice Filling: Mandatory to fill preferences in every round.
  4. Seat Allotment: Based on NEET rank, reservation, and domicile.

MBBS Fees in Private Colleges of UP

  • General Fee Range: Annual tuition fees at private MBBS colleges range from approximately ₹10–25 lakh per year, varying by college and facilities.
  • NRI Quota Fees: If you enroll under the NRI quota, the fees are significantly higher—typically ₹30–50 lakh per year or even more, depending on the college.
  • Other Charges: In addition to tuition fees, hostel fees (₹1–3 lakh/year), security deposit (refundable), exam fees, and miscellaneous charges (library, lab, etc.) are also included.

Facilities & Infrastructure

Private MBBS colleges in UP generally provide modern infrastructure, including smart classrooms, digital libraries, seminar halls, and advanced medical equipment.

  • Teaching Hospitals: Every medical college has an affiliated multi-specialty hospital, where students have the opportunity to understand real-time patient care and treatment processes.
  • Clinical Exposure: Due to the high patient flow in UP hospitals, students receive strong clinical exposure, which improves their practical skills and diagnostic abilities.
  • Hostel & Campus Facilities: Colleges provide separate hostels for boys and girls, mess, security, sports facilities, and a comfortable campus environment.
  • Labs and Research: Well-equipped laboratories (anatomy, pathology, microbiology, etc.) and research facilities are available, giving students hands-on learning and research opportunities.

How to Choose the Best Private MBBS College

  • Check NMC Approval: First, ensure the college is approved by the National Medical Commission (NMC) so that the degree is valid and recognized.
  • Look at Hospital Strength: Check the patient flow, bed capacity, and departments of the college’s attached hospital. More patients mean better clinical exposure.
  • Faculty & Infrastructure: Experienced faculty, modern labs, smart classrooms, and updated infrastructure are very important for quality education.
  • Location & Connectivity: The college should be located in a way that makes travel easy and basic facilities are available nearby.
  • Budget: Choose a college according to your budget, keeping in mind the fees structure, hostel charges, and other expenses.

Conclusion 

Choosing the right private MBBS college in Uttar Pradesh for 2026 is an important step toward a successful medical career. While private colleges offer easier admission compared to government institutions, students must carefully evaluate fees, infrastructure, faculty, hospital exposure, and NMC approval. With NEET 2026 as the entry route, securing a good score can help in getting a better college. Always compare options, check total costs, and stay active in counseling to make a well-informed and confident decision.

FAQs

1. What is the average fee for private MBBS colleges in UP in 2026?

The average fee ranges from ₹10 lakh to ₹25 lakh per year, depending on the college. Some colleges may have higher fees under management or NRI quota.

2. Is NEET 2026 mandatory for admission in private MBBS colleges in UP?

Yes, NEET 2026 is compulsory for admission to all MBBS colleges in India, including private institutions.

3. What is the expected NEET cutoff for private MBBS colleges in UP 2026?

The expected cutoff may range between 150 to 450 marks, depending on the college, category, and quota (general/management).

4. Can I get MBBS in a private college with low NEET marks?

Yes, some private colleges offer admission through management quota, where the cutoff is relatively lower compared to government colleges.

5. Are private MBBS colleges in UP approved and safe for education?

Most reputed private colleges are approved by the National Medical Commission (NMC) and provide quality education. Always verify approval before admission.

6. What is the duration of the MBBS course?

The MBBS course duration is 5.5 years, including 1 year of compulsory internship.

7. Is hostel accommodation available in private medical colleges?

Yes, most private MBBS colleges in UP provide separate hostel facilities for boys and girls with basic amenities.

8. How can I apply for MBBS admission in UP private colleges?

You need to:

  • Qualify NEET 2026
  • Register for UP state counseling
  • Participate in choice filling and seat allotment

title: Top 6 Cheapest Countries for MBBS in 2026
content:
Top 6 Cheapest Countries for MBBS in 2026

Studying MBBS abroad has become a popular choice for Indian students due to limited government seats and high paying fees of private medical colleges. Every year lakhs of students qualify NEET, but not everyone gets a government seat. For this reason students are choosing the abroad option  and looking for Cheapest Countries for MBBS in 2026. 

For 2026, Some countries have emerged as the most budget-friendly and reliable options for Indian students. Including top choices such as Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Kazakhstan, Russia, Georgia, and Philippines. These options are not only affordable but medical universities are also recognized by the NMC (National Medical Commission), which is mandatory for practice in India. 

The total tuition fees for MBBS in these countries range from approximately ₹15 lakh to ₹35 lakh, which is quite low compared to private colleges in India. Furthermore, the cost of living is reasonable, hostel, food, and daily expenses are easily manageable.

Another advantage is that MBBS in these countries is offered in English medium, so language barriers are not an issue. The infrastructure, practical exposure, and international environment also prepare students for global standards.

If you are looking for a budget-friendly, quality education and global exposure, these top 6 cheapest countries may be your best options.

Key Factors to Consider

MBBS Abroad has become a smart choice for Indian students, especially when the competition has high and expensive fees. Countries like Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Kazakhstan, Russia, Georgia, and the Philippines are providing quality education with affordable fees. Let’s understand the important factors in key details:

  • Tuition Fees: The total MBBS fee in these countries is approximately ₹15–35 lakhs. This is quite affordable compared to private medical colleges in India (₹60 lakh–₹1 crore).
  • Cost of Living: Monthly expenses (hostel + food + transport) range between ₹8,000–₹20,000. This is budget-friendly and easily manageable for students.
  • NMC Approval: Many universities in all these countries are NMC (National Medical Commission) approved. This means the degree is valid in India, and you can practice by taking the FMGE/NexT exam.
  • Medium of Instruction (English): MBBS courses are available in English medium, so Indian students don’t face any language barriers in their studies.
  • FMGE/NExT Passing Rate: Passing rates depend on the university, but good universities offer proper coaching and clinical exposure, which helps in clearing the FMGE/NExT. Students also need to maintain strong self-study.
  • Safety & Lifestyle: These countries are generally safe for international students. Hostel facilities, Indian food availability, and a student-friendly environment make the lifestyle comfortable.

If you’re planning MBBS abroad , then these 6 countries are Cheapest Countries for MBBS in 2026 options for Indian students. Below are the key highlights of each country given:

  • Rapidly emerging MBBS destination
  • Affordable fees structure
  • Popularity is increasing among Indian students
  • Average Cost: ₹12–22 Lakhs total

Advantages of choosing Cheapest Countries for MBBS in 2026

If you’re choosing MBBS abroad, then here are some major advantages which are very beneficial in the companies of Indian private colleges:

  • Lower Cost Compared to Private Colleges in India
    The total cost of MBBS abroad is between ₹10 to 40 lakhs, while the Indian private colleges fees range from ₹60 lakhs to ₹1 crore. 
  • International Exposure
    Studying abroad students get exposure to diverse cultures, healthcare systems and  global medical practices. It enhances both your personality and career.
  • Better Infrastructure
    Many foreign universities provide modern labs, advanced technology and well-equipped hospitals, which gives students better practical learning and clinical experience.
  • No Donation Required
    Private colleges in India have capitation/donation fees, but admission to universities abroad is purely merit-based there are no donations or hidden charges.

Who Should Choose MBBS Abroad?

MBA abroad isn’t for every student, but it can be a perfect option for some specific students. Let’s explain in simple words:

Students with Low NEET Scores: If your NEET score isn’t high enough to secure a government seat in India, MBBS abroad is a strong alternative. The admission process is comparatively easy, and you can continue your dream of becoming a doctor.

Budget-Conscious Families: For families who can’t afford the ₹60 lakh to ₹1 crore fees of private medical colleges in India, MBBS abroad is a cost-effective option. Here, you can complete your medical degree within a budget of ₹10 to 40 lakh.

Students Open to Global Opportunities: If you’re open to international exposure, new cultures, and global career opportunities, MBBS abroad is best for you. This gives you a chance to explore careers in different countries in the future.

Conclusion

Choosing the right country for MBBS abroad in 2026 is a crucial decision that can shape your entire medical career. With rising competition and high fees in India, many students are looking for Cheapest Countries for MBBS in 2026 options like Russia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, and others. These countries offer quality education, globally recognized degrees, and budget-friendly tuition fees.

FAQs

1. Which is the cheapest country for MBBS in 2026?

Kyrgyzstan and Kazakhstan are considered among the cheapest countries, with low tuition fees and affordable living costs.

2. Is MBBS from abroad valid in India?

Yes, MBBS from abroad is valid in India if:

  • The university is approved by NMC
  • You qualify NEET before admission
  • You pass the FMGE/NExT exam after completing your degree

3. What is the minimum NEET score required for MBBS abroad?

Students must qualify NEET (meet the cutoff marks). There is no high score requirement, but qualifying is mandatory.

4. Which country is best for Indian students for MBBS?

Countries like Russia, Kazakhstan, and the Philippines are popular due to affordability, English-medium programs, and good education standards.

5. What is the total cost of MBBS abroad?

The total cost typically ranges between ₹10 lakhs to ₹40 lakhs, depending on the country, university, and lifestyle.

6. Is IELTS required for MBBS abroad?

Most countries do not require IELTS for Indian students, especially if the course is offered in English.

7. Can I practice in India after MBBS abroad?

Yes, but you must clear the FMGE (Foreign Medical Graduate Examination) or the upcoming NExT exam to practice in India.

8. What are the main challenges of studying MBBS abroad?

  • FMGE/NExT licensing exam
  • Language barriers in local hospitals
  • Cultural and climate differences

title: MBBS Abroad vs India – Which is Better in 2026
content:
MBBS Abroad vs India – Which is Better in 2026

 Choosing an MBBS Abroad vs India in 2026 depends primarily on your budget and NEET score. If you have a high NEET score and secure admission to a government medical college, India is definitely the best option. Fees are quite low (₹1–5 lakh total), education quality is strong, and most importantly clinical exposure is abundant. Due to the high patient load in India, students get real-life practice much earlier. Plus, you don’t have the stress of a separate licensing exam to practice directly in India after graduation.

But if your NEET score is low or you can’t afford a private college (where fees can range from ₹60 lakh to ₹1 crore), MBBS abroad becomes a practical option. In countries like Russia, Kazakhstan, Georgia, Philippines, etc., the total cost ranges from ₹15–50 lakh, which is much more affordable than Indian private colleges. The admission process is also relatively simple, mostly qualifying NEET is enough; a high rank is not required.

However, there is one important factor with MBBS abroad you will have to clear FMGE/Next to return to India and practice. This requires self-discipline and proper preparation.

What is MBBS Abroad?

MBBS abroad means pursuing a medical degree outside India—especially in countries like Russia, Kazakhstan, Georgia, the Philippines, etc. This option is popular for students with low NEET scores or who cannot afford the high fees of private medical colleges in India.

Compared to India, the admission process for MBBS abroad is quite simple. You don’t need to take a separate entrance exam, only qualifying the NEET is mandatory (as per Indian regulations). This is why many students choose this option.

Key Features:

  • Duration: MBBS abroad typically lasts 5–6 years, and includes academic study and an internship.
  • Easy Admission Process: There is no additional entrance exam; NEET qualification is sufficient for admission.
  • Affordable Fees: The total cost ranges from approximately ₹15–50 lakh, which is quite low compared to Indian private colleges.
  • Globally Recognized Degree: Many universities are recognized by WHO, NMC (as per guidelines), and other international bodies, allowing you to explore careers in multiple countries.

MBBS admission in India is highly competitive, requiring a top NEET score for limited government seats, while abroad offers a direct, non-competitive process with lower costs. Indian admissions rely on counseling (MCC/states), whereas foreign universities usually focus on class 12th science marks and require only NEET qualification, not a high rank.

Admission Process: MBBS Abroad vs India

Admissions in India are extremely competitive, with a strong focus on entrance exams like JEE and NEET, along with high Class 12 marks. In contrast, international admissions follow a more holistic approach, considering factors such as extracurricular activities, personal essays, and recommendation letters, often with applications beginning a year earlier. While studying in India is generally more cost-effective, pursuing education abroad provides wider and more diverse opportunities.

MBBS in India:
The first step to MBBS admission in India is qualifying the NEET. Simply qualifying the NEET isn’t enough a high score is also essential. The admission process is conducted through centralized counseling, such as MCC (All India Quota and Deemed University), and individual state counseling. Competition is high (2 million+ students vs limited seats), making selection difficult. There are multiple counseling rounds, due to which the entire process can take quite a long time.

MBBS in Abroad :
Qualifying NEET is mandatory for Abroad MBBS (if you want to practice in India in the future), Admission is mostly direct, based on your 10th and 12th PCB marks. The process is simple, and many universities offer seats on a “first-come-first-served” basis. The timeline is also fast – quick application, quick confirmation.

Fees Structure (MBBS Abroad vs India)

Studying abroad in countries like the US, UK, or Canada is considerably more expensive, with total tuition and living costs for master’s programs typically ranging between ₹20–80+ lakhs. In comparison, India provides a much more budget-friendly option, where tuition fees at public institutions for specialized courses are often under ₹15 lakhs. However, there are also affordable international options countries like Germany and Malaysia offer quality education at a lower cost, usually around ₹10–20 lakhs per year.

Fees in India:

Government Colleges: Fees are quite affordable here approximately ₹10,000 to ₹1 lakh per year. However, getting admission here can be very difficult because of the high cutoff.

Private Colleges: Fees are quite high here around ₹10 lakh to ₹25 lakh per year. The total MBBS cost can easily range from ₹50 lakh to ₹1 crore+.

Fees Abroad:

MBBS fees at foreign universities range from a comparatively low ₹3 lakh to ₹8 lakh per year (average).

Taking into account the total cost, the overall expense of MBBS abroad is significantly lower than that of private Indian colleges.

Quality of Education (India vs. Abroad) 

Studying abroad typically focuses on practical application, innovation, and flexible, student-centric learning, while Indian studies emphasize a strong theoretical foundation, rigorous assessment, and academic discipline. International studies offer better research facilities and global exposure, while India offers a more affordable, structured, and familiar academic environment.

Education Quality in India:

Top government medical colleges in India are highly reputed and offer strong clinical exposure. Hospitals have a high patient flow, giving students valuable experience handling real-life cases. This builds strong practical knowledge, which is very helpful for future practice.

Education Quality Abroad:

Universities abroad generally provide modern infrastructure, advanced labs, and updated technology. Classrooms and facilities are well-developed. However, clinical exposure may be limited in some countries, as patient flow is not as high as in India. Because of this, hands-on experience may be slightly less (depending on the country and university).

Degree Recognition (India vs. Abroad)

Indian degrees, especially from premier institutes like IITs and IIMs, are widely recognized globally in fields such as IT, engineering, and management. However, for certain international jobs or higher studies, they may require validation, such as AIU equivalency. On the other hand, foreign degrees from countries like the US, UK, and Australia provide strong global mobility and specialized learning opportunities, but they must comply with UGC guidelines to be officially recognized in India.

Degree Recognition in India:

If you completed your MBBS from India, your degree is directly valid in India. After completing your internship, you can directly begin medical practice or apply for a PG degree there’s no need to take any additional licensing exams (as per the current system).

Degree Recognition Abroad:

If you completed your MBBS from abroad, you must clear a licensing exam, such as the FMGE (Foreign Medical Graduate Examination) or, in the future, the NEXT (National Exit Test), to make your degree valid in India.

  • Clearing the FMGE/Next is mandatory to start practicing.
  • The exam’s difficulty level is quite high.
  • Only after passing can you register in India.

Who Should Choose MBBS in India? 

  • Students with high NEET scores: If you have a strong score, your chances of getting into a government college in India are high.
  • Those targeting government colleges: Benefit from low fees and the best clinical exposure.
  • Want to practice in India: By completing an MBBS here, you can start practicing directly, without taking an extra licensing exam.

Who Should Choose MBBS Abroad? 

  • Students with low NEET scores: For those who find it difficult to get a seat in India, abroad is a good option.
  • Cannot afford the high fees of a private college: The total cost of an MBBS abroad is often lower than that of private colleges in India.
  • Want international exposure: You get to experience a new culture, global education system and modern infrastructure.

Conclusion 

The decision between MBBS Abroad vs India isn’t a one-size-fits-all one. If you have a high NEET score and can get into a government college, India is the best option offering the benefits of low fees, strong clinical exposure, and direct practice.

But if you have a low score or can’t afford the high fees of private colleges, MBBS abroad becomes a practical and smart alternative. The admission process is easy, fees are comparatively moderate, and you also get international exposure.

FAQs

Q1. Is MBBS abroad valid in India?

Yes, it is valid but clearing the FMGE/Next exam is mandatory to practice in India.

Q2. Can you pursue MBBS abroad without NEET?

You can get admission, but if you want to practice in India in the future, qualifying NEET is a must.

Q3. Is MBBS abroad cheaper in India or in India?

Government colleges are the cheapest in India. But if we compare private colleges in India vs. abroad, abroad is more affordable in many cases.

Q4. Is there a language barrier abroad?

It may be in some countries, but many universities also offer English-medium MBBS.

Q5. Can you get a job in India after an overseas MBBS?

Yes, but you’ll need to clear the FMGE/Next first, only then can you practice or work in India.

Q6. Is MBBS abroad safe?

Yes, if you choose a recognized and NMC-approved university, it’s a safe and reliable option.

If you have a high NEET score and secure admission to a government medical college, India is definitely the best option. Fees are quite low (₹1–5 lakh total), education quality is … Read more

title: How to Get MBBS with a Low NEET Score in 2026 – Complete Guide
content:
How to Get MBBS with a Low NEET Score in 2026 – Complete Guide

NEET  (National Eligibility cum Entrance Test) is an all-India level medical entrance exam in India, to get admission in courses like MBBS, BDS and other medical courses NEET exam is mandatory. Qualifying the NEET Exam is most important because NEET scores are the primary basis for admission to both government and private medical colleges. Millions of students take NEET each year, but due to limited seats, competition is quite tough.

But an important point is that not all students achieve high scores, and this doesn’t mean their medical dreams are over. Many options are available for students aiming for MBBS with a low NEET score. Options like private colleges, deemed universities, and in some cases, MBBS abroad can also be considered. 

Other than this, the counselling process and state quota system also provides opportunities to students even with low marks. With smart planning, right guidance, and timely decisions, it is possible to get the MBBS seat.

So, if your NEET scores are low, then don’t need to panic. With proper research and strategy, you can still achieve your dream of becoming a doctor.

What is considered a Low NEET Score?

It is helpful to have an idea of approximate ranges to understand your NEET score.

  • 600+ marks: Chances of getting into top government medical colleges are strong.
  • 500–600 marks: You can get into good government or decent private colleges.
  • 400–500 marks: Some state-quota government colleges or private colleges are options.
  • Below 400 marks: From here, the situation becomes a little challenging, but for MBBS with a low NEET score, private colleges, deemed universities, or foreign options can be considered.
  • Below cutoff marks: If you score below the qualifying cutoff, MBBS admission in India is unlikely, but MBBS abroad may still be an option.

Most importantly, the NEET cutoff changes every year. This depends on factors such as paper difficulty, number of candidates, and available seats. Therefore, a score considered low one year may create a different situation the next.

For this reason, instead of focusing solely on marks, students should also pay attention to the counseling process, college options, and backup plans. With the proper strategy, even with average or low scores, you can find your way to a medical career.

Can You Get MBBS with a Low NEET Score?

Yes, but options are limited.

If you’re targeting MBBS with a low NEET score, admission depends on a few key factors:

  • Category (General/OBC/SC/ST): The cutoff for reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC) is comparatively lower, so your chances may be slightly better compared to the General category.
  • State Quota vs. All India Quota: 85% of seats are filled through the state quota, where the cutoff is sometimes lower. The All India Quota (15%) is more competitive.
  • Budget: Fees are quite high in private and deemed universities. If your budget is strong, your chances of getting an MBBS seat even with a low score increase.
  • Flexibility to Study Abroad: If you do not get a seat in India, then MBBS in countries like Russia, Kazakhstan, Philippines, etc. can be an alternative option.

How to Pursue MBBS with a Low NEET Score: Best Alternative Pathways

While a high NEET score is usually required for government medical colleges, students with lower scores still have multiple pathways to pursue MBBS or build a successful career in the medical field.

  1. Private Medical Colleges (Management Quota)

Many private medical colleges offer Management Quota seats with significantly lower cutoff requirements compared to government or merit-based seats.

Entrance Criteria

  • Requires NEET qualification only
  • Approximate qualifying scores:
    • General: 137–164
    • OBC/SC/ST: 107–127

Cost

  • ₹15 lakh to ₹30 lakh per year
  • Fees vary depending on the college

Best States for Low Scores

  • Uttar Pradesh: Transparent online counselling, open to non-domicile students
  • Karnataka: Large number of private seats (around 35% management quota)
  • Tamil Nadu & Maharashtra: High availability of private and deemed seats
  1. Deemed Universities

Deemed universities are autonomous institutions that participate in centralized counselling conducted by the Medical Counselling Committee (MCC).

Strategy

Focus on Mop-up and Stray Vacancy rounds, where cutoffs tend to drop significantly.

Example Institutions & Approximate Closing Scores

  • Dr. D.Y. Patil Medical College, Pune: Around 260+
  • Santosh Medical College, Ghaziabad: Around 245+
  • SRM Medical College, Chennai: Rank around 5.5–6.3 lakh
  1. MBBS Abroad

Studying MBBS abroad is often a cost-effective option for students with low but qualifying NEET scores.

Important Guidelines

As per National Medical Commission (NMC):

  • Minimum 54 months course duration
  • 12-month internship mandatory
  • University must be NMC-approved

Popular Destinations

  • Russia: Affordable fees (₹3.5–5 lakh/year), good infrastructure
  • Kazakhstan & Uzbekistan: Shorter duration (5–6 years), globally recognized degrees
  • Georgia: Safe environment, English-medium programs
  1. NRI Quota Seats

Students with NRI status or NRI sponsorship can access seats with the lowest cutoff requirements in private and deemed universities.

Key Benefit

  • Seats that typically require 500+ marks may be available at 150–200 marks under this quota

Important Note

  • Requires valid NRI documentation and sponsorship proof
  • Fees are significantly higher than other quotas
  1. Allied Medical & AYUSH Courses

If MBBS is not financially feasible, several alternative medical courses offer strong career opportunities with lower NEET requirements.

Popular Options

BDS (Dental)

  • 250–350 marks can secure good private or some government-aided colleges

AYUSH Courses (BAMS/BHMS)

  • Ayurveda and Homeopathy are growing fields
  • 240–300 marks are competitive in top private colleges

BSc Nursing & Physiotherapy (BPT)

  • High demand healthcare careers
  • Admission may be based on 12th marks or low NEET score 

Step-by-Step Admission Process 

Understanding the admission process is crucial to securing an MBBS seat, especially with a low NEET score. Follow these steps carefully:

Appear for NEET 2026

The first and most important step is to appear for the NEET 2026 exam. Qualifying NEET is mandatory for admission to any medical college in India, including private and deemed universities.

Check Your Score and Rank

Once the results are declared, analyze your:

  • NEET score
  • All India Rank (AIR)
  • Category rank

This will help you understand your admission chances and plan your next steps.

Register for Counselling

You must apply to different counselling authorities based on your target colleges:

MCC (All India Quota)

  • Covers 15% All India Quota seats
  • Includes deemed universities and central institutions

State Counselling

  • Conducted by individual states
  • Covers 85% state quota seats and private colleges

Choose Colleges Wisely

While filling choices:

  • Prioritize colleges based on your score
  • Include a mix of safe, moderate, and ambitious options
  • Consider factors like fees, location, and clinical exposure

Participate in Multiple Rounds

Do not rely on just one round. Actively participate in:

  • Round 1
  • Round 2
  • Mop-up Round
  • Stray Vacancy Round

Cutoffs often decrease in later rounds, increasing your chances of getting a seat.

Consider Backup Options

Always keep alternative plans ready:

  • Management quota seats
  • Deemed universities
  • MBBS abroad
  • Allied medical courses

Tips for Getting an MBBS with Low Marks

  • Apply to Multiple Counseling Rounds
  • Have Backup Options (BDS, BAMS, BHMS)
  • Be Flexible About Location
  • Consider Moving Abroad Early
  • Seek Expert Guidance

Common Mistakes to Avoid

  • Waiting Only for Government Colleges
  • Not Participating in All Counseling Rounds
  • Ignoring Deemed/Private Colleges
  • Falling Into the Spurs of Fake Agents

Alternative Medical Courses

  • BDS (Dental)
  • BAMS (Ayurveda)
  • BHMS (Homeopathy)
  • BPT (Physiotherapy)

Conclusion

Getting admission into MBBS with a low NEET score in 2026 may seem challenging, but it is definitely not impossible. While government medical colleges have high cutoffs, there are still multiple pathways available such as private medical colleges, deemed universities, and MBBS abroad options. The key is to stay informed, explore all available opportunities, and make timely decisions during the counseling process. Visit AR Group of Education for more. 

FAQs 

1. What is the minimum NEET score required for MBBS in 2026?

The minimum NEET score depends on the category, state, and type of college. For government colleges, it is usually higher (500+), while private colleges may accept lower scores (around 250–400). Cutoffs change every year.

2. Can I get MBBS with 300 marks in NEET 2026?

Yes, you may get admission in private medical colleges, deemed universities, or through management quota. You can also consider MBBS abroad as a strong option.

3. Is MBBS abroad a good option for low NEET scorers?

Yes, MBBS abroad is a popular and affordable option. Many countries offer quality education with lower fees and no donation. However, qualifying NEET is mandatory.

4. Can I get MBBS without NEET?

No, NEET qualification is compulsory for MBBS admission in India and even for studying MBBS abroad (for Indian students).

5. Which countries are best for MBBS abroad in 2026?

Popular options include Russia, Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Philippines, and Georgia due to their affordable fees and recognized universities.

6. Should I drop a year or take admission with a low score?

It depends on your situation. If you believe you can significantly improve your score, taking a drop may help. Otherwise, exploring available options in the same year is a practical choice.

7. What are the alternatives if I don’t get MBBS?

You can consider other medical courses like BDS, BAMS, BHMS, or BPT, which also offer good career opportunities in healthcare.

Qualifying the NEET Exam is most important because NEET scores … Read more

title: NEET UG Counselling 2026 Step-by-Step Process
content:
NEET UG Counselling 2026 Step-by-Step Process

Getting admission into top medical colleges in India after qualifying NEET UG 2026 is a dream of many. But the qualifying exam is not enough. The NEET UG Counselling process is equally important. If you follow the counselling steps correctly, you can easily secure a seat in MBBS, BUDS or other medical courses.

First, you need to register for counselling on the official portal. This is followed by the choice filling and locking process where you select your preferred colleges and courses. This step is crucial as seat allotment is based on this. Then comes the seat allotment result, in which you are informed which college you have been allotted. 

If you like the allotted seat, you need to go through document verification and reporting. Required documents include NEET scorecard, admit card, 10th-12th marksheet, ID proof, and category certificate (if applicable). If you are not satisfied, you can participate in the next rounds.

Success in NEET UG counseling requires smart strategies analyzing previous year cut-offs, filling in realistic college choices, and avoiding deadlines. With proper planning and guidance, you can achieve your dream medical college.

What is NEET UG Counselling 2026?

NEET UG Counselling 2026 is a centralized admission process through which qualified candidates are allotted seats in MBBS, BDS, AYUSH and other medical courses. 

The counselling is conducted by:

  • MCC (Medical Counselling Committee) – for All India Quota (AIQ) seats
  • State counselling authorities – for state quota seats 

Medical Counselling Committee (MCC), which handles All India Quota (AIQ) seats, and the respective state counselling authorities, which are responsible for state quota seats. Candidates have to follow the steps of registration, choice filling, seat allotment and document verification. With the right strategy and timely participation, students can secure admission in their preferred medical college.

Types of NEET Counselling

There are mainly two types of counselling in NEET:

1. All India Quota (AIQ) Counselling

  • Covers 15% MBBS/BDS seats in government colleges
  • Conducted by MCC
  • Includes Deemed Universities, AIIMS, JIPMER

2. State Quota Counselling

  • Covers 85% seats in state medical colleges
  • Conducted by respective state authorities
  • Admission based on NEET score + state eligibility

NEET UG Counselling 2026: Step-by-Step Process

Step 1: Registration

  • Visit the official MCC or state counselling website
  • Register using NEET roll number and details
  • Create login credentials

Step 2: Payment of Counselling Fee

  • Pay the required registration fee online
  • Fee varies for General, OBC, SC/ST categories

Step 3: Choice Filling & Locking

  • Select preferred MBBS colleges, courses, and locations
  • Arrange choices in priority order
  • Lock choices before deadline

Step 4: Seat Allotment Result

  • Based on:
      • NEET rank
      • Preferences filled
      • Seat availability
  • Result published online

Step 5: Reporting to Allotted College

  • Download allotment letter
  • Report to the college within deadline
  • Verify documents and confirm admission

Step 6: Upgradation (Optional)

  • If not satisfied, participate in next rounds
  • Option to upgrade to better college 

NEET Counselling Rounds 2026

NEET UG counselling is conducted in multiple rounds:

  1. Round 1
  2. Round 2
  3. Mop-Up Round
  4. Stray Vacancy Round

Documents Required for NEET UG Counselling 2026

Keep these documents ready for smooth MBBS admission through NEET:

  • NEET 2026 Admit Card
  • NEET Rank Card / Scorecard
  • Class 10 & 12 Marksheet
  • ID Proof (Aadhar/PAN)
  • Passport Size Photos
  • Category Certificate (if applicable)
  • Domicile Certificate
  • Allotment Letter

Eligibility for NEET UG Counselling 2026

To participate in counselling, candidates must:

  • Qualify NEET UG 2026
  • Meet minimum cutoff percentile
  • Fulfill age and academic criteria
  • Register for counselling

NEET Cutoff 2026 (Expected)

Expected NEET cutoff for MBBS admission:

  • General: 50th percentile
  • OBC/SC/ST: 40th percentile

Higher scores increase chances of getting top government medical colleges.

Tips to Secure MBBS Seat Through NEET Counselling

  • Fill as many choices as possible
  • Prioritize government colleges
  • Analyze previous year cutoffs
  • Keep documents ready in advance
  • Participate in all counselling rounds

Conclusion

The NEET UG Counselling 2026 step-by-step process is the final and most crucial stage in your journey toward becoming a doctor. Proper understanding of registration, choice filling, and seat allotment can significantly improve your chances of getting an MBBS seat in a top medical college.

Stay updated with official notifications and make informed choices to secure your dream medical college.

FAQs 

Q1. Who conducts NEET UG counselling 2026?

MCC conducts AIQ counselling, while state authorities handle state quota counselling.

Q2. How many rounds are there in NEET counselling?

There are usually 4 rounds: Round 1, Round 2, Mop-Up, and Stray Vacancy.

Q3. Can I participate in both AIQ and state counselling?

Yes, eligible candidates can apply for both.

Q4. Is NEET compulsory for MBBS admission?

Yes, NEET is mandatory for MBBS admission in India.

Q5. What is choice locking in NEET counselling?

It is the process of confirming selected colleges before final submission.

title: NTA Extends NEET UG 2026 Application Deadline – Apply Before 11 March
content:
NEET UG 2026 Registration Deadline Extended; Apply by March 11

The National Testing Agency (NTA) has extended the last date for NEET (UG) 2026 registration. Candidates who have not yet submitted their applications have been given one last chance to complete the registration process.

NEET UG is a national-level entrance exam which is mandatory for aspiring medical students who want to make their career in the field of medical. Through this exam students are eligible for admission in MBBS, BDS, AYUSH and other medical courses.

To get admission to undergraduate medical programs in recognized medical colleges and institutes in India qualifying NEET is mandatory. The exam is conducted by the National Testing Agency (NTA) every year and is considered one of the most important exams for medical education.
Therefore, students who have not yet applied must complete their NEET UG 2026 registration before March 11 so that they can appear in this year’s exam.

NEET UG 2026 Important Dates

Event Extended Deadline
Online Application Submission 11th March 2026 (Until 09:00 P.M.)
Last Date for Fee Payment 11th March 2026 (Until 11:50 P.M.)

NEET UG 2026-How to Apply?

InstructionDetails
Visit the Official WebsiteAccess the registration form at nta.ac.in.
Fill the Online FormComplete the online application form with required personal and academic details.
Submit Before DeadlineEnsure the form is submitted by 11th March 2026 (09:00 P.M.).
Pay the Examination FeeFees must be paid online by 11:50 P.M. on 11th March 2026.
Final ConfirmationCandidates are advised to complete all steps within the extended date as no further extensions will be granted.

Therefore, candidates should complete the process as quickly as possible to avoid last-minute technical issues. For any help or queries, students can contact the helpline at +91-11-40759000 or email neetug2026@nta.ac.in.

Candidates are advised to check all the latest updates and important information by visiting official website nta.nic.in and you can also check our website AR Group of Education for all latest information.

title: Uttarakhand AYUSH Counselling 2025: Complete Guide for BAMS, BHMS & BUMS Admissions
content:
AYUSH courses are gaining immense popularity in India as a large number of students are looking to pursue careers in traditional medicine. Courses include BAMS (Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery), BHMS (Bachelor of Homeopathic Medicine and Surgery), BUMS (Bachelor of Unani Medicine and Surgery), BSMS (Bachelor of Siddha Medicine and Surgery), and BNYS (Bachelor of Naturopathy and Yogic Sciences). Each program offers a different path to holistic healthcare and provides professional opportunities both in India and abroad.

Uttarakhand has emerged as a leading hub for AYUSH education among several states. Also known as Devbhoomi, the state has a rich heritage of Ayurveda, yoga and natural healing practices. Its serene environment, combined with a host of prestigious institutions such as Rishikul Ayurvedic College, Gurukul Ayurvedic College and several private AYUSH colleges, make it a preferred destination for students who wish to pursue traditional medicine. 

About UTTRAKHAND  AYUSH NEET Counselling 2025

The schedule of Uttarakhand AYUSH NEET Counselling 2025 has been released. The counselling is starting from 16th September 2025. This counselling process is important for students who want to ensure admission in courses like BAMS, BHMS and BUMS in government and private colleges of the state. This counselling process is conducted online, in which students from all over India participate.Candidates are advised to keep their documents ready and stay updated with the official schedule to avoid missing important deadlines. Starting on 16 September 2025, this counselling round will be the first step for aspirants to begin their journey in holistic healthcare.

Eligible candidates can participate in AYUSH NEET Counselling 2025 if they have successfully cleared NEET UG 2025. Only students who meet the minimum qualifying percentile and fulfill the eligibility criteria set by Uttarakhand authorities can register for the counselling process. 

Step-by-step — Uttarakhand AYUSH NEET Counselling 2025

1. Visit the official counselling website (to be notified by the authority).

2. Click on the “AYUSH NEET 2025 Counselling Registration” link.

3. Complete the registration by entering NEET roll number, application ID, and personal details.

4. Pay the registration and security fee online.

5. Upload the required scanned documents.

6. Fill in your preferred colleges and courses.

7. Submit the form and take a printout for reference.

Students are advised to keep all documents ready in advance and carefully check the counselling schedule to avoid missing deadlines.

Uttarakhand AYUSH NEET Counselling 2025 – Eligibility at a Glance

  • Nationality & Domicile: Only Indian nationals. Uttarakhand domicile required for state quota; others eligible under 15% All India Quota.
  • Qualification: 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry & Biology; minimum 50% for general category (relaxation for reserved categories).
  • NEET Cut-off: General – 50th percentile, OBC/SC/ST – 40th percentile, PwD – 45th percentile.
  • Age Limit: Minimum 17 years as of 31 Dec 2025; no upper age limit.
  • Reservation Policy: SC/ST/OBC/EWS & PwD as per state norms; NRI seats in private colleges.

Document checklist (upload & carry originals)

  • NEET 2025 Admit Card & Scorecard
  • Class 10 & 12 marksheets/certificates
  • Domicile / State residence certificate (if applying under state quota)
  • Caste/category certificate (if applicable)
  • Photo ID (Aadhaar / PAN / Passport) + passport photos
  • Transfer/Migration certificate, gap certificate (if applicable)

Top AYUSH Colleges in Uttarakhand

Conclusion

title: MCI Approved MBBS Colleges Abroad – Complete Guide for Indian Students 2025
content:
MBBS abroad is becoming one of the most popular choices for Indian students due to the high cost of private medical colleges in India and limited seats in government colleges. But one of the most important factors to consider while choosing a medical university abroad is whether it is MCI (Medical Council of India) approved, now under NMC (National Medical Commission). This ensures that after completing your MBBS abroad, you can practice in India by clearing the FMGE/NExT exam.In this blog, we will provide a complete list of MCI-approved MBBS colleges abroad, details about their fees, eligibility criteria, and admission process.

Why Choose MCI Approved MBBS Colleges Abroad?

  1. Recognition in India: Only MCI/NMC approved colleges allow you to appear for FMGE/NExT in India.
  2. High-Quality Education: Most of these colleges follow international standards in medical education.
  3. Affordable Fees: MBBS abroad is often more affordable than private colleges in India.
  4. Global Exposure: Studying abroad gives exposure to diverse healthcare systems and practices.

Top MCI Approved MBBS Colleges Abroad

Eligibility Criteria for MBBS Abroad

  • Minimum Qualification: 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology
  • Minimum Marks: 50% in PCB for general category, 40% for reserved categories

Documents Required for Admission

  • Passport and Passport Size Photos

Advantages of Studying MBBS Abroad

  • Affordable fees compared to private colleges in India.

Yes, NEET qualification is mandatory for Indian students to pursue MBBS abroad.

title: Study MBBS in Madhya Pradesh (MP) 2025 – A Complete Guide for Medical Aspirants
content:
Madhya Pradesh (MP), located in central India, is one of the top choices for students aspiring to pursue Study MBBS in Madhya Pradesh due to its affordable government colleges, state-of-the-art private institutions, and streamlined admission process. With increasing demand for quality medical education, MP has emerged as a hub for aspiring doctors from all over the country.

Madhya Pradesh is a state of India with top medical colleges, offering students a chance to pursue their careers in the medical field. For those who want to become doctors, Study MBBS in Madhya Pradesh is a great opportunity, with a variety of government and private institutions to choose from. The process mainly revolves around NEET (National Eligibility cum Entrance Test) scores, followed by counseling for seat allocation. To make the most of this opportunity, it’s important for students to be well aware of the eligibility requirements, application steps, and important deadlines to secure their place in one of the state’s famous medical colleges.

 Why Choose MP for MBBS in 2025?

  •  Availability of both government and private medical colleges
  •  Reasonable fee structure in comparison to other states
  •  NEET-based transparent admission through centralized counseling
  •  Modern infrastructure and experienced faculty
  •  Great student-friendly environment and developing medical ecosystem

 Study MBBS in Madhya Pradesh – Course Highlights

 Top Private MBBS Colleges in MP

  1. LN Medical College, Bhopal
  2. RKDF Medical College, Bhopal
  3. Index Medical College, Indore
  4. Amaltas Institute of Medical Sciences, Dewas
  5. People’s College of Medical Sciences, Bhopal
  6. Modern Institute of Medical Sciences, Indore

Eligibility Criteria for Study MBBS in Madhya Pradesh

The eligibility criteria for Study MBBS in Madhya Pradesh are as follows:

Age: Candidates should be at least 17 years of age at the time of admission or before 31st December of the academic year.

Educational Qualification: Candidates should have completed their 10+2 or equivalent examination from a recognised board with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotechnology, and English as compulsory subjects.

Minimum Marks: Candidates belonging to the general category must have secured a minimum aggregate of 50% marks in the qualifying examination, while candidates belonging to SC/ST/OBC categories must have secured a minimum aggregate of 40% marks.

NEET: Candidates must have appeared for the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET) and have obtained a valid score.

Domicile: Candidates should be citizens of India and should satisfy the domicile criteria of Madhya Pradesh, which vary from college to college.

It is important to note that the eligibility criteria mentioned above may vary from college to college.

 Admission Process for Study MBBS in Madhya Pradesh 2025

Admission to MBBS in Madhya Pradesh is conducted by the Directorate of Medical Education (DME), MP through MP NEET UG Counselling. The steps are:

  1. NEET UG 2025 Exam – Appear and qualify
  2. Online Registration on DME MP portal
  3. Choice Filling and Locking
  4. Seat Allotment Result – Round-wise
  5. Document Verification & Reporting
  6. Final Admission in allotted college

👉 MP State Quota includes 85% seats for MP domicile candidates and 15% AIQ (All India Quota).

 Documents Required for MBBS in Madhya Pradesh

  • NEET UG 2025 Scorecard
  • 10th & 12th Marksheet and Certificate
  • Domicile Certificate (for MP State Quota)
  • Category Certificate (if applicable)
  • Migration/Transfer Certificate
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Passport size photographs
  • Allotment letter

Private Medical Colleges in Madhya Pradesh 2025 Fees

Here is a list of private medical colleges, along with their estimated annual fee structures and seat capacities.

Why Study MBBS in Madhya Pradesh

Here are some potential reasons why you might consider pursuing an Study MBBS in Madhya Pradesh:

  1. Availability of Medical Colleges: Madhya Pradesh has a good number of medical colleges, both government and private, offering MBBS programs. This translates to more MBBS seats and potentially higher chances of securing admission, especially compared to states with fewer colleges.
  2. Government College Fees: Government medical colleges in Madhya Pradesh typically have lower fees compared to private colleges across India. This can be a significant advantage if you’re looking for a more affordable medical education.
  3. Quality Education: Many government medical colleges in Madhya Pradesh have a good reputation for providing quality medical education. Some colleges might be affiliated with well-established universities, offering a well-rounded learning experience.
  4. Exposure to Diverse Medical Cases: Madhya Pradesh is a large state with a sizeable population. This can translate to a wider range of medical cases that you might encounter during your clinical rotations, providing valuable practical experience.

 Career Opportunities After Study MBBS in Madhya Pradesh

After completing MBBS, students can pursue:

  • Postgraduate Courses – MD/MS via NEET PG
  • Government Jobs – State/central healthcare services
  • Private Practice or Hospitals
  • Medical Officer roles
  • Research or Abroad Studies

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Q1. What is the minimum NEET score required for MBBS in MP Government College?

A: General category students usually require 550+ marks, but it varies yearly based on cutoffs.

Q2. Can other state students apply for private MBBS colleges in MP?

A: Yes, students from other states can apply under the Management/NRI quota in private colleges.

Q3. How many MBBS seats are there in Madhya Pradesh in 2025?

A: Approximately 5,000+ MBBS seats are available in MP, combining both government and private colleges.

Q4. Is MP good for MBBS studies?

A: Yes, MP offers reputed medical colleges, affordable fees, and well-managed NEET counseling, making it a preferred destination.

Q5. Who conducts MBBS counseling in MP?

A: DME MP (Directorate of Medical Education, Madhya Pradesh) conducts MBBS counseling based on NEET scores.

title: Studying MBBS in Georgia 2025-26: A Complete Guide for Aspiring Medical Students
content:
If you dream of becoming a doctor and are looking for international alternatives, Studying MBBS in Georgia may be the right opportunity for you. With world-class infrastructure, globally recognised degrees and cheap fees, Georgia has become a popular destination for Indian students seeking medical education abroad.

Studying MBBS in Georgia has become a popular alternative among Indian students. Georgian Medical University offers high-quality medical education, affordable school fees and globally recognised degrees. Georgian medical universities are recognised by organisations such as WHO, NMC and ECFMG. 

Georgian medical universities are equipped with advanced facilities, well-stocked libraries, modern laboratories and highly experienced faculties. 

MBBS Cheap in Georgia is an excellent choice for the ambition for medical professionals in search of high-quality education.In this blog, we will discover everything needed to learn about studying MBBS in Georgia for the 2025–26 study years.

Studying MBBS in Georgia Highlights

Studying MBBS in Georgia : Eligibility Criteria

To be eligible for Studying MBBS in Georgia in 2025, Indian students must meet the following criteria:

  • It will be 17 years old by December 31, 2025.
  • Minimum 50% marks in physics, chemistry, and biology (40% for SC/ST/OBC).
  • NEET must be eligible (in accordance with NMC rules).
  • It should be a valid passport.
  • Good command of English (some universities can conduct an interview).

Studying MBBS in Georgia Courses

The MBBS course in Georgia is similar to Western standards and follows the European Credit Transfer System (ECTS). The course includes:

  1. Basic Sciences – Anatomy, Physiology, Biochemistry
  2. Pre-Clinical Subjects – Pathology, Microbiology, Pharmacology
  3. Clinical subjects – medicine, surgery, pediatrics, maternity care and gynecology, psychiatry

Why Studying MBBS in Georgia ?

The main reasons why thousands of Indian students choose Georgia:

  • Globally Recognised Degrees – Approved by NMC, WHO, and USMLE-ready.
  • No Donation or Capitation Fees – Transparent and affordable fee structure.
  • English Medium Instruction – No need to learn a new language.
  • Modern Infrastructure – Digital classrooms, advanced labs, and hospital tie-ups.
  • Safe and Welcoming – Friendly local community and a low crime rate.
  • Pathway to Global Exams – USMLE, PLAB, and FMGE preparation support.

Studying MBBS in Georgia : Admission Process 2025

Here’s a step-by-step breakdown of the admission process:

  1. Research & Choose University – Based on budget, ranking, and recognition.
  2. Apply Online – Submit scanned copies of academic documents.
  3. Receive Offer Letter – From the selected university.
  4. Pay Admission Fees – To confirm your seat.
  5. Apply for Visa – With university invitation letter.
  6. Book Flight & Travel – After getting visa approval.

Most universities begin the intake process from April to May for the September session.

Required Documents

Make sure you have the following documents ready:

  • 10th & 12th Mark Sheets
  • NEET Scorecard
  • Passport (minimum 2 years validity)
  • Passport-size Photos
  • Admission Letter from University
  • Birth Certificate (with Apostille)
  • Medical Certificate (HIV & COVID test)
  • Visa Application Form
  • Travel Insurance

MBBS in Georgia Fee Structure 2025

Here is a general overview of the fee structure for Studying MBBS in Georgia (approximate values):

MBBS in Georgia Vs MBBS in India

Cost of Education

Georgia: Georgian Medical University offers cheap school fees.

India: Private medical universities in India have high school fees.

Admission Process

Georgia: Georgian medical universities have a very simple recording process and require a qualified neet point and basic educational qualifications. 

India: The recording process in Indian medical universities is very competitive based on the NEET point, which has limited seats.

Exposure and Opportunities

Georgia: Georgia provides international risk to its students and opportunities by studying with students from different countries. 

India: Indian Medical University offers rich clinical risk due to the country’s large population and different types of cases.

Duration

Georgia: An MBB’s duration in Georgia is 6 years, including 1 year of clinical internship. 

India: The duration of the MBBS course in India is 5.5 years, including 1 year of internship.

Scholarship

Georgia: Georgian Medical University offers limited scholarships.

India: Indian Medical University offers different authorities and private scholarships.

Quality of Education

Georgia: Georgian Medical University offers English-metropolitical courses and is a global reputation, but language barriers may arise for students during clinical training, including interaction with local patients. 

India: MBBS colleges in India have good education, and there will be no language barriers.

Recognition and Licensing

Georgia: Georgian medical university degrees are globally recognised by WHO and NMC. However, to practise in India, students must pass the FMGE/NExT exam, which has a low passing rate. 

India: Indian medical universities do not require any additional licensing exams; it directly qualifies students for internships and practice in India. 

Postgraduate Graduate Opportunities

Georgia: Graduates have to take additional exams to pursue post-graduation. 

India: Graduates have to clear NEET-PG to pursue post-graduation.

Best Medical Colleges in Georgia for Indian Students

Here are the top NMC-approved medical universities in Georgia:

  1. Tbilisi State Medical University (TSMU)
  2. David Tvildiani Medical University
  3. Batumi Shota Rustaveli State University
  4. Georgian National University (SEU)
  5. East European University
  6. New Vision University
  7. BAU International University Batumi
  8. International Black Sea University

These universities are known for high FMGE passing rates and excellent clinical exposure.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Is Studying MBBS in Georgia valid in India?

Yes, MBBS (MD) from Georgian universities approved by NMC is valid in India. You must pass the FMGE (now next) exam to practise.

2. What is the MBBS period in Georgia?

The total duration is 6 years, including 1 year of internship.

Is NEET compulsory for Studying MBBS in Georgia?

Yes, NEET is compulsory for Indian students to get admission and NMC qualification.

4. Is Georgia safe for Indian students?

Georgia is considered very safe for international students, with a welcoming and peaceful environment.

5. Is scholarship available?

Some universities provide merit-based scholarships. You should check with the university or advisors for specific criteria.

Not necessarily. With proper preparation and university support, many students clear it successfully.

title: The Best Countries for Low-Cost MBBS Education After NEET—Smart Option for Medical Students
content:
If you’ve cleared NEET but your score is too low for a government seat, or you could not afford the high fees in private colleges in India, there’s no need to worry. There are various countries offering low-cost MBBS education with top-quality teaching and global recognition. 

In this blog, we’ll explore the best countries for MBBS abroad that offer affordable medical education after NEET.

MBBS in Russia – Affordable & Globally Recognized

Russia is one of the most preferred destinations for Indian students. The universities here are NMC and WHO approved, and the degree is valid globally.

The duration of MBBS in Russia is 6 years (including internship).
And the top universities are:

  • Kazan Federal University
  • Crimea Federal University
  • Volgograd State Medical University 

Average Fees: ₹2 to ₹4 lakhs per year 

Why Russia?

No donation, English-medium classes, Indian food availability, and strong FMGE results.

MBBS in Kyrgyzstan – Budget-Friendly Option

Kyrgyzstan is gaining popularity due to its low tuition fees and student-friendly environment. It’s one of the cheapest MBBS destinations abroad. 

The duration of MBBS in Kyrgyzstan is 5 years, and the top universities are:

  • Kyrgyz State Medical Academy (KSMA)
  • Osh State University

Average Fees: ₹2 to ₹5 lakhs per year

Why Kyrgyzstan?

Low cost of living, direct admission after NEET, English-taught programs, and NMC-approved colleges.

MBBS in Kazakhstan – Modern Education at Low Cost

Kazakhstan offers modern medical education infrastructure and affordable tuition. Its universities are gaining recognition among Indian students. Offers world-class labs, Indian mess, English-medium MBBS, and a high FMGE passing ratio.

The duration of MBBS in Kazakhstan is 5 years, and the top universities are:

  • Al-Farabi Kazakh National University
  • Semey State Medical University

Average Fees: ₹2 to ₹5 lakhs per year 

MBBS in Georgia—European Standard Education

Georgia is a popular European country known for high-quality medical education at an affordable cost. It offers a modern lifestyle and safe environment. 

The duration of MBBS in Georgia is 6 years, and the top universities are: 

  • Tbilisi State Medical University
  • Batumi Shota Rustaveli State University

Average Fees: ₹3 to ₹6 lakhs per year

Why Georgia?

Safe for Indian students, globally accepted degree, no entrance test other than NEET.

MBBS in Nepal—Just Like Home

If you want to study MBBS in a familiar culture with no language barrier, Nepal is a great choice. The curriculum is similar to India’s, and no visa is needed. The duration of MBBS in Nepal is 5.5 years, and the top universities are 

  • Top Universities:
    • Kathmandu Medical College
    • B.P. Koirala Institute of Health Sciences

Average Fees: Around ₹10 lakhs per year

Why Nepal?

No entrance test (besides NEET), a Hindi-speaking environment, MCI/NMC recognition, and close to home.

Admission Process for MBBS Abroad Step-by-Step Guide

If you want to study MBBS abroad, you need to follow a proper process to get admission to a recognized foreign medical university. Here is the complete step-by-step process:

Step 1: Clear NEET Exam 

Step 2: Choose the Right Country & University

Step 3: Apply to the University. 

Step 4: Get Admission Letter

Step 5: Apply for Passport (if not ready)

Step 6: Visa Application 

Step 7: Pay Tuition Fees 

Step 8: Flight Booking & Travel

Step 9: Airport Pickup & Hostel Allotment

Step 10: Start Your MBBS Journey!

Important Tips 

  • Always choose NMC-approved universities
  • Keep all documents ready and verified
  • Check FMGE passing ratio of the university

Final Thoughts

Choosing to pursue low-cost MBBS education after NEET is no longer just an option—it’s a smart and budget-friendly option for Indian students. Countries like Russia, Kyrgyzstan, Kazakhstan, Georgia, and Nepal offer low-cost MBBS education without compromising on quality or global recognition. 

With the right guidance, timely documentation, and an NMC-approved university, you can fulfill your dream of becoming a doctor without burning a hole in your pocket.

So don’t let a low NEET score stop you — explore your low-cost MBBS education abroad options today and take the first step toward a successful medical career!

  • Transparent Process & Genuine Guidance

We ensure you don’t miss any opportunity to pursue your MBBS dream — regardless of your score.

title: MBBS Abroad with Low NEET Score in 2025 – A Guide by AR Group of Education
content:
Are you worried about your low NEET Score 2025 point? Don’t lose hope. While a low NEET point can limit the possibility of getting a medical seat in India, it does not mean that your medical dream is the end of the dream. Many prestigious international universities accept Indian students with low NEET scores, providing cheap quality training.

In the AR group of education, we specialise in helping students pursue MBBS abroad with low NEET points. Here’s all you need to know.

Why Do Indian Students Choose MBBS Abroad with Low NEET Scores?

  1. Limited Government Seats in India
  2. High Competition & Cut-Offs
  3. Expensive Private Medical Colleges
  4. Affordable Tuition Fees Abroad
  5. Global Recognition of Foreign Medical Degrees
  6. High FMGE Passing Rate from Some Countries
  7. Modern Infrastructure & English Medium Education
  8. Easy Admission Process with No Hidden Donation

Best Countries for MBBS Abroad After Low NEET Score 2025

Here’s a list of top countries accepting Indian students for MBBS even with low NEET marks:

Top MBBS Universities Abroad Accepting Low NEET Scores 2025

How to Choose the Right University Abroad with a Low NEET Score?

Choosing the right university is critical for your career. Follow these steps:

  1. Verify NMC & WHO Recognition
  2. Check FMGE Passing Rate
  3. Choose English Medium Programs
  4. Look for Indian Student Support & Hostels
  5. Check Cost of Living and Safety
  6. Review Clinical Training Opportunities

Tips to Choose the Right Medical University Abroad (2025)

  • ✔ Prefer Government/Public Universities
  • ✔ Research Alumni Reviews
  • ✔ Attend Webinars & Counseling Sessions
  • ✔ Confirm University Ranking & Infrastructure
  • ✔ Ask About Visa Success Rate & Travel Support

FAQs: MBBS Abroad with Low NEET Score – 2025

Q1. Can I study MBBS abroad with a NEET score below 150?
 A: Yes, many countries such as Russia, Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan accept NEET-qualified students with low scores.

Q2. Is NEET mandatory to study abroad in 2025?
 A: Yes, the NEET qualification (passed) is mandatory according to NMC guidelines.

Q3. Do I have to give IELTS/TOEFL for MBBS abroad?
 A: No, most universities in CIS countries do not require IELTS/TOEFL.

Q4. Are degrees valid from abroad in India?
 A: Yes, if the university is recognized by the NMC, and you clarify FMGE.

Q5. Is MBBS abroad cheaper than private colleges in India?
 A: Absolutely. MBBS programs abroad can save 30-40 lakh compared to Indian private colleges.

Q6. What is the MBBS period abroad?
 A: In general, it occurs from 5 to 6 years of internships.

Q7. Can I practise in India after completing an MBBS abroad?
 A: Yes, after clearing FMGE (foreign medical degree exam) or the next exam (from 2026).

Q8. How do I apply for MBBS abroad with a low NEET point?
 A: Connect to the AR group of education – we will guide you from application to admission.

Q9. Is a hostel and food available for Indian students abroad?
 A: Yes, Indian food and separate hostels are available in most top universities.

Q10. Which is the safest country for Indian students with a low NEET score?
A: Countries such as Georgia, Russia and Kazakhstan are considered very safe and student friendly.

title: Low NEET Score? Options After Low NEET Score: Don’t Give Up On Your MBBS Dream!
content:
Cracking NEET is an important milestone for any medical aspirant in India. However, not everyone scores enough to do so in the most important medical colleges in the authorities. But don’t expect! A low NEET point does not mean that the end of your dream is to become a doctor.

In the AR group of education, we help students navigate alternative routes and make smart decisions. In this blog, we will guide you with a list of medical colleges in India that accept the low NEET point and suggest how to secure MBBS entry despite the low ranking.

NEET Cut off 2025

The NEET 2025 cutoff was introduced via NTA on June 14, 2025, alongside side the result. While the NEET 2025 cutoff rating has been reduced, the NEET cutoff percentiles remain the same. The following desk brings the class-smart NEET cutoff 2025 rankings and percentiles.

Can You Get MBBS Admission with a Low NEET Score?

Yes, in reality! There are several personal, deemed, and management quota seats to be had for students with low NEET scores. While reduce-offs might also range year to year, your NEET qualification gives you a chance to discover one-of-a-kind options.

Top Options After Low NEET Score

1. Private Medical Colleges in India

Private faculties offer MBBS admission under management or NRI quota. These seats normally have decreased cut-offs and are ideal for students with low ratings.

 Popular states: Uttar Pradesh, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Rajasthan

  Examples: Santosh Medical College, Subharti Medical College, SRM Medical College

2. Deemed Universities

Deemed universities centralised counselling through MCC (Medical Counselling Committee) and provided seats with barely lower NEET score necessities.

Examples:

  • D.Y. Patil Medical College (Pune)
  • Kalinga Institute of Medical Sciences (Bhubaneswar)
  • Amrita Institute of Medical Sciences (Kochi)

3. MBBS Abroad

This is one of the most favoured and less expensive options for Indian college students. Countries like Russia, Uzbekistan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Georgia, and Nepal offer best training and MCI/NMC-identified levels.

Benefits:

  • No donation
  • Low tuition fees
  • Global exposure
  • English-medium education

 Popular choices:

  • MBBS in Russia
  • MBBS in Uzbekistan
  • MBBS in Kazakhstan

4. Allied Health Sciences Courses

If you don’t want to pursue an MBBS, there are different healthcare-related diploma applications:

  • BDS (Dental)
  • BAMS (Ayurveda)
  • BHMS (Homeopathy)
  • BPT (Physiotherapy)
  • B.Sc. Nursing
  • B.Sc. Radiology / Medical Lab Technology

These courses offer excellent career opportunities in the medical and healthcare field.

Types of Colleges Accepting Low NEET Score

  1. Private Medical Colleges
  2. Deemed Universities
  3. Management Quota Seats
  4. NRI Quota Seats
  5. State Quota in Certain States (Low Cut-off States)

List of Medical Colleges for Low NEET Score Admission

Here is a list of some popular colleges where students with low NEET scores can apply:

 Private Medical Colleges

Top States with Lower NEET Cut-offs

Students with low NEET scores can benefit by applying in states where cut-offs are generally lower:

  • Uttar Pradesh
  • Karnataka
  • Tamil Nadu
  • Puducherry
  • Rajasthan

Key Tips for MBBS Admission with Low NEET Score

  1. Apply for Management/NRI Quota Seats
  2. Target Deemed Universities
  3. Consult Education Experts like AR Group of Education
  4. Stay Updated with Counseling Dates

title: NEET Result Out 2025 – Check Your Score Now!
content:
Check the NEET UG result date Here; it is expected to be announced today on neet.nta.nic.in, with over 22.7 lakh candidates awaiting their scorecards.

By AR Group of Education

The NEET 2025 result has officially been declared! This is a huge moment for lakhs of medical aspirants across India. If you appeared for the NEET UG 2025 exam, it’s time to check your performance and take the next step in your journey to becoming a doctor.As your trusted guide in medical education, AR Group of Education brings you everything you need to know about the NEET 2025 result, how to check it, download your scorecard, and plan your next move.

NEET Results 2025 – Key Highlights

NEET Exam Result – Important Dates

How to Check NTA NEET 2025 Result?

Candidates can easily check their NEET-UG Result 2025 online with these steps:

  • Visit the official NTA website(s) – nta.ac.in/ and neet.nta.nic.in/
  • Input your Application Number and Date of Birth (or password) – the same details used during NEET 2025 registration.
  •  Click the “Submit” button to proceed.
  •  You will be taken to the next page where your NEET result will be displayed.
  • Download and save your scorecard. Don’t forget to take a printout for counselling and admission requirements.

What to Do After NEET Result 2025?

After downloading your result, you should:

  • Check the NEET cut-off marks for your category
  • Compare your score with previous years’ cut-offs
  • Apply for counseling (AIQ and State Quota)
  • Explore MBBS options if not qualified in India – AR Group of Education can help you!

NEET 2025 Rank & College Predictor

The NEET 2025 Rank & College Predictor helps aspirants estimate their All India Rank (AIR) based on their expected marks and predict the medical colleges they may get admission into. This tool uses previous years’ cut-off trends, category-wise seat allotment, and counseling data to provide accurate predictions.To use the NEET Rank Predictor, candidates must enter their expected NEET score, category, and state of domicile. The predictor will then generate an estimated AIR rank and suggest possible medical colleges under the All India (AIQ) and State Quota. The College Predictor considers factors like seat matrix, reservation policies, and past admission trends to suggest the best government and private medical colleges where a candidate can secure admission. 

How to Calculate NEET Percentile 2025?

The NEET percentile is calculated using the formula: Percentile = (Your NEET Rank / Total Candidates) × 100

The NEET percentile is calculated using the formula: Percentile = [(Total Candidates – Your Rank) / Total Candidates] × 100

  FAQs About NEET Result 2025

1. Where can I check my NEET 2025 result?

You can check it on the official NTA website: https://neet.nta.nic.in

2. What credentials do I need to log in?

You need your NEET Application Number, Date of Birth, and Security Pin.

3. Can I get my result via SMS or email?

No, the result is available only through online login. However, NTA may send notifications via registered email or mobile.

4. What details are available on the NEET scorecard?

It includes subject-wise marks, total marks, AIR, category rank, cut-off, and qualifying status.

5. What is the NEET 2025 cut-off?

The cut-off varies by category and year. Once released, NTA will publish it on the official website.

6. What is the qualifying percentile for NEET?

Typically, it is 50th percentile for General, 40th for SC/ST/OBC, and 45th for PwD candidates.

7. What if I forgot my NEET application number?

You can retrieve it using the “Forgot Application Number” option on the NTA website.

8. Can I apply for rechecking of NEET result?

No, NTA does not allow rechecking or revaluation of NEET answer sheets.

9. Can AR Group of Education help with MBBS admission after NEET?

Absolutely! We guide students through MBBS in India and abroad, including admission, counseling, and visa process.
