title: MBBS Abroad For Indian Students : Countries, Colleges & Budget
content:
Pursuing an MBBS abroad for Indian students has become an increasingly popular choice due to limited seats and high competition in Indian medical colleges. Many students now opt to study medicine in countries with high-quality education, recognized degrees, and affordable tuition fees. 

This guide provides comprehensive insights into pursuing MBBS abroad for Indian students, covering everything from the advantages to eligibility criteria, the admission process, and top destinations.

Why Choose MBBS Abroad?

MBBS abroad is an ideal option for Indian students or international students. Many students wish to pursue budget-friendly medical degrees with globally recognized degrees. Below we mention some reasons to study MBBS abroad. 

  • Affordable tuition fees: Medical education in countries like Russia, Georgia, China, and the Philippines often comes at a fraction of the cost of private medical colleges in India.
  • No Entrance Exam: Only NEET qualification is necessary for studying MBBS abroad. 
  • Global Recognition: Medical universities abroad are recognized by international bodies such as the World Health Organization (WHO), the National Medical Commission (NMC), and the Educational Commission for Foreign Medical Graduates (ECFMG).
  • State-of-the-Art Facilities: Many foreign medical universities offer advanced facilities, high-tech labs, and world-class infrastructure, providing students with hands-on training and clinical exposure.
  • Multicultural Environment: Studying MBBS abroad exposes Indian students to a diverse, multicultural environment, improving their adaptability and communication skills.

Eligibility Criteria for MBBS Abroad

The eligibility criteria for MBBS abroad for Indian students may vary depending on the country and university. However, the general requirements are as follows:

  • Age: Students must be at least 17 years old by the 31st of December of the year of admission. 
  • Academic Qualification: The student should have completed 10+2 with a science stream (Physics, Chemistry, and Biology) and scored a minimum of 50% (40% for reserved categories) in PCB.
  • NEET Qualification: NEET qualification is mandatory for Indian students planning to pursue MBBS abroad to practice medicine in India after graduation.
  • English Proficiency: Some universities may require proof of English language proficiency through IELTS/TOEFL scores.

Admission Process for MBBS Abroad

The admission process for MBBS abroad for Indian students generally includes the following steps:

  • Research: Choose the country and medical university that suits your budget and preferences.
  • Application: Fill out the application forms and submit the required documents, such as academic transcripts, NEET scores, passports, etc.
  • Offer Letter: If your application is accepted, the university will issue an offer letter.
  • Visa Processing: Apply for a student visa by submitting necessary documents, including the university’s offer letter.
  • Travel and Accommodation: Book your tickets and arrange for accommodation in the host country.
  • Start Your Studies: Join the university and begin your journey in the field of medicine.

Required Documents for Admission

  • 10th and 12th-grade mark sheets
  • NEET scorecard
  • Birth certificate
  • Passport-sized photographs
  • Passport copy
  • Medical fitness certificate
  • ID Proof 
  • Migration certificate

Top Countries to Study MBBS Abroad

Here’s a table of some popular countries offering MBBS abroad, along with the average duration and estimated fees:

These countries are renowned for offering quality medical education at a more affordable cost compared to private medical colleges in India.

Advantages of MBBS Abroad for Indian Students

  • High-Quality Education: Medical universities abroad often have an updated curriculum that aligns with global medical standards, providing students with cutting-edge knowledge.
  • Direct Admission: Unlike in India, many countries offer direct admission into the MBBS program without an entrance exam.
  • Exposure to International Medical Practices: Studying MBBS abroad allows students to understand different healthcare systems and practices, increasing their global employability.
  • NMC/WHO Recognition: Most foreign medical universities are recognized by the National Medical Commission (NMC) and the World Health Organization (WHO), making the degree globally accepted.
  • Easy Admission Process: The admission process is generally more straightforward, with minimal paperwork.

Challenges of MBBS Abroad

While pursuing MBBS in abroad for Indian students has many benefits, some challenges include:

  • Language Barrier: In countries where the primary language is not English, students might need to learn the local language for clinical interactions.
  • Cultural Differences: Adapting to a new culture can be difficult, especially for students traveling abroad for the first time.
  • FMGE Screening Test: After completing MBBS abroad, students must clear the Foreign Medical Graduate Examination (FMGE) to practice in India.

Things to Consider Before Applying

Before opting for MBBS abroad, Indian students should consider:

  • University Recognition: Ensure that the university is recognized by NMC, WHO, and other global medical bodies.
  • Course Structure: Check if the course curriculum aligns with the Indian medical syllabus.
  • Internship Opportunities: Find out if the university offers clinical internships and hands-on experience during the course.
  • Living Conditions: Research the cost of living, accommodation options, and climate of the country.

Scholarships for MBBS Abroad

Studying MBBS abroad has become a viable alternative for Indian students facing intense competition in India. With various countries offering affordable and high-quality education, MBBS abroad for Indians opens doors to diverse career opportunities globally. However, it is crucial to carefully research the universities, eligibility criteria, and career prospects before making a decision.

FAQs

1. Is NEET compulsory for pursuing MBBS abroad for Indian students?

Yes, NEET qualification is mandatory for Indian students who wish to study MBBS abroad and practice medicine in India after graduation.

2. What is the average cost of MBBS abroad?

The cost varies depending on the country and university, but on average, it ranges from INR 15 to 50 lakhs for the entire course duration.

3. Can Indian students practice in India after completing MBBS abroad?

Yes, after completing MBBS abroad, students need to clear the Foreign Medical Graduate Examination (FMGE) to practice medicine in India.

4. Which countries are the most popular for MBBS abroad for Indian students?

Russia, Ukraine, China, the Philippines, and Kazakhstan are some of the most popular countries for MBBS abroad .

5. How long is the MBBS course abroad?

The course duration generally ranges from 5 to 6 years, depending on the country’s educational system.

title: Top Medical Colleges In India, Fees, Courses 2024-25
content:
The total number of recognized top medical colleges in India is around 740 (as per the NMC website). Medical education in India is highly sought after, attracting thousands of aspirants every year. 

With a vast number of medical institutions across the country, selecting the best ones is essential for a promising career in healthcare. 

India’s top medical colleges offer world-class education, advanced infrastructure, experienced faculty, and excellent placement opportunities, making them a prime choice for aspiring doctors. 

Overview of Top Medical Colleges in India

India boasts several medical colleges recognized globally for their rigorous academic curriculum and quality training. 

These institutions are renowned for producing top-tier medical professionals, with some colleges ranking among the best in the world. 

The medical colleges are mainly governed by the National Medical Commission (NMC), and admissions are based on various competitive entrance exams, such as the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET-UG and PG).

The top medical colleges in India not only provide degrees such as MBBS, MD, and MS but also offer specialty courses like MCh and DM. Many of these colleges are affiliated with top hospitals, providing hands-on training and exposure to real-world medical challenges. 

Their state-of-the-art research facilities and collaborations with global institutions add to their prestige.

Highlights of Top Medical Colleges in India (2024)

Admission 2024 for Top Medical Colleges in India

MBBS in India – admission process for 2024 in India’s top medical colleges primarily relies on the NEET (National Eligibility cum Entrance Test) scores. 

NEET is the gateway for undergraduate (MBBS/BDS) and postgraduate (MD/MS) medical admissions across the country.

Key Points for Admission: 

  • Undergraduate Admission (MBBS): Candidates must clear the NEET-UG 2024 exam and participate in the All India or State Counseling process for seat allocation. 
  • Postgraduate Admission (MD/MS): For postgraduate medical programs, candidates need to clear NEET-PG 2024 and meet the eligibility criteria set by individual colleges.
  • Application Process: The application process usually begins in April for NEET, followed by exams in May or June. Counseling rounds for admissions start in July or August.
  • Reservation: Many top medical colleges follow the reservation policy of the government, ensuring seats for SC/ST/OBC and economically weaker sections.

List of Top Medical Colleges in India with Fees (2024)

Top Medical Colleges in India with the highest placement offer 

One of the biggest advantages of studying at top medical colleges in India is the excellent placement opportunities. 

Many of these institutions have tie-ups with reputed hospitals and medical research centers, providing their graduates with highly paid positions and research opportunities. 

  • AIIMS New Delhi: offers placements with salaries up to ₹2.5 lakhs per month for fresh graduates, with opportunities to pursue further studies and research at reputed global institutions.
  • CMC Vellore: Graduates often find placements in top private and government hospitals, with initial packages ranging from₹1.5 ₹ to₹2 lakhs per month.
  • AFMC Pune: Medical officers are commissioned directly into the Armed Forces with starting salaries of ₹75,000 per month, alongside various allowances and perks.
  • MAMC New Delhi: Offers placements in prestigious government hospitals like Lok Nayak Hospital, with salaries ranging from ₹1 to ₹1.5 lakhs per month.

Top Medical Colleges in India: Exam Schedule 2024 

For more details visit @ AR Group Of Education

Conclusion

Top medical colleges in India offer an excellent platform for students to pursue their dreams of becoming medical professionals. 

With world-class education, affordable fees, and excellent placement opportunities, these institutions remain the top choice for medical aspirants in the country. 

If you are preparing for medical admissions in 2024, start early, focus on cracking NEET, and aim for one of these prestigious colleges!

FAQs

Q1. Which is the No. 1 medical college in India?
The All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi, is widely regarded as the best medical college in India.

Q2. What is the fee structure for AIIMS New Delhi?
The fees for MBBS at AIIMS New Delhi are among the lowest in the country, around ₹1,628 annually.

Q3. What is the admission process for MBBS in India?
MBBS admissions in India are based on NEET-UG scores, followed by a counseling process conducted at the national or state level.

Q4. What is the highest salary offered to MBBS graduates from AIIMS?
The highest salary offered to MBBS graduates from AIIMS can go up to ₹2.5 lakhs per month, especially for top performers and those placed in reputed hospitals or research centers.Q5. When will NEET UG 2024 be conducted?
NEET UG 2024 is expected to be held in May 2024, with applications starting in April.

title: NEET PG Counselling Registration – 2024
content:
The NEET PG counselling registration is among the most important steps for medical graduates aspiring to secure seats in postgraduate medical programs across India. After qualifying for the NEET PG exam, candidates must register for the counselling process to compete for admission into their desired institutions. 

This article covers all the essential details related to NEET PG counselling registration, eligibility criteria, important dates, the counselling process, and information about the state quota seats. 

The MCC connected the NEET PG counselling in three rounds: AIQ 1, AIQ 2, and AIQ 3, followed by a stray vacancy round for leftover seats. 

NEET PG Counselling: Eligibility

Before starting the NEET PG counselling registration, candidates must ensure they meet the eligibility criteria for participating in the counselling process. Below are the key eligibility requirements for NEET PG counselling:

  1. Qualification: Candidates must have qualified for the NEET PG 2024 exam by securing the required minimum percentile.
  • For general candidates: 50th percentile
  • For SC/ST/OBC candidates: 40th percentile
  • For PWD candidates: 45th percentile
  1. Degree Requirement: Candidates should hold an MBBS degree from a recognized medical institution.
  2. Internship: Aspirants must have completed or be completing their one-year internship by March 31, 2024, to be eligible for counselling.
  3. Medical Council Registration: Registration with the Medical Council of India (MCI) or the State Medical Council is mandatory.
  4. Domicile Criteria: For state quota counselling, candidates should fulfill the domicile criteria set by the respective states.

Candidates meeting these criteria can proceed with the NEET PG counselling registration for both All India Quota (AIQ) seats and state quota seats.

NEET PG 2024 Dates

The Medical Counselling Committee (MCC) will announce the NEET PG counselling registration dates for 2024 after the release of the NEET PG exam results. Below is a tentative timeline for the key events:

Note: First round counselling registration dates are confirmed by the MCC, but from the second round, these are tentative dates. Candidates must regularly check the official MCC website for updated timelines.

NEET PG Counselling Process

The NEET PG counselling registration process is straightforward but requires attention to detail. Below is a step-by-step guide to help you understand the process better:

Step 1: Registration on MCC Portal

  • Visit the official MCC website (www.mcc.nic.in).
  • Click on the “PG Medical Counselling” tab and select NEET PG counselling registration.
  • Provide your NEET PG 2024 roll number, date of birth, and other required details to generate login credentials.
  • After generating your credentials, log in and fill in the necessary details, such as personal information, educational qualifications, and contact information.

Step 2: Payment of Counselling Fees

After completing the NEET PG counselling registration, candidates must pay a non-refundable registration fee and a refundable security deposit. The fee structure is as follows:

Make sure to pay the fee through online methods such as credit card, debit card, or net banking.

Step 3: Choice Filling and Locking

Once the payment is successful candidates can select their preferred colleges and courses from the available options. You can select multiple options, and it is advisable to prioritize your choices carefully.

  • Choice Filling: Choose the courses and colleges based on your NEET PG score and preferences.
  • Choice Locking: After making your selections, lock your choices. Ensure you review the list before locking, as no changes can be made after this step.

Step 4: Seat Allotment

Based on the choices filled and your NEET PG rank, the MCC will allot seats. The seat allotment is done in multiple rounds, and the result will be publish on the official MCC website.

  • Round 1: If you are allotted a seat in Round 1, you must either accept the seat or upgrade to the next round.
  • Round 2: If you didn’t get a seat in Round 1 or opted for an upgrade, you will be considered for Round 2.
  • Mop-up Round: If there are vacant seats after the first two rounds, a mop-up round will be conducted. 

Step 5: Reporting to the Allotted College

Once the seat is allotted, candidates must report to the allotted medical college within the given timeline. They need to carry all original documents for verification and complete the admission formalities.

Step 6: State Quota Counselling

Candidates who wish to apply for state quota seats must also participate in the NEET PG counselling registration process for their respective states. Each state conducts its counselling separately, and the domicile criteria will apply.

Important Points to Remember

  • The NEET PG counselling registration is mandatory for all candidates seeking admission to PG medical courses in India.
  • Make sure to keep an eye on the official MCC website for notifications regarding dates and other details.
  • Double-check all documents before attending the counselling session to avoid any last-minute hassle.
  • Stay updated with the counselling processes for both All India Quota and State Quota seats to maximize your chances of securing a seat.

For more information visit us @ AR Group Of Education

Conclusion

The NEET PG counselling registration process is an essential step for candidates looking to pursue postgraduate medical education in India. By carefully following the registration process, paying the fees, and filling out choices based on preferences, candidates can increase their chances of securing admission into top medical colleges. 

Make sure you stay updated with the timelines and fulfill all eligibility requirements for both the All India Quota and the State Quota counselling processes.

Frequently Asked Questions

  1. How to do registration for NEET PG counselling?

Visit the official website of mcc.nic.in and click the link for NEET PG Counselling registration, then click on the registration yourself. Once done with the application form, pay the fee and submit the application form. 

  1. What is the fee for NEET PG 2024 counselling?

Registration fees depend upon the category. Check below 

  • General category: Rs. 25,000
  • SC/ST/PWD: Rs  10,000
  • For deemed universities (all categories): Rs. 2,000,000
  1. How many seats are available for NEET PG 2024?

Through the NEET PG counseling, candidates will be admitted to approx. 26,188 MD seats, 13,649 MS seats, 1,979 PG Diploma seats, and 1326 DNB CET seats. 

  1. How many rounds are in NEET PG 2024 Counseling?

There are 4 rounds for NEET PG counselling i.e., AIQ 1, AIQ 2, AIQ 3, and Stray vacancy round.

title: NCR Medical College Meerut Fees, Courses, Admissions 2024

With a focus on producing competent medical professionals, National Capital Region Institute of Medical Sciences, Meerut provides a conducive learning environment through practical exposure, extensive research opportunities, and a comprehensive curriculum.

Highlights of NCR Medical College Meerut for PG Courses

Admission Requirements for PG Medical Course at NCR Medical College Meerut

To secure admission to the PG medical courses (MD/MS) at National Capital Region Institute of Medical Sciences, Meerut, candidates must fulfill specific eligibility criteria and follow the admission process outlined by the college. Here are the detailed admission requirements:

  1. Educational Qualification: Candidates must have completed their MBBS degree from a recognized medical institution with a mandatory one-year internship.
  2. Entrance Exam: Admission to the PG courses at NCR Medical College Meerut is primarily based on the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test for Postgraduate (NEET-PG) scores. The candidates must qualify for NEET-PG with a valid score.
  3. Internship Completion: Aspirants must have completed their MBBS internship by March 31st of the year they are applying for PG admission.
  4. Counseling and Seat Allocation: After the NEET-PG results are announced, candidates must participate in the UP State Medical Counseling for seat allocation. Admissions are made strictly based on merit and seat availability.
  5. Document Verification: During the counseling process, candidates need to present the following documents:
    • NEET-PG Admit Card and Scorecard
    • MBBS Degree Certificate and Mark Sheets
    • Internship Completion Certificate
    • Medical Council Registration Certificate
    • Passport-size Photographs
    • Valid ID Proof (Aadhaar, Passport, etc.)

Fee Structure for PG Medical Courses at NCR Medical College Meerut

National Capital Region Institute of Medical Sciences, Meerut offers an affordable fee structure for its PG courses, making it an ideal choice for aspiring doctors. The fee may vary based on the specialization chosen and government norms. Below is a general overview of the PG course fee structure:

Note: The fee structure is subject to change as per college norms and government regulations. Candidates are advised to check the official website or contact the college administration for the latest fee details.

Facilities and Infrastructure University

Why Choose NCR College Meerut for PG Courses?

National Capital Region Institute of Medical Sciences, Meerut is a preferred choice for medical aspirants due to its comprehensive curriculum, experienced faculty, and holistic approach to medical education. 

The college’s focus on both academic and practical training ensures that students are well-prepared for their future careers. Furthermore, the college’s reasonable fee structure makes it accessible to a broader range of students.

Conclusion

NCR Medical College Meerut is a prestigious institution offering a robust platform for students aspiring to pursue postgraduate medical education. With its well-structured courses, expert faculty, state-of-the-art infrastructure, and affordable fees, it stands as a top choice for PG medical studies. 

If you are aiming to pursue your PG in medical sciences, NCR Medical College Meerut might just be the perfect destination to achieve your academic and professional goals.

title: Russian Medical University : Top Russian MBBS Universities 2024
content:
Best Russian medical university pursuing an MBBS abroad is a significant decision for medical aspirants. Among the many destinations, Russia has emerged as a preferred choice due to its globally recognized universities, affordable education, and advanced healthcare system. 

Those candidates who plan to study MBBS in Russia, so choose the best Russian medical university. Russia is the home of top medical universities; there are more than 54+ medical universities, and they provide outstanding world-class medical education. 

Top Russian Medical University 2024-25

Russia is home to several top-notch medical universities known for their academic excellence, experienced faculty, and state-of-the-art infrastructure. These institutions have a strong global presence, offering an MBBS degree recognized by the World Health Organization (WHO), the National Medical Commission (NMC), and other international bodies. 

The best Russian medical university provides a curriculum that integrates practical and theoretical learning, offering students hands-on experience with advanced medical equipment. Many of these universities conduct their courses in English, making them an ideal choice for international students, particularly from India and neighboring countries.

Highlights of the best Russian medical university

How are Russian MBBS colleges the best?

1. Global Recognition and Accreditation: The best Russian medical university is accredites by the World Health Organization (WHO), the National Medical Commission (NMC), and other international medical councils, allowing graduates to practice medicine worldwide. 

2. Affordable Tuition Fees: One of the key reasons for considering Russian MBBS colleges the best is their affordability. The annual fees at the best Russian medical university range between USD 4,000 and USD 6,000, which is significantly lower than private medical colleges in many countries. 

3. Modern Infrastructure and Advanced Technology: Russian medical universities are providing best modern infrastructure and advanced medical equipment. 5. Simplified Admission Process

Unlike other countries, Russian universities have a streamlined admission process. There is no entrance exam conducted in Russia for MBBS admissions. Students are selected based on their NEET scores and academic performance in high school, making the process less stressful.

In conclusion, the best Russian MBBS colleges offer a blend of quality education, affordable fees, and a globally recognized degree, making Russia a top choice for aspiring medical professionals.

Yes, the medical degrees awarded by the best Russian medical university are recognized by major global medical councils, including WHO and NMC. Graduates can practice medicine in various countries after clearing the respective licensing exams.

Sudha Medical College Eligibility Criteria

Documents Required

title: NEET PG Counselling Schedule 2024 Dates & Schedule
content:
NEET PG Counselling Schedule 2024 is the timeline followed by MCC to fill these seats. Candidates who qualify for the NEET PG exam and fulfill the eligibility criteria can register for the counselling process online. Knowing the counseling dates is important to ensure you don’t miss any crucial deadlines.

NEET PG counselling ensures that eligible candidates secure seats in government, private, and deemed medical universities. In 2024, the Medical Counselling Committee (MCC) will conduct counselling sessions for 50% of All India Quota (AIQ) seats, while respective state bodies will handle the remaining 50% for state quota seats.  

The National Eligibility cum Entrance Test for Postgraduates (NEET PG) is the entry gate to admission into various MD/MS and PG Diploma courses across India. After clearing the NEET PG exam, the next critical step is the counselling process. 

In this blog, we will cover the essential aspects of NEET PG counselling 2024, including key dates, steps to register, reservation criteria, and more.

NEET PG Counselling Schedule 2024

NEET PG counselling Schedule is a centralized process conducted by MCC for admission to postgraduate medical courses. The counselling process is divided into several rounds: Round 1, Round 2, Mop-up Round, and a stray vacancy round (if necessary). Both AIQ seats and state quota seats are offered through this process.

For AIQ, MCC conducts the counselling, while state quota seats are managed by the respective state authorities. It’s essential to understand the process clearly to ensure timely and correct registration.

There will be four rounds to the counseling process: Round 1, Round 2, Round 3, and the AIQ Stray vacancy round. 

NEET PG Counselling 2024: Key Dates

To help candidates plan better, here’s an overview of important dates for NEET PG Counselling 2024:

Note: These dates are provisional and subject to change. Candidates are advised to keep checking the official MCC website for updates.

NEET PG Counselling 2024: Steps to Register

The registration process for NEET PG counselling is straightforward but requires careful attention to detail. Follow these steps to successfully register for the counselling process:

  1. Registration on the MCC Website:
    • Visit the official MCC website (www.mcc.nic.in).
    • Click on the link for NEET PG 2024 counselling registration.
    • Fill in your NEET PG roll number, application number, and other required details to register.
  2. Payment of Counselling Fee:
    • After registering, candidates must pay the non-refundable registration fee and a refundable security deposit.
    • The fee varies depending on the category and the type of institution (government/private) chosen.
  3. Choice Filling and Locking:
    • Once the fee is paid, candidates can select their preferred colleges and courses.
    • Ensure to lock the choices before the deadline to avoid automatic locking.
  4. Seat Allotment:
    • Based on the candidate’s rank, preferences, and availability of seats, the seat allotment result will be published.
    • Candidates can view their seat allotment status on the official website.
  5. Reporting to the Allotted College:
    • Candidates who are satisfied with their allotted seat must report to the designated college within the stipulated time frame to complete the admission process.
    • Failing to report will result in forfeiture of the seat and the security deposit.
  6. Upgradation Option:
    • If candidates wish to participate in subsequent rounds for better preferences, they can opt for seat upgrades during reporting.

NEET PG Reservation Criteria for AIQ Counselling

NEET PG counselling for AIQ seats follows specific reservation policies to ensure representation across various categories. Here’s a breakdown of the reservation criteria:

Note: OBC reservation is applicable only in central institutions such as AIIMS, JIPMER, and others.

Other Important Information Regarding NEET PG Counselling 2024

1. Seat matrix: The seat matrix will be displayed before each round of counselling, allowing candidates to make informed decisions during the choice-filling process. The seat matrix will list the number of available seats across various institutes.

2. Free Exit in Round 1: In the first round of counselling, candidates have the option to not join the allotted seat without any penalties. This is referred to as a “free exit.” No deduction from the refundable deposit will be made.

3. Exit with Forfeiture in Round 2: After Round 2, if candidates do not wish to join the allotted seat, they may exit with forfeiture, which means the refundable deposit will not be returned.

4. Mop-Up Round: This round is conducted to fill the vacant seats after the two main rounds. It is primarily aimed at Deemed/Central universities and certain private colleges.

5. Document Verification: Candidates are required to submit their original documents for verification during the reporting process. The key documents required are:

  • NEET PG Admit Card
  • NEET PG Scorecard
  • MBBS Degree Certificate
  • Internship Completion Certificate
  • Photo ID (Aadhaar, Passport, etc.)
  • Caste/Category Certificate (if applicable)
  • PwD Certificate (if applicable)

6. Reporting to the College: Once allotted a seat, candidates must physically report to the college with all original documents. The college will verify these documents before finalizing the admission.

For more information visit : AR Group Of Education

Summary 

NEET PG counselling Schedule is the most important step in your medical career, so make sure you are well-prepared and timely in completing each step. Good luck with your journey ahead!

Frequently Asked Questions 

Q1. What is the NEET PG 2024 Counselling registration fee?

The registration fee for NEET PG counselling varies by category: general candidates need to pay ₹1,000, while SC/ST/OBC/PwD candidates pay ₹500. Additionally, there’s a refundable security deposit of ₹25,000 for government colleges and ₹2,00,000 for deemed universities.

Q2. Can I participate in the second round of counselling without attending Round 1?

Yes, candidates can participate in Round 2 for seat upgrades without losing their seats from Round 1. If upgraded, the new seat will be allotted; otherwise, the Round 1 seat remains.

Q3. What happens if I do not join the allotted seat after Round 2?

If you do not join the allotted seat after Round 2, you forfeit the refundable deposit, and the seat is lost. It is considered an “exit with forfeiture.”

Q4. What is a free exit in NEET PG counselling?

A free exit allows candidates to not join the seat allotted in Round 1 without any financial penalty. This option is only available in Round 1.

Q5. How can I check the NEET PG seat allotment result?

The NEET PG seat allotment result will be published on the official MCC website. Candidates can check the result by logging in with their credentials.

title: UP NEET UG Counselling For The Second Round 2024
content:
The UP NEET UG counselling for the second round is a very important phase for medical aspirants who dream of securing a seat in MBBS/BDS programs across Uttar Pradesh. 

After the first round of seat allocation, many students may not get their desired colleges, or they might want to boost their seats. 

On September 9, 2024, registration for the second round of UP NEET 2024 counseling opened. To take part in the UP seat distribution process, candidates must register on the official website, upneet.gov.in, and submit an application. 

September 13, 2024, is the deadline for registering for UP NEET 2024 cycle 2. On September 14, 2024, a merit list for round two will be made public. 

Options for second-round counseling can be selected between September 14, 2024, and September 18, 2024. On September 19, 2024, the results of the second round of UP NEET 2024 counseling will be announced. 

Overview of UP NEET UG Second Round Counselling

The UP NEET UG second round of counselling is conducted by the Directorate General of Medical Education and Training, Uttar Pradesh (DGME UP). 

This counselling is for those candidates who did not secure a seat in the first round or those who wish to upgrade their allotted seats. 

This round includes government and private medical/dental colleges across the state.

Key features of the second round:

  • Eligibility: Candidates who did not participate in the first round, those who did not get a seat, and those looking for seat upgrades are eligible for this round.
  • Vacant Seats: The second round fills the vacant seats left after the first round of counselling.
  • New Registrations: Candidates who didn’t register for the first round can also register for the second round.

UP NEET UG Counselling 2024 Schedule

Here is the schedule for UP NEET UG Counselling 2024 Round 2:

Candidates are advised to keep an eye on the official website of DGME UP for the latest updates regarding the exact dates.

UP NEET UG Counselling 2024 Round 2 Registration Process

The registration process for the UP NEET UG counselling second round is quite simple, but candidates must ensure they follow the steps carefully to avoid any mistakes. Here is a step-by-step guide for the registration process:

  • Step 1: Visit the official website of DGME UP for NEET UG Counselling (e.g., www.upneet.gov.in).
  • Step 2: Click on the NEET UG round 2 seat allotment registration link. 
  • Step 3:  Enter the required details and submit the registration fees. 
  • Step 4: Enter the choices for allotment. 
  • Step 5: Save the application and click to apply. 

UP NEET UG Counselling 2024: Documents Required 

All the candidates who are eligible for the NEET UP state quota counselling need to submit some necessary documents. It’s mandatory to carry the original copy of these documents. 

  • NEET UG admit card
  • NEET UG scorecard 2024
  • Class 10th mark sheet
  • Class 12th mark sheet
  • Domicile certificate 
  • A valid photograph 
  • ID proof 
  • Caste certificate 
  • Online registration slip, etc. 

Important Points to Remember for UP NEET UG Counselling Round 2

  • Upgradation Option: Candidates who have already secured a seat in the first round but wish to upgrade to a better college can participate in the second round without losing their current seat.
  • Free Exit: If a candidate is allotted a seat in the second round but does not want to accept it, they can choose not to join the college. This will be considered a “free exit,” and no penalty will be imposed.
  • Document Verification: Ensure that all necessary documents are ready and verified before reporting to the allotted college.

Conclusion

The UP NEET UG second-round counselling provides a crucial opportunity for students to secure their dream medical seat. 

By following the schedule and registration process diligently, candidates can enhance their chances of getting admission to top medical colleges in Uttar Pradesh. 

Keep track of the important dates, ensure all documents are in place, and stay updated with official notifications to make the most of this round of counselling. Good luck to all the aspiring medical students!

If you face any query, contact us and visit our website, i.e., AR Group of Education. 

Frequently Asked Questions 

  1. Who is eligible for UP NEET UG second-round counselling?

Candidates who didn’t secure a seat in the first round, those who opted for an upgrade, and new candidates who didn’t participate in the first round are eligible for the second round of counselling.

  1. Is it necessary to register again for the second round if I participated in the first round?

No, candidates who have already registered for the first round do not need to register again. However, they must participate in the choice-filling process for the second round.

  1. What happens if I do not lock my choices during the choice-filling process?

If you do not lock your choices, your preferences will not be considered during the seat allotment process, and you may lose the opportunity to get a seat in the second round. 

  1. Can I participate in the second round if I have already secured a seat in the first round?

Yes, candidates who have secured a seat in the first round can still participate in the second round for an upgrade. However, if a seat is allotted in the second round, the earlier seat will automatically be forfeited.

  1. What documents are required for reporting to the allotted college in the second round?

Candidates must carry their NEET UG scorecard, allotment letter, class 10th and 12th mark sheets, identity proof, domicile certificate, caste certificate (if applicable), and other relevant documents while reporting to the allotted college.

Eligibility Criteria For MBBS Abroad

If you also want to take MBBS admission abroad, you should satisfy all the eligibility criteria put forth by different countries to admit foreign national students. Each country has its own requirements, but normally for all countries with the best medical education, the eligibility criteria remains constant.

Following are the basic eligibility criteria that have to be satisfied in order to get MBBS admission abroad.

  • The candidate has to pass the intermediate level (10+2) with Physics, Chemistry, Biology (PCB) and English as mandatory subjects.
  • The minimum Aggregate score required for MBBS Admission Abroad depends on the country that you choose. All the interested candidates are advised to check the score requirement of the country that you wish to choose.
  • The candidate should at least be 17 years old during his/her admission or should attain the same age by the end of the year.
  • NEET UG 2024 Qualification is generally required for all countries. 


Documents Required For MBBS Abroad

Documents are a very important component during any admission. All the candidates are advised to check with their respective college about the documents that have to be furnished during the verification process. It’s better to get all documents ready beforehand to avoid any last minute hassles.

Mentioned below is a list of documents required for MBBS Admission Abroad.

  • 12th Class Marksheet and Certificate

NEET is the medical entrance examination of India and in other countries only NEET Qualification is required which is easy provided that you prepare well for it.

title: Best MD Colleges In India – PG NEET | MD/MS In India
content:
India is home to some of the finest medical colleges offering Doctor of Medicine (MD) programs, making it a top destination for aspiring medical professionals. The best MD colleges in India are renowned for their state-of-the-art facilities, experienced faculty, and wide range of specializations. 

In India, there are more than 800 top MD schools. 200+ of them are privately held, 140+ are owned by the government, and the other 15+ are semi-government-owned. The greatest entrance exams for admission to the top MD institutions in India are NEET PG, AIAPGET, INI CET, and NEET. The top MD schools in India include KGMU, PGIMER, Kasturba Medical College Manipal, CMC Vellore, and AIIMS Delhi.

An MD degree opens up numerous career paths in medicine, including specializations in general medicine, cardiology, dermatology, and more. This post will highlight the best medical colleges for pg, entrance exams for admission, and the top specializations offered by these institutions.

Highlights of Best MD Colleges in India

MD programs in India typically last three years and require candidates to have completed an MBBS degree along with a one-year internship. Admission to the best medical colleges for pg is based on entrance exams like NEET PG, INI-CET, and JIPMER. 

Best MD Colleges in India: Entrance Exams 2024

Admission to the top MD colleges in India is highly competitive; those candidates who desire to pursue an MD program in India are required to clear national-level entrance exams. The most popular exams for MD admissions are:

  2. INI-CET 2024: This is the entrance exam for MD courses at prestigious institutes like AIIMS, PGIMER, JIPMER, and NIMHANS.
  3. JIPMER PG 2024: The Jawaharlal Institute of Postgraduate Medical Education and Research (JIPMER) conducts its own exam for MD admissions.

  • General Medicine
  • Pediatrics
  • Dermatology
  • Cardiology
  • Radiology
  • Neurology
  • Anesthesiology

Top specializations offered by the best MD colleges in India 

The India Top MD colleges offer a pathway to advanced medical careers with a variety of specializations and world-class education. Whether choosing a government or private institution, pursuing an MD in India ensures that you are well-equipped to excel in the medical field. Visit us for more information @ AR Group Of Education.

Candidates must have completed an MBBS degree and a one-year internship to be eligible for MD programs.

The main entrance exams are NEET PG, INI-CET, and JIPMER PG.

Both government and private colleges offer excellent education, but government colleges tend to have lower fees.

title: UP NEET UG First Round Result 2024 Seat Allotment Result
content:
The Directory of Medical Education and Research (DMER) Uttar Pradesh has released the UP NEET UG first round result seat allotment result for the National Eligibility Cum Entrance Test (NEET-UG) counselling, 2024. Those candidates who applied for UP NEET UG counselling 2024 for MBBS and BDS can check their allotment status on the official website, upneet.gov.in. 

Thousands of medical aspirants have eagerly awaited the UP NEET UG counselling result for 2024. This result marks a crucial step in the journey for students aiming to secure a seat in some of the top medical colleges in Uttar Pradesh. The first-round result is a significant milestone, as it sets the tone for the subsequent rounds of counseling and the eventual allocation of seats.

As per the counselling schedule, candidates who were allotted seats in the first round must download the allocation order and report to the allotted institution by August 31st, 2024, to September 5th, 2024. 

How to Check the UP NEET UG first round result

Candidates are advised to follow these simple steps to check their UP NEET UG first round result:

  1. Visit the official website: upneet.gov.in.
  2. Click on the link for “UP NEET UG First Round Result 2024.”
  3. Enter your NEET roll number, application number, and password.
  4. Click on “Submit” to view your result.
  5. Download and take a printout of the result for future reference.

The UP NEET UG counselling first-round result will include details such as the candidate’s name, NEET roll number, the college allotted, and the category under which the seat has been allotted. It’s important to carefully review all the information provided in the result.

Documents verification

  • 10th or 12th result
  • NEET UG 2024 scorecard
  • Domicile certificate
  • Category certificate 
  • Passport-size photographs 
  • Medical certificate, etc. 

 What to Do After the UP NEET UG first round result

Once the UP NEET UG first round result is declared, candidates who have been allotted a seat must follow the steps outlined below:

  1. Reporting to the Allotted College: Candidates must report to the allotted college within the specified dates mentioned in the table above. It must be important to carry all necessary documents, including the NEET UG admit card, scorecard, allotment letter, and other required certificates.
  2. Fee Payment: After reporting to the college, candidates need to pay the admission fee as per the college’s guidelines. Failure to do so within the given timeframe will result in the forfeiture of the allotted seat.
  3. Upgradation and Second Round Counseling: Candidates who are not satisfied with their allotted seat or wish to try for a better option can opt for upgradation. They can participate in the second round of counseling, where they may get a chance to secure a seat in a preferred college.

Conclusion

The order of the UP NEET UG counselling first-round result is a moment of relief and joy for many students. However, it also sets the stage for further decision-making regarding their medical education. 

Candidates need to act promptly, complete the necessary formalities, and stay informed about the subsequent rounds of counseling. For those who did not secure a seat in the first round, there are still opportunities ahead, and they should remain hopeful and prepared for the upcoming rounds.

The UP NEET UG first round result is just the beginning, and with careful planning and timely action, candidates can navigate the process successfully to secure their place in one of the prestigious medical colleges in Uttar Pradesh.

For expert guidance to make your bright career, contact us and visit our website for more details. i.e., AR Group of Education.

For MBBS admission in UP 2024, a total of 8485 seats are available in government or private medical colleges in Uttar Pradesh.

  1. How to download the seat allotment letter for NEET UG?

Candidates now access their seat allotment letter by logging in with their application number, password, and 14-digit PIN on the official website. 

  1. When will UP NEET UG first round result?

As per the Directory of Medical Education and Research (DMER), the seat allotment for the UP NEET UG first round is by August 31st, 2024.

title: NEET PG Scorecard 2024 Released -Download
content:
The NEET PG scorecard is a crucial document for candidates aspiring to pursue postgraduate medical courses in India. It reflects the performance of candidates in the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test for Postgraduates (NEET PG) and plays a vital role in determining their admission to various medical colleges across the country. 

This NEET PG scorecard key information includes the candidate’s name, roll number, date of birth, category, total number out of 800, and their All India Ranking (AIR). Check out this article for more information. 

Overview of NEET PG Score-card

The National Board of Examinations (NBE) announced the NEET PG results on August 23. Candidates can download their NEET PG scorecard 2024 starting August 30, 2024. The scorecard 2024 will display essential details such as the candidate’s name, roll number, scores obtained, and overall rank.

The scorecard is the gateway to securing a seat in prestigious medical colleges for postgraduate studies. It serves as proof of the candidate’s eligibility and performance, making it one of the most important documents in the entire admission process.

How to Download the NEET PG Scorecard 2024

Downloading the NEET PG scorecard 2024 is a straightforward process. Follow these steps to access your scorecard:

  1. Visit the Official Website: Go to the official website of the National Board of Examinations (NBE) at nbe.edu.in.
  2. Navigate to the NEET PG Section: On the homepage, locate and click on the NEET PG section.
  3. Click on the Scorecard Link: Look for the link that says “NEET PG Scorecard 2024″ and click on it.
  4. Login to Your Account: You will be prompted to log in using your NEET PG credentials, such as your registration number and password.
  5. Download the Scorecard: Once your scorecard 2024 is displayed on the screen. Review the details and download the scorecard in PDF format.
  6. Print the Scorecard: It is advisable to print multiple copies of the scorecard for future reference, as it will be required during the counseling and admission process.

NEET PG Score-card 2024: Details Mentioned

The details listed are the details that will be mentioned on the NEET scorecard. Candidates can refer to cross-check the details. Here’s what you can expect to find on your NEET PG scorecard:

  • Candidate’s Name: Your full name as registered during the NEET PG application process.
  • Roll Number: Your unique roll number is assigned for the NEET PG exam.
  • Application Number: The number you received during the application process.
  • Date of Birth: Your date of birth as per the records.
  • Category: Your category, such as General, OBC, SC, or ST.
  • Scores: Your scores in each section of the exam.
  • Total Marks: The total marks you obtained in the NEET PG exam.
  • Percentile: The percentile score, which reflects your performance relative to other candidates.
  • Rank: Your All India Rank (AIR) based on your NEET PG scores.
  • Qualifying Status: Indicates whether you have qualified for the exam based on the minimum cutoff criteria.
  • Cutoff Score: The cutoff score for your category.

NEET PG 2024 Scorecard: Importance

The NEET PG 2024 is more than just a document; it is a gateway to your future as a medical professional. Here’s why the NEET PG 2024 scorecard is so important:

  1. Eligibility for Counseling: The scorecard determines whether you are eligible for the NEET PG counseling process. Only candidates who meet the cutoff criteria will be allowed to participate in the counseling sessions.
  2. Admission to Colleges: The NEET PG 2024 scorecard is used by medical colleges to assess your eligibility for various MD/MS/PG Diploma courses. Higher scores increase your chances of securing a seat in top medical institutions.
  3. Ranking and Seat Allotment: Your All India Rank (AIR) mentioned on the scorecard is a critical factor in the seat allotment process. Candidates with higher ranks are given preference during counseling.
  4. Proof of Qualification: The scorecard serves as official proof that you have qualified for the NEET PG exam, which is a mandatory requirement for postgraduate medical education in India.

NEET PG Counselling 2024

Once the NEET PG 2024 scorecard 2024 is released, the next step in the admission process is the NEET PG counseling. The counseling process is conducted by the Medical Counselling Committee (MCC) and various state authorities for different quotas, including the All India Quota (AIQ), State Quota, and Deemed/Central University Quota.

Steps in the NEET PG Counselling 2024

  1. Registration: Candidates must register on the official MCC website to participate in the counseling process.
  2. Choice Filling and Locking: After registration, candidates must fill in their preferred choices of colleges and courses. Choices should be locked before the deadline.
  3. Seat Allotment: Based on your NEET PG 2024, rank, and choices filled, seats will be allotted in various rounds of counseling.
  4. Reporting to Allotted College: Once a seat is allotted, candidates must report to the respective college within the specified time frame to complete the admission process.
  5. Document Verification: During reporting, candidates must carry their NEET PG scorecard 2024 and other required documents for verification.
  6. Upgradation and Further Rounds: Candidates can opt for upgradation if they wish to participate in further rounds of counseling for a better seat. Vist us @ AR Group Of Education

Documents Required for NEET PG Counselling 2024

  • NEET PG scorecard 2024
  • NEET PG admit card
  • MBBS degree certificate
  • Internship completion certificate
  • Permanent/provisional registration certificate issued by MCI or State Medical Council
  • Category certificate (if applicable)
  • Birth certificate or Class 10th passing certificate as proof of age
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar card, PAN card, passport, etc.)

Conclusion

The NEET PG scorecard 2024 is an indispensable document for aspiring medical postgraduates. From determining your eligibility for counseling to securing admission to top medical colleges, the scorecard plays a pivotal role in shaping your medical career. 

Understanding how to download the scorecard, the details it contains, and its significance can greatly assist you in navigating the post-exam process smoothly. As you await the NEET scorecard 2024, ensure that you stay updated with the latest information and prepare diligently for the upcoming counseling sessions.

Frequently Asked Questions 

  1. Is the NEET PG 2024 scorecard released?

The National Board of Examinations (NBE) will release the scorecard on August 30, 2024. 

  1. How can I get my NEET PG scorecard?

Go to the official of nta.edu.in. Open the NEET PG option on the website, scroll down, and click on the link given on the pdf. Search your result by using your roll number. 

  1. What details are included in the NEET PG 2024 scorecard?

The scorecard includes the names of the candidates, roll number, application number, total marks out of the 800, individual marks of each subject, and All India Rank (AIR), etc. 

  1. How many rounds of NEET PG 2024 counselling?

NEET PG counselling has 4 rounds that will be held for 50% of the All India Quota by MCC. Candidates need to register themselves with the official MCC. 

  1. How many years of NEET PG scores are valid?

NEET PG scorecard is valid for only 1 year.

title: NEET PG College Cutoff 2024, Download, Choice-Filling, Dates
content:
The Medical Counselling Committee (MCC) conducts the NEET PG college cutoff to provide admissions in various postgraduate medical courses in India. The NEET-PG cutoff plays a very important role in determining candidates’ eligibility for admission to MD, MS, and diploma courses. Understanding the MCC NEET-PG counselling process and cutoff is necessary for aspirants aiming for postgraduate medical education. 

Based on the NEET PG 2024 counselling 26,168 Doctors of Medicine (MD), 13,649 Masters of Surgery (MS), 922 PG Diplomas, and 1,338 DNB CET seats will be filled. A total of 6,102 colleges and 649 hospitals will participate in NEET PG counseling in 2024. 

What is MCC NEET PG Counselling?

MCC NEET PG 2024 counselling is a centralized process conducted by the Medical Counselling Committee under the Directorate General of Health Services (DGHS), Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India. 

The counselling is conducted for 50% of All India Quota (AIQ) seats, deemed universities, central universities, and ESIC institutes. Through this process, candidates qualified for the NEET-PG exam can secure seats in postgraduate medical courses based on their merit and preferences.

NEET PG Highlights

Before diving into the specifics of the counselling process, it’s important to understand the key highlights of the NEET PG exam:

NEET PG Cutoff for Government Colleges

Those candidates who qualified in NEET PG exam, check below the cutoff table for government medical colleges.

NOTE: Check the closing rank of Deemed Medical Colleges for MD/MS. Download PDF.

Factors determine the NEET PG cutoff in 2024. 

NBE publishes the cut-offs along with the NEET PG results. Only candidates who pass the exam are eligible to proceed with the counseling phase. The NEET PG cutoff is determined by the following factors:

  • Number of candidates who appear for the NEET PG exam. 
  • Total number of seats available at a respective college.
  • The difficulty level of the NEET PG exam.
  • Previous year NEET PG cutoff. 

NEET PG Cutoff 2024 

After the NEET PG cutoff, students need counselling to further process, so below we mention some information related to NEET PG counselling.

NEET PG Counselling 2024 Registration

Registration for NEET PG 2024 counselling is the first and most essential step for candidates who wish to participate in the counselling process. Here’s how it works: 

  1. Online Registration: Candidates need to visit the official MCC (Medical Counselling Committee) website and register themselves by providing the required details, such as their NEET PG roll number, application number, and personal information. 
  2. Payment of Fees: After registration, candidates must pay the counselling fees, which vary depending on the category and type of institution (government, private, or deemed). 
  3. Choice Filling and Locking: Once the registration and fee payment are complete, candidates can proceed to fill in their choices of colleges and courses. After finalizing the choices, they must lock them before the deadline. 
  4. Seat Allotment: Based on the candidate’s rank, preferences, and seat availability, the MCC will release the seat allotment results for each round.

NEET PG Counselling Fees 

This section provide the NEET PG counselling registration fees. Meanwhile, students can check the Medical PG counselling registration fees. Here’s a breakdown:

Important Documents Required for NEET PG Counselling

Candidates must keep the following documents ready for verification during the NEET PG counselling process:

  • NEET PG Admit Card
  • NEET PG Scorecard
  • MBBS Degree Certificate
  • Internship Completion Certificate
  • Permanent or Provisional Registration Certificate issued by MCI or State Medical Council
  • Date of Birth Certificate (Class 10 or 12 mark sheet)
  • Valid Photo ID (Aadhaar Card, Passport, etc.)
  • Caste Certificate (if applicable)
  • PwD Certificate (if applicable)
  • Passport-size photographs

Types of Counselling

MCC conducts different types of counselling depending on the category and type of seats:

  1. All India Quota (AIQ) Counselling: For 50% AIQ seats in government medical colleges across India.
  2. Deemed and Central University Counselling: For seats in deemed and central universities.
  3. ESIC Counselling: For seats in Employee’s State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) medical colleges.
  4. State Quota Counselling: Conducted by respective state authorities for the remaining 50% of state quota seats.

NEET PG 2024 Counselling Dates

The NEET PG 2024 counselling process is conducted in multiple rounds. Below is a table summarizing the important dates:

NEET PG Counselling Round 1

The Medical PG counselling process for round 1 involves initial registration, choice filling, and seat allotment. The table below provides the key details:

The Mop-up Round is conducted to fill the remaining vacant seats after the first two rounds of counselling:

NEET PG Counselling Stray Vacancy Round

The Stray Vacancy Round is the final round, conducted for seats that remain vacant after the Mop-up Round:

NEET PG 2024 Reservation

Conclusion

The MCC NEET PG cutoff and counselling process are critical for securing a postgraduate medical seat in India. Candidates must stay updated on the official MCC website to ensure they meet all deadlines and have the necessary documents ready. Proper planning and timely action during the counselling rounds can significantly increase the chances of securing a seat in a preferred institution.

The counselling will start most likely the second week of September 2024. Candidates will have to register on the official website of the Medical Counselling Committee (MCC) to participate in the seat allotment process.

To register for MCC NEET PG counselling 2024, visit the official MCC website and click on the “New Registration” link. Fill in your NEET PG roll number, application number, and other required details. After submitting the information, pay the counselling fee online. Once the payment is confirmed, proceed to fill in your preferred choices of colleges and courses, then lock your choices before the deadline.

  1. Why is counselling for NEET PG is important?

NEET PG counselling is important because it determines the allocation of seats in postgraduate medical programs based on merit, preferences, and reservation criteria. It ensures a fair and transparent process for candidates to secure admissions in MD, MS, and PG Diploma courses across India.

Around 2 lakh students appear for the NEET PG exam every year. The candidate’s number varies from year to year. This year around 2,28,540 candidates appear for the NEET PG.

title: Eligibility Criteria For NEET PG 2024
content:
The National Board of Examinations has released the eligibility criteria for NEET PG exam 2024 for desired candidates. Only eligible students apply for the NEET PG application form. The National Board of Examinations (NBE)  conducts the NEET PG exam for medical graduates every year.  

Meeting the eligibility criteria for NEET PG 2024 is the first step toward a successful application. Those candidates who desire to do postgraduation in MBBS and want to get admission in the best medical colleges in India. Students need to meet the eligibility criteria for NEET PG. 

Basic Eligibility Criteria

The eligibility criteria for NEET PG 2024 are divided into several categories. These include educational qualifications, internship requirements, nationality, and more. Below is a detailed breakdown:

Educational Qualifications: 

  • Candidates must have an MBBS degree recognized by the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956.
  • An MBBS degree is recognized by the National Medical Commission (NMC).
  • Students must have completed their one-year mandatory internships.
  • You must be an Indian. 
  • Foreign medical graduates pursuing MBBS through NEET PG must register with the National Medical Commission (NMC) before the exam date.
  • International students must have passed the Foreign Medical Graduate Examination (FMGE) conducted by the NBE.
  • Those candidates with disabilities are eligible for reservations under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016.
  • All the students must meet all the other eligibility criteria.

NEET PG 2024 Highlights Table

Here is a summarized table for a quick overview of the  NEET PG 2024:

NEET PG Exam Pattern

The NEET PG exam pattern includes 200 multiple-choice questions that need to be completed in 3 hours and 30 minutes. 

Conclusion

Understanding the eligibility criteria for NEET PG 2024 is essential for all aspirants aiming to take the exam. Ensure that you meet all the qualifications before applying to avoid any last-minute surprises.

So, check your qualifications, complete your internship on time, and make sure you fulfill all the eligibility criteria for NEET PG 2024 to pave your way toward a successful medical career! Visit AR Group Of Education for more.

Frequently Asked Questions 

  1. What are the eligibility criteria for NEET PG exam?

To be eligible for NEET PG, you must have completed the MBBS degree with a recognized medical college and complete your one-year internship. 

  1. Is it mandatory to complete the internship before applying for NEET PG 2024?

Yes, candidates must complete their compulsory rotating internship on or before March 31, 2024, to be eligible for NEET PG 2024.

  1. Can foreign nationals apply for NEET PG 2024?

Yes, foreign nationals can apply for NEET PG 2024. However, they must register with the Medical Council of India (MCI) or the State Medical Council (SMC) before the exam date.

  1. What is the eligibility criterion for Foreign Medical Graduates (FMGs) for NEET PG 2024?

Foreign Medical Graduates must have passed the Foreign Medical Graduate Examination (FMGE) conducted by the National Board of Examinations (NBE) and must be registered with MCI/SMC to meet the eligibility criteria for NEET PG 2024.

  1. Is there any age limit to apply for NEET PG 2024?

No, there is no age limit for candidates applying for NEET PG 2024. The eligibility is primarily based on educational qualifications and internship completion.

  1. Can candidates with a disability apply for NEET PG 2024?

Yes, candidates with disabilities can apply for NEET PG 2024. They are eligible for reservations under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, provided they meet all other eligibility criteria.

  1. I am currently doing my internship, which will be completed after March 31, 2024. Am I eligible for NEET PG 2024?

No, to meet the eligibility criteria for NEET PG 2024, your internship must be completed on or before March 31, 2024.

  1. Is MCI/SMC registration mandatory for applying to NEET PG 2024?

Yes, all candidates, including foreign medical graduates, must be registered with either the Medical Council of India (MCI) or the State Medical Council (SMC) to be eligible for NEET PG 2024.

  1. Do I need to submit my internship completion certificate while applying for NEET PG 2024?

Yes, candidates need to provide proof of internship completion during the application process to fulfill the eligibility criteria for NEET PG 2024.

  1. What happens if I don’t meet the eligibility criteria for NEET PG 2024 after submitting my application?

If a candidate fails to meet the eligibility criteria for NEET PG 2024, their application may be rejected, and they will not be allowed to appear for the examination. It is essential to ensure all criteria are met before applying.

title: Bihar NEET UG Cutoff 2024 For MBBS
content:
The NEET cutoff is the minimum score required for admission to medical and dental colleges. The Bihar NEET cutoff 2024 is influenced by the state reservation policy, which reserves a certain percentage of seats for different categories such as ST/ST/OBC. 

The National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET) is the single-entry exam for aspiring medical students in India to secure a seat in prestigious medical colleges. In Bihar, the competition is intense, and the cutoff marks play a very important role in determining admission. 

The Bihar NEET Cut Off 2024 is excitedly awaited by thousands of students who have appeared for the exam, hoping to secure a seat in one of the state’s medical colleges. Here we describe the details of the Bihar NEET Cutoff 2024.

Bihar NEET Cutoff 2024

The Bihar Combined Entrance Competitive Examination Board (BCECEB) will release the Bihar NEET Cutoff 2024 to offer admission to various medical colleges in India. It is expected that the NEET Cutoff 2024 for Bihar will range from 720 to 120 for this academic year. 

The BCECEB officials will release a separate cut-off score for all the institutions to offer 85% of seats under the state quotes, while 15% of seats will be offered under the All India Quote by MCC Counselling. The highest NEET cut-off, which is Bihar Medical College, closed admission to the MBBS program in the first round. 

Highlights of the Bihar NEET Cutoff 2024  

Bihar NEET Cut Off 2024: Deciding Factors

The Bihar NEET Cut Off 2024 is determined by several factors. These factors are important in deciding the cutoff marks for each category and each round of counseling. Here are the key factors that influence the Bihar NEET Cut Off 2024:

  1. Number of Candidates: The total number of candidates appearing for NEET from Bihar significantly impacts the cutoff. A higher number of candidates leads to higher competition, which may result in higher cutoff marks.
  2. Difficulty Level of the Exam: The overall difficulty level of the NEET exam is another critical factor. If the exam is more challenging compared to previous years, the cutoff marks might be slightly lower.
  3. Availability of Seats: The total number of MBBS and BDS seats available in Bihar also affects the cutoff. With limited seats and a high number of aspirants, the cutoff marks tend to be higher.
  4. Reservation Policies: Bihar follows a strict reservation policy, with a certain percentage of seats reserved for various categories such as SC, ST, OBC, and EWS. The cutoff for each category varies based on the number of reserved seats.
  5. Previous Year Cut-Off Trends: The cutoff marks from previous years also play a role in determining the current year’s cutoff. The authorities consider the previous trends while setting the cutoff for the current year.

Category-wise Bihar NEET Cutoff 2024 

The authorities of the Bihar Combined Entrance Competitive Examination Board (BCECEB) will announce the Bihar NEET cutoff on the official portal to provide admission to medical and dental institutions in the state. 

Note: The above cutoff marks are indicative and based on previous trends. The actual Bihar NEET Cut Off 2024 may vary.

How to Check Bihar NEET Cut-Off 2024?

Once the results are announced, the Bihar NEET Cut-Off 2024 will be released by the Bihar Combined Entrance Competitive Examination Board (BCECEB). Here’s how you can check the cutoff: 

  1. Visit the Official Website: Go to the official website of BCECEB.
  2. Find the NEET Section: Look for the NEET (UG) section on the homepage.
  3. Look at the Cut-Off Link: Click on the link for “Bihar NEET Cut Off 2024.”
  4. Select Your Category: Choose your category to check the cutoff marks.
  5. Download and Save: Download the cutoff list and save it for future reference.

Bihar NEET UG Counselling 2024 

After completing all the things, some documents are required for the Bihar NEET counseling. Below we mention the most important documents. Visit AR Group Of Education.

  • Original NEET UG scorecard
  • UGMAC 2024 rank card
  • Result of 10th or 12th class.
  • Caste certificate (if required)
  • Copy of Aadhar card
  • Any other documents are required 

Conclusion

The Bihar NEET Cut-Off 2024 is a critical aspect of the admission process for medical colleges in the state. Understanding the cutoff trends, category-wise cutoffs, and the factors that influence the cutoff will help candidates better prepare for the counseling process. Keep an eye on the official BCECEB website for the latest updates on the Bihar NEET Cut-Off 2024, and ensure you meet the eligibility criteria for a smooth admission process.

Whether you are aiming for a government medical college or a private institution in Bihar, being aware of the Bihar NEET Cut-Off 2024 will give you a clear picture of your chances and guide you in making informed decisions.

Frequently Asked Questions 

  1. What is the cut for NEET in Bihar?

Bihar NEET cutoff 2024 for the general category is 720 to 164, 164 to 146 for EWS, and 163 to 129 for SC/ST/OBC. 

  1. What is the cutoff for MBBS at Patna Medical College?

Patna Medical College’s cut-off for the general category is 14284, and for the reserve category is 6935. 

  1. How will Bihar MBBS admission 2024 be granted?

 Admission to Bihar MBBS and dental colleges will be granted based on NEET score. 

  1. Who is eligible for Bihar NEET admission under the state quota?

Those candidates who have domicile in Bihar and get qualifying marks in NEET UG are eligible to secure admission under the 85% state quota.

  1. How many government seats are there in Bihar NEET?

There are a total of 2765 seats in Bihar MBBS. Among the 1615 seats are government medical colleges, including AIIMS & JIPMER. 

  1. What is the fee for NEET counseling in Bihar?

Bihar NEET counseling fees for the general category are 1,200 rs, or the SC/ST category are 600 rs.

title: Apply MBBS In Russia: Admission Process For Indian Students
content:
Russia is one of the most significant choices if you are planning to pursue an MBBS degree abroad. With its high-quality education, affordable fees, and globally recognized degrees, Russia has become a popular destination for medical aspirants. Below we talk about how to apply MBBS in Russia.

  2. Check Eligibility Criteria: To apply for MBBS in Russia, students need to meet eligibility criteria. Students must qualify 12th class with 50% marks and complete their 17 years. 
  3. Submit Application Form: After choosing your desired university, visit the official website to fill out the application form. Make sure to apply for MBBS in Russia early, as the admission process can be competitive.
  4. Receive an Invitation Letter: After checking your application, the university will issue an invitation letter if you meet their criteria. This letter is important it confirms your admission and allows you to apply for a student visa. 
  5. Apply for a Student Visa: Indian students need to student visa to apply for MBBS in Russia. The visa process usually takes a 7-8 working days, so ensure you apply in advance.
  6. Plan Your Travel: Once your visa is approved, you can start planning your travel to Russia. It’s advisable to arrive a few weeks before the commencement of your course to get accustomed to the new environment.

Important Tips

  • Climate condition: Russia is a little bit colder in comparison to the Indian winter weather.

Conclusion

Start your journey today and apply MBBS in Russia to make your dream of becoming a doctor a reality. Visit AR Group Of Education for more detail.

According to NMC rules, those candidates who want to get admission must have qualify for the NEET exam for Russian universities.

Yes, Russian universities are recognized by the WHO and NMC, that allows medical degree valid in everywhere including in India or all over the world.

title: Kazakh National Medical University Fees
content:
Kazakh National Medical University (KazNMU) is a top choice for students seeking medical education abroad, particularly in Kazakhstan. Known for its high-quality education, experienced faculty, modern facilities, and Kazakh National Medical University fees, the university attracts students from all over the world. 

However, one of the most important aspects for any student is understanding the Kazakh University fees. Here we describe the university fees. If you are planning to study MBBS in Kazakhstan, choose Kazakh National Medical University as an ideal destination. 

Overview of Kazakh National Medical University Fees

The Kazakh National Medical University fees are structured to provide affordable education to international students while maintaining the quality of education. The fees vary based on the course, duration, and other factors. Below is a breakdown of the Kazakh Medical University fees for the most popular programs.

Undergraduate Programs

  1. MBBS (Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery)
    • Tuition Fees: The Kazakh Medical University fees for the MBBS program range from $4,000 to $5,000 per year. 
    • The total duration of the course is 6 years, which includes five years of academic learning and one year of internship.
    • Hostel Fees: Hostel fees range from $600 to $1,200 per year, depending on the type of accommodation chosen.
  2. Dentistry 
    • Tuition Fees: The Kazakh Medical University fees for the Dentistry program are approximately $4,500 per year. The program duration is five years.
    • Hostel Fees: Similar to the MBBS program, hostel fees for Dentistry students range from $600 to $1,200 per year.
  3. Pharmacy 
    • Tuition Fees: The Kazakh Medical University fees for the Pharmacy program are around $3,500 per year. This course lasts for five years.
    • Hostel Fees: Hostel fees remain consistent, ranging between $600 and $1,200 per year.

Postgraduate Programs

  1. MD/MS (Doctor of Medicine/Master of Surgery)
    • Tuition Fees: The Kazakh Medical University fees for MD/MS programs range from $5,000 to $6,000 per year. The course duration varies depending on the specialization, typically lasting two to three years. 
    • Hostel Fees: Postgraduate students can expect to pay between $600 and $1,200 per year for accommodation.
  2. MDS (Master of Dental Surgery)
    • Tuition Fees: The Kazakh Medical University fees for MDS programs are approximately $5,500 per year. The course duration is generally three years.
    • Hostel Fees: As with other programs, hostel fees range from $600 to $1,200 per year.

Additional Costs

While the Kazakh Medical University fees cover tuition and accommodation, students should also consider additional expenses such as:

  • Food: On average, students spend around $100 to $150 per month on food.
  • Books and Supplies: This can cost between $300 and $500 per year.
  • Medical Insurance: Mandatory for all international students, costing around $100 per year.

Why Choose Kazakh National Medical University?

MBBS in Kazakhstan, understanding the Kazakh Medical University fees is crucial, but it’s also important to recognize the value of the money you receive. The university is renowned for its excellent academic reputation, modern facilities, and the opportunity for students to gain practical experience in affiliated hospitals.

Summary

Understanding the KNMU fees is essential for prospective students planning to pursue their medical education in Kazakhstan. By carefully considering these costs, students can make informed decisions and ensure they are financially prepared for their academic journey.

Frequently Asked Questions

  1. What is the average annual tuition fee for the MBBS program at Kazakh National Medical University?

The average annual tuition fee is around $4,000 to $5,000.

  1. Are the hostel fees included in the KNMU fees?

No, hostel fees are separate and range from $600 to $1,200 per year.

  1. What are the additional costs apart from Kazakh Medical University fees?

Additional costs include food, books, supplies, and medical insurance.

  1. Is there a difference in fees for international students?

No, the Kazakh National University fees are the same for all international students.

  1. Can the KNMU fees be paid in installments?

Yes, the university offers the option to pay fees in installments.

  1. Do Kazakh Medical University fees cover practical training?

Yes, the fees include practical training as part of the curriculum.

  1. Is there a scholarship available to reduce Kazakh National Medical University fees?

The university offers scholarships to meritorious students, which can reduce the fees.

  1. Are Kazakh Medical University fees refundable in case of withdrawal?

Refund policies vary; it’s advisable to check with the administration.

  1. How do Kazakh National Medical University fees compare to other universities in Kazakhstan?

KazNMU fees are competitive and offer good value compared to other medical universities in Kazakhstan.

  1. What payment methods are accepted for KNMU fees?

The university accepts various payment methods, including bank transfers, credit cards, and online payments.
