title: MBBS Bangladesh: Reach out your Academic Goal
content:
We are resilient to any obstruction in the journey of our continuous growth and we always push ourselves to the utmost to prove this statement. We are here for those who want to pursue a medical degree abroad and Bangladesh could be one of the options in your bucket list to study MBBS in Bangladesh. So, let’s start with what usually a candidate seeks out for taking admission into a College of any specific country and there comes the question of “Why to consider Bangladesh” in the top priority among the bunch of abroad countries. 

Most Enrichment Environment For Learning- “MBBS in Bangladesh”

If we talk about Bangladesh as a country, It is having a vibrant history and variability in culture at every mile of distance, Grand festival gatherings which cherish people and purify spirits to engage and interact with not only natives but also the people who come from different origins of the world. 

As a Country…

  • Easier for Students to interact and connect with Faculties, local students and people as mostly speak either “Bangla” and somewhat an accent of “Hindi”.
  • The Medium of education across all universities is English which makes it further more convenient for students. 
  • Similarity in climate and Commonality in preferences over food and daily routine activities.
  • A developing country which is rigorously working in the areas of healthcare and medical sciences and performing better and better from past consecutive years.

As an option for MBBS…

  • Offers a High Standard of Medical Sciences Education  which is comparable to Top private colleges in India.
  • Incorporation of Multiple Latest implements and Machinery coupled with integration of technology and digitally enabled resources. 
  • Safe and Comfortable commute and accommodation and high quality infrastructure in and out of the campus area. 
  • Accessibility to major facilities whether related to academic resources or recreational facilities.

Now we have concrete reasons to look out for MBBS in Bangladesh and for the perfect approach, we AR Group of Education are here to help you out at every step and provide selfless support so you can achieve your academic objectives to study MBBS in Bangladesh and become a medical professional. 

For further progress we will dive into the college details specific to the areas of keen interest in order to provide you with the most precise and concentrated form of information available from all resources over the internet. If we look upon the top Universities and colleges to study MBBS in Bangladesh, there are multiple top tier universities which are renowned in the medical realm all over the world for their specialization in specific departments and their contribution in the field of medical sciences. 

  • Jalalabad Ragib-Rabeya Medical College
  • The University of Dhaka
  • Rajshahi University
  • University of Chittagong
  • Gonoshasthaya Samaj Vittik Medical College

All the listed Universities offer best of its kind Healthcare and medical care facilities. The commonalities across the top listed universities include-

  • Accredited by MCI, WHO & UNESCO which shows the credibility of the listed universities as they follow the prescribed guidelines as per the requirements that are critically necessary for the establishment and recognition of a medical university worldwide.
  • Decades of experience as all the described Universities established more than a couple of decades ago which accommodates senior professional faculties and management team to facilitate the learning and practical working process.
  • The Universities have more than one Hospital and even have sister institutes for certain speciality and muti-speciality Hospital infrastructure.
  • Universities offer state of the art technology integration and cutting edge medical implements and facilities for providing the best in class medical care facilities which includes from the starting point of diagnosing to the end part of getting accurate results.
  • The academic curriculum is similar to what is offered in Indian Medical Colleges and the course is more inclined towards the industry requirements for providing more career opportunities and professional growth.

Till now we all get to know about the features, facilities and services offered by the medical universities in Bangladesh and it is one of the best option to purse mbbs in abroad. From now on, the process of application to take admission come in, which is necessary to be well known by candidates who have made their decisions after thorough research and reading out the above given information i.e.

How to correctly apply for admission in MBBS in Bangladesh?

  • He/She must have passed out in their Boards Examination with the subjects Physics, Chemistry and Biology as major subjects.
  • There is a need to score at least 60% to get enrolled into the Top Medical Universities and colleges but this is not a necessary requirement and even not applicable across all Medical universities.
  • Not more than two years of gap after Class 12th (Higher Secondary).
  • Candidates must have to qualify NEET Examination and the NEET scorecard is a necessary requirement to further proceed your application in your preferred or chosen Universities or Colleges.

title: MBBS in Kursk State Medical University: Dynamic & Influential
content:
Kursk State Medical University is more than an institute and for the students aspiring for MBBS, it is a go to option if you want to embark your journey in medical sciences. The State Medical University is the oldest institution offering Medical, Dentistry and Pharmacy courses.

Embark your Medical journey or planning for post graduation, this is surely your chance to be in one of the most prestigious institutions in Russia which allows students to interact, socialize and indulge across various extracurricular activities to develop professional and interpersonal skills.

Kursk State Medical University at a Glance

Established in 1935 and university status granted in 1994. The city defines its own name as “A city of West Russia” and is one of the oldest cities of Russia. On the order of the people’s commissar health service 1944, the restoration of the university started back then and admitted just 250 students. The level of changes university goes through is ridiculously huge.

At present, the university is under the supervision of current rector ”Prof. Viktor Anatolyevich Lazarenko” and he has an inviting message for the students that here in Kursk State Medical University, students are adorned with a balanced life of Academic learning and engaging cultural events & sporting activities.

Renowned University Worldwide

The Kursk State Medical University marked its name on the stone of excellence & with its relevant innovations and research work in the medical field has achieved the position among the top rankings in the medical realm. The university is accredited by MCI, WHO,ECFMG, WDOMS, FAIMER, AMC, GMC PLAB and its educational programs are accredited across 15 foreign countries.

What Kursk State Medical University is Offering?

No More Restrictions

Making it more convenient & accessible for the students to do MBBS admission in Russia, the university offers-

  • No entrance test
  • No donation policy
  • English medium courses
  • Lowest tuition fees

Over the top Experience

These are the reasons why every year the number of applications are always 2-4 times the number of MBBS admission seats. The experience in our university environment is resilient to all problems related to the academic life of a student- 

  • Offering personalized learning as a student: professor ratio is 13:1 which means more time for you,
  • If you miss any class you will get extra lectures, 
  • Medical treatment & insurance for all students, 
  • Simulation Centre for practical training & real time experience,
  • Academic mobility gives opportunities to students & professors to do research on any particular topic for a specific period of time and enter into the academic schedule.
  • All of this you will get with an international learning environment.

Ease of Living in Kursk

Accommodation provided to all KSMU students and even the overall living cost in Kursk is one of the most economical for doing MBBS in Russia. More options for traveling to reach the campus and back to Room & for touring across the city. 

Conclusion–

In a nutshell, features & facilities like ‘Simulation Centre’, ‘Academic Mobility’ and ‘Innovative technology’ delivers unmatched experience in learning and practical training & application. To enrich the students academic experience furthermore we provide alternate class schedules for better understanding & practice and offer extra classes in free time.

 Lastly, we always encourage the students who visit on our platform “AR Group of Education” to consider russian universities among their top choices in terms of both academic excellence they provide and the most economical package they deliver in every budget.

title: Pursue MBBS in Russia- overall package in one option
content:
You are here, which means either you are planning to pursue an MBBS or a related medical degree or you are just seeking out information to calm your information hunger. And, if you have already planned to study MBBS and qualified for the NEET, then there are a lot more options available for you, not only in India but also abroad, and from the vault of options, we will give you a brief narrative and authentic information about  MBBS in Russia and why it’s some of the most popular options for MBBS aspirants in India.

As we have already mentioned above, it’s mandatory to qualify for the NEET to Pursue MBBS in Russia, and if you are clear with the above statement, we can go further. Russia is a developed country, and the federal government of the country has highly invested in the education and healthcare systems across the country.

Over the years, this investment in quality education, training, infrastructure, and an enduring environment has yielded the productivity Russia is enjoying today.

Cause of the Rigorous Effort

There are multiple perks and benefits that are provided by the university to students of varied origins. This is necessary for improving your overall exposure in a different community of students.

Russia, at the end of the world war, experienced this: if we had to grow independent from our competitors, we had to leverage the skills of mentoring because if you can teach, it transcends not only your skills but also your perceptions and thinking.

To get admission to the top universities, you should be aware of the education system, curriculum, and methodology of teaching. Apart from this, learn how to accommodate, and sustain your life, and participate in academic and cultural events and practices. Let’s start with the entry gate, i.e., the eligibility to get admission to medical universities in Russia.

Eligibility: your first and foremost requirement 

  1. He or she should have completed the minimum academic learning period of 17 years.
  2. He or she must have obtained at least 50% marks in their board examination from either CBSE or ICSE.
  3. You must have all your documents related to your academic learning, like the 10th marksheet, the 12th marksheet, and the NEET report card.
  4. Apart from the academic documents you must need a passport and passport size photograph, HIV Report and a CoVid -19 Report.

Now you are ready to apply for the MBBS course at Russian Medical University and get enrolled in the college as Russia is one of the best country to pursue MBBS in Abroad.

But before knowing the academic details about Russian medical universities, how can you select one?

Brief Narrative over the Academic Pattern about Pursue MBBS in Russia

    1. First and foremost, the medium of learning is available in English, so you don’t have to spend a whole year learning Russian first. There are a total of 12 universities that offer their curriculum in English.
    2. The classes are held on alternate days for each subject, and regular test sessions are taken, particularly by your professors, to bring up your performance in the end.
    3. Every student will be encouraged that the teacher-to-student ratio in most of the classes is 7:1, and this makes it possible to leverage more time from your professors, especially on those days if you miss the classes and your teachers will take extra classes for you.
    4. You can participate in college festivals, annual academic events, and other cultural events not only inside campus but also outside of your academic square.
    5. Finally, you must pass the end-term examination of each semester to further continue your sessions, and if you fail to do so, your professors will help to bring you up by removing your setbacks.
    6. The academic year starts from September of the current year until June of next year, including two semesters, the first ending in January and the latter beginning in February.
    7. Every student, whether native or international, will get medical insurance and treatment facilities from their university.
    8. Your professors will give you personalised mentoring for your further learning in post-medical courses and also for different screening tests conducted across the countries where the student belongs and goes back to practice in medicine, like for Indian students, they provide mentoring for ‘MCI Screening’.
    9.  

    Among the Few Top MBBS Universities in Russia

      • Moscow State Medical University, named after I.M.: Sechenov is centered in Moscow City and was established in 1758. The First state medical university which contributed the most in the field of medical sciences. The University gave the best medical practitioner and the reacher in the healthcare industry.

      • Saint Petersburg State Medical University: Founded in 1897, the university not only cherishes students on its campus but also contributes to innovations in the medical world, especially in the Russian healthcare system.

      • Kabardino Balkarian State University: This university has a renowned history of over 65 years since 1957 and has built its reputation by positioning itself in the top 100 universities in the world accredited by MCI.

      MBBS fees in Russia are more affordable as compared to other universities across the world, and even with the tightest budget, you can surely go further and live your dreams.

      Lastly, your decision to pursue an MBBS in Russia already puts you ahead of all the students who are not even trying to search for opportunities that they must grab before the sands run out from your hand. Studying MBBS in Russia is a more convenient and affordable option, especially for Indian students who want a high standard of learning, practice with cutting-edge technology in healthcare, and state-of-the-art infrastructure for both learning and growing their interpersonal skills. Join hands with the AR Group of Education to get a well-specified University Abroad because your success is our success.

title: From NEET PG to NExT Exam: Changes to Chances
content:
NExT EXAM is a mandatory examination conducted by the Commission or an approved authority in India. It serves two purposes. First, it certifies whether a medical graduate is eligible to practice modern medicine in the country, essentially acting as a Licentiate Examination. Second, it determines the eligibility and ranking of individuals who wish to pursue further education in postgraduate medical specialties. In this regard, it also functions as an entrance examination for admission to postgraduate medical courses. You can learn more about this as you continue reading!

Shedding Light on NExT EXAM

  • It applies to various categories of individuals. Firstly, it includes all undergraduate medical students enrolled in MBBS programs across Medical Colleges approved by the National Medical Commission, including Institutes of National Importance.
  • Secondly, it encompasses Abroad Medical Graduates who meet the requirements specified by the National Medical Commission for obtaining a license to practice medicine in India and for enrollment in the State Register or National Register. The regulations determine the specific manner in which these individuals can pursue these actions.
  • Thirdly, individuals with a Medical Degree who intend to pursue academic courses, observerships, or any other purposes specified and approved by the National Medical Commission through official notifications or regulations fall under the scope of NExT.
  • Lastly, those who have already been granted a license to practice medicine and are registered in the National Medical Register or State Medical Register, and who wish to seek admission to Postgraduate courses in broad medical specialties, are also subject to the NExT examination

NEXT EXAM Step 1 

  • The first step of the NExt Exam will be a theory examination.
  • The questions in this examination will be of the multiple-choice type.
  • The exam will be conducted online using a computer-based format.
  • The specific procedures for conducting common counseling for admission to postgraduate medical courses will be determined by the Commission and notified by the Government of India.
  • Students who have completed their III MBBS/Final MBBS from a recognized medical college approved by the Commission will be eligible to take the NExT Step 1 exam.

Now Get the Opportunities Twice

The Step 1 exams will be held twice a year, in May and November, or as notified by the Commission. Candidates are allowed multiple attempts to pass NExT Step 1 and Step 2 exams within 10 years of joining the MBBS course. However, candidates can only take the Step 1 exam to improve their scores after completing the Step 2 exam. The NExT Step 1 will replace the conventional university/institutional theory exams for III MBBS/Final MBBS Part II. Practical/clinical exams for III MBBS/Final MBBS will continue to be conducted in the conventional manner unless notified otherwise by the Commission.

Syllabus of NEXT Step 1 

The NExT examination will consist of six papers that cover subjects from III MBBS/Final MBBS, both Part 1 and Part 2. The subjects covered are:
●  Medicine and allied disciplines
●   Surgery and allied disciplines
●   Obstetrics and Gynaecology
●   Pediatrics
●   Otorhinolaryngology
●   Ophthalmology
These papers will include the applied aspects of all subjects covered under I MBBS, II MBBS, and III MBBS/Final MBBS Part 1.

Next step 2 

The NExT examination’s second step, known as NExT Step 2, focuses on evaluating practical/clinical skills, clinical decision-making, and communication skills expected of an Indian Medical Graduate. It is an Objective Structured Clinical Case-based examination that involves simulated cases or patients.

The examination is conducted in person by the respective state Health Universities/Institutions, following the standards and guidelines provided by the Commission. In cases where state Health Universities do not exist, the Commission determines the authorized University/Institution to conduct NExT Step 2 for the respective colleges.

Regular NExT Step 2 Examinations are held twice a year, and there is also a NExT Step 2 Supplementary Examination conducted twice a year. The supplementary examination is specifically for candidates who have failed in one or more of the seven subjects and need to repeat those subjects. If a candidate fails in more than three subjects, they must appear in all seven subjects again in the supplementary examination.

There is no limit to the number of attempts a candidate can make in NExT Step 2, as long as they have passed both NExT Step 1 and NExT Step 2 Examinations within 10 years of joining the MBBS Course.

Syllabus of next step 2 

NExT Step 2 is a Practical/Clinical and Viva Voce Examination.The purpose of NExT Step 2 is to assess practical skills, clinical knowledge, and decision-making abilities of the candidates.

● It covers seven clinical subjects/disciplines, including Medicine and allied disciplines, Surgery and allied disciplines, Obstetrics and Gynaecology, Paediatrics, Otorhinolaryngology, Ophthalmology, and Orthopaedics and PMR (Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation).
● The examination is conducted in an Objective Structured format.
● It is a Clinical Case Based examination, meaning that it focuses on real-life clinical scenarios.

Eligibility for Provisional Internship

Indian Medical Graduate: A student from a recognized medical college in India is eligible for compulsory rotating internship if they meet the following criteria:
a) Pass all six theory papers of NExT Step 1.
b) Pass the practical/clinical examination of III MBBS/Final MBBS (Part 2) regulated by the university/institution.

Foreign Medical Graduate:

A student who completed their medical education overseas and fulfills the requirements specified by the Foreign Medical Graduate Regulations of the Commission is eligible for compulsory rotating internship if they pass all six theory papers of NExT Step 1.

Eligibility for Medical Registration and Practice

Indian Medical Graduate or Foreign Medical Graduate: To be registered in the National Medical Register/State Medical Register and obtain a license to practice medicine in India, the following conditions must be met:

a) Completion of the required duration of internship as applicable.
b) Passing the NExT Step 2 practical/clinical examination.
c) Meeting the requirements stated in the Registration of Medical Practitioners and License to Practice Medicine Regulations of the National Medical Commission.
d) Fulfillment of any other requirements specified by the Commission.
Eligibility for Postgraduate Medical Courses:

Graduates with Medical Registration: Those who have obtained a license to practice medicine and are registered in the National Medical Register/State Medical Register are eligible for admission to postgraduate medical courses in broad specialty subjects, provided they meet the following criteria:
a) Eligible and granted a license to practice modern medicine in India.
b) Participation in the common counseling process as prescribed by the Commission through an approved designated authority.

Apart from the mentioned eligibility criteria, the NExT Step 1 scores can be used by the Government of India, State Governments, and other organizations for purposes such as employment, scholarships, fellowships, etc.

Note: The MBBS degree is awarded upon passing the Final MBBS examination and successfully completing a one-year internship.

Important dates for next exam

EventDate of Exam/CommencementDate of Result/Completion
NExT Step 1May/November1st week of June/December
III MBBS/FINAL MBBS Part 2University 1st Week June/December3rd Week June/December
Internship1st January/July31st December/30th June following year
NExT Step 2 Regular3rd Week June/December4th week of June/December
NExT Step 2 Supplementary1st week Sept/March3rd week Sept/March
Post Graduate AdmissionMay-June (Counseling)30th June
Postgraduate Course1st July/1st week January–

Note: There will be another round of counseling in December for any remaining vacant seats after the earlier counseling held in May-June.

title: Top 5 Russia Medical Universities for Indian Students
content:
Indian students for the MBBS degree should consider Russia. Students are drawn to their lifestyle and high-quality education. In Russia, the MBBS program lasts for five to six years. Students looking for the best country to study medicine abroad will find the MBBS in Russia to be the ideal option. Russian medical universities give you high-quality education at a very affordable cost, and all medical universities have a solid infrastructure and the most excellent facilities, making MBBS in Russia the best alternative, especially for Indian students. The Russian medical university has a very advanced and well-developed educational system. The majority of Russian universities provide MBBS programs in English. The W.H.O.  and the NMC approve the medical university in Russia.

Russia is one of the nations where Indian students go to pursue MBBS degrees overseas. The top medical universities in Russia provide students with the greatest medical education at affordable fees. Because the best medical institutes in Russia have straightforward admissions policies, many Indian candidates prefer to do their MBBS in Russia. In this article, we will guide you through the top 5 medical universities in Russia 
 

1. North Caucasian State Academy, Russia

Established in 1991, the North Caucasian State Medical Academy has emerged as a leading centre of medical education in North Russia. The academy holds authorization and recognition from prestigious bodies such as the World Health Organization, the Medical Council of India and the Foundation for Advancement of International Medical Education and Research. 
This college is home to more than 5,500 students, making it a vibrant and diverse learning community. One of the major attractions for international students is the globally recognized MBBS in Russia at an affordable cost. 
 
The fee structure for North Caucasian State Academy is as follows:
 
College fee: 2,90,000 rubles per year
Hostel fee: 40,000 rubles per year
 
In terms of rankings, according to the 4ICU rankings, the North Caucasian State Medical University is positioned at 82nd place in the country. 
 

2. Pskov State Medical University, Russia

Pskov State Medical University, established in 1932, is a highly esteemed institution of higher education located in the Northwestern region of Russia. The university has a rich history and strong research in various fields, providing students with a conducive environment to develop their skills and become accomplished medical professionals through the utilization of scientific methodologies. The university holds recognition and approval from reputable bodies such as the National Medical Commission and the World Health Organization.
 
The fee structure for Pskov State Medical University, Russia is as follows:
 
College fee:2,98,000 Rub rubles per year
Hostel fee: 7000 rubles per year
 
In terms of rankings, according to the 4ICU rankings, the Pskov State Medical University, Russia positioned at 219 places in the country. 
 

3. Kazan State Medical University, Russia 

Kazan State Medical University, established in 1814, is an institution operating under the governance of an academic council and adhering to the university’s regulations. It has been officially recognized by the State Committee of the Russian Federation of Higher Education, obtaining license number 16 G – 235 on March 6, 1994. This license grants the authority to engage in professional education activities, solidifying its status as a reputable educational institution. This multi-institutional setup allows for a diverse range of educational programs and opportunities within the university.
 
The fee structure for Kazan State Medical University, Russia is as follows:
 
Tuition  fee: 4,38,000 rubles per year
Hostel fee: 15,000 rubles per year
 
In terms of rankings, according to the 4ICU rankings, the Kazan State Medical University, Russia positioned at 33 places in the country.
 

4. Kazan Federal Medical University, Russia

Kazan Federal University, located in Kazan, Russia, is renowned as one of the most prestigious universities in the region. It has a rich history, dating back to its establishment in 1804 as Imperial Kazan University, and has maintained its esteemed status as it evolved into Kazan Federal University. The university’s exceptional infrastructure and advanced teaching methodologies have attracted Indian and international students alike.
 
As the second largest university in Russia, Kazan Federal University offers a vast range of educational opportunities. Each year, over 50,000 students enroll in the university’s 700+ degree programs, spanning both undergraduate and postgraduate levels. The university’s commitment to academic excellence is evident through its diverse curriculum and extensive program offerings.
 
The fee structure for Kazan Federal Medical University, Russia is as follows:
 
Tuition  fee: 4 68,000 rubles per year
Hostel fee: 12,000  rubles per year
 
In terms of rankings, according to the 4ICU rankings, the Kazan Federal Medical University, Russia positioned at 5 places in the country.
 

5. First Moscow State Medical University 

I.M. Sechenov First Moscow State Medical University (Sechenov University) is recognized as one of the top medical universities for studying MBBS in Russia. With its establishment dating back to 1758 as the medical faculty at Imperial Moscow University, it holds a rich history of being a famous institution in the Russian Healthcare System. Over the years, it has been home to numerous renowned scientists and physicians who have made significant contributions to the advancement of medicine. Today, Sechenov University has changed into an academic, research, and resource centre of excellence within the Russian Healthcare System. The university is dedicated to shaping the Medicine of the Future through a strong academic foundation in clinical medicine.
 
The fee structure for the first Moscow Medical University, Russia is as follows:
 
College fee: 6,98,000 rubles per year
Hostel fee: 90,000  rubles per year
 
In terms of rankings, according to the 4ICU rankings, the first Moscow Medical University, in Russia is positioned at 17th place in the country.
 
Conclusion 
AR Group of Education is dedicated to providing a supportive and comfortable experience for its Indian students studying at MBBS in abroad. To ensure their well-being, the group has arranged various facilities such as hostel accommodations, an Indian mess, and counsellors, and representatives in universities for real-time assistance. With our team of experienced and well-informed counsellors, we provide comprehensive assistance to help you choose the right college and obtain your visa and travel arrangements. We continue to provide support until you reach your desired college.

title: NEET UG Official Answer Key 2023 Released
content:
The national eligibility entrance test for undergraduate or NEET UG 2023 , answer key was made public by the National Testing Agency ( NTA)  on 13 June 2023. The organization has previously stated that the NEET UG 2023 result and the official answer key will be released jointly. On the official website, neet.nta.nic.in as well as ntaresults.nic.in, applicants who took the exam can view the answer key and results. 

The NEET UG 2023 test was given on May 7. 20,87,449 students took the exams at 4,097 locations in 499 cities. On June 13, 2023, the results of the NEET UG were released. For the convenience of the candidates, the testing organisation has now made the final answer key of the exam public. With 720 points, Prabhanjan J and Bora Varun Chakravarthi have achieved Rank 1. Kaustav Bauri finished third with a score of 716. 

NEET UG Final Answer Key 2023: How to download

 Here are the steps to download the final answer key for NEET UG 2023 : 

  • Open your web browser and visit the official website of NTA NEET at neet.nta.nic.in.
  • look for the “NEET UG Final Answer Key 2023” link.
  • Click on the “NEET UG Final Answer Key 2023” link, and a new page will load.
  • On the new page, a PDF file containing the answer key will open automatically.
  • Take your time to carefully review the answers provided in the PDF file, comparing them with your own responses from the NEET examination.
  • If necessary, you can download the PDF file by clicking on the download button usually located on the top right corner of the PDF viewer.
  • Once the file is downloaded, You can either print it out or save it in a secure location on your device.
Over 20 lakh students enrolled for NEET UG 2023, while 8,81,967 male candidates showed up for the test. A remarkable 4,90,374 of the applicants among them were qualified. On the other hand, there were 11,56,618 more female applicants who registered and showed up for the test. Outstandingly, 6,55,599 female candidates passed the medical examination.
 
Visit Now :- NEET UG Rank Predictor 2023
 
NEET UG 2023 Important dates 
The NEET UG exam was conducted on May 7, 2023. In Manipur, the NEET UG exam took place on June 6, 2023. The provisional answer key for NEET was released on June 4, 2023. Candidates were given until June 6, 2023, at 11:50 PM to challenge the provisional answer key. For Manipur candidates, the last date to challenge the provisional answer key was June 12, 2023, till 4 pm.
 The final answer key for NEET was released on June 13, 2023. On the same day, June 13, 2023, the NEET results were also announced.
 

NEET UG 2023 Marking Scheme 

After the completion of the NEET 2023 exam, the NTA officials will allot marks to the candidates based on the marking scheme. Here is the marking scheme for NEET 2023:
 
Correct Answer: For each correct answer, candidates will be awarded four marks (+4).
Incorrect Answer: For each incorrect answer, one mark will be deducted (-1).
 
Unanswered: If a question is left unanswered, no marks will be allotted (0).
To calculate the NEET UG scores, candidates can use the following formula:
 
NEET marks = (4 X total correct answers) – (1 X total incorrect answers)
 
By applying this formula, candidates can determine their NEET scores based on the number of correct and incorrect answers they have provided in the exam.

title: NEET UG Result (Out): Download Your Scorecard Now @neet.nta.nic.in
content:
The National Testing Agency has announced the results of the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET-UG) 2023. For all students who took the test, the National Testing Agency (NTA) has made the NEET 2023 result available online. 1145976 out of 2038596 candidates who took the NEET UG 2023 exam were successful in passing. The NEET (UG) is conducted for admission to undergraduate medical, dental, Indian system of medicine, and homeopathy courses in various institutions across India. The examination was conducted on 7th May 2023, with a total of 2,087,462 candidates appearing at 4,097 different centers in 499 cities within the country, including 14 cities outside India.

NEET Variability in Conducting the EXAM

In response to the request from the State Government of Manipur, the NEET (UG) was also conducted in Manipur on 6th June 2023. A total of 8,753 candidates appeared for the exam at 34 centres in 11 cities within the state. The examination was held in 10 other cities, namely Aizawl (Mizoram), Kohima (Nagaland), Dimapur (Nagaland), Shillong (Meghalaya), Guwahati (Assam), Jorhat (Assam), Silchar (Assam), Kolkata (West Bengal), Delhi, and Bengaluru (Karnataka).

The NEET (UG) 2023 was conducted in 13 languages, including Assamese, Bengali, English, Gujarati, Hindi, Kannada, Malayalam, Marathi, Odia, Punjabi, Tamil, Telugu, and Urdu. This multi-lingual approach aims to provide equal opportunities to candidates from diverse linguistic backgrounds.

Recognition in Abroad

The examination was held in various international cities to facilitate the participation of candidates residing abroad. These cities include Abu Dhabi, Bangkok, Colombo, Doha, Kathmandu, Kuala Lumpur, Lagos, Manama, Muscat, Riyadh, Sharjah, Singapore, Dubai, and Kuwait City.

With the announcement of the NEET (UG) 2023 results on 13th June 2023, candidates can now access their scores and rankings. The results will determine their eligibility for admission to undergraduate medical and related courses in the participating institutions.

Candidates who have appeared for NEET (UG) 2023 can access their results on the official NTA website or through the designated result portals.

title: MBBS Admission in Top Medical Colleges in Uttar Pradesh
content:
MBBS is a highly reputed undergraduate program in the field of medicine, with several esteemed medical colleges offering this course in Uttar Pradesh. The duration of the MBBS course in Uttar Pradesh is 5.5 years, including one year of internship.

The admission process for MBBS in Uttar Pradesh is on the basis of the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET). Candidates who have completed their 10+2 education with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology as compulsory subjects are eligible to appear for NEET. Based on their NEET scores, candidates can participate in the counselling process for admission to MBBS colleges in Uttar Pradesh.

While NEET is the preferred entrance examination, some universities in Uttar Pradesh may also conduct their own entrance exams for MBBS admissions. However, NEET scores hold crucial weightage in the admission process. Aspiring MBBS candidates should thoroughly research and verify the eligibility criteria, admission process, and other details of their preferred colleges before submitting their applications to take admission to MBBS in U.P.

UP MBBS Admission 2023
Course NameMBBS
Admission AuthorityDirectorate of Medical Education and Training, Uttar Pradesh
Application ModeOnline
Entrance ModeNEET
FrequencyOnce a year

Explore The Fees Structure of UP Colleges

UP MBBS Admission 2023: Eligibility Criteria

  • Minimum 50% marks in English, Physics, Chemistry, and Biology for the general category.
  • SC/ST/OBC candidates require a minimum of 40% marks in each subject.
  • Physically handicapped candidates (general category) need at least 45% marks in the specified subjects.
  • Residents of Uttar Pradesh or those who passed the 10+2 exams from Uttar Pradesh are eligible.
  • NEET entrance exam appearance and registration for counselling are mandatory.

UP MBBS Admission Process 2023

  • Register on the official website and pay a registration fee of INR 2000.
  • Pay a refundable security deposit via Demand Draft to the Directorate of Medical Education and Training.
  • Verify documents at the designated nodal centres within the given timeframe.
  • Log in and select preferred colleges in order of preference.
  • DMET reviews preferences and releases the seat allotment list.
  • Report to the allotted college before the specified deadline.

Documents Required

  • NEET Admit Card
  • NEET Rank Card
  • Class 10 and 12 mark sheets
  • Uttar Pradesh Domicile Certificate
  • Caste Certificate
  • Printed Online Registration Form
  • Receipt of Registration Fee

  • MBBS fees in Uttar Pradesh are comparatively lower than many other states in India, making it a cost-effective choice for students seeking medical education.
  • Uttar Pradesh offers a rich cultural heritage, creating a lively and enriching student life experience.
  • Uttar Pradesh provides medical students with extensive opportunities to gain hands-on experience and exposure to a wide range of medical conditions.
  • The presence of numerous hospitals and healthcare facilities in Uttar Pradesh opens up excellent careers for medical graduates in both the government and private sectors.

title: NEET UG 2023: Answer Key and OMR Response Sheets are Now Available On neet.nta.nic.in
content:
The National Testing Agency (NTA) has released the Provisional Answer Keys, Scanned Images of OMR Answer Sheets, and Recorded Responses for the National Eligibility Entrance Test (UG) – 2023. The examination was conducted on 7th May 2023, with a total of 2,087,449 candidates appearing at 4,097 different centres across 499 cities in India, including 14 cities outside the country.

To ensure credibility and provide candidates with an opportunity to challenge the provisional Answer Key, NTA has made the materials available on their official website: https://neet.nta.nic.in. Candidates can challenge the Answer Key by paying a non-refundable processing fee of ₹200 per answer challenged. The challenge window is open from 4th June 2023 to 6th June 2023, up to 11:50 PM (IST). Candidates can also contest the OMR grading by submitting representations and paying a non-refundable processing fee of ₹200 per question challenged.

Direct Link to Check NEET UG Rank Predictor

It is important to note that the processing fee must be paid through Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, or Paytm by 6th June 2023, 11:50 PM (IST). Challenges without the receipt of the processing fee will not be entertained, and no other mode of challenge submission will be accepted.

The challenges submitted by candidates will be thoroughly verified by a panel of subject experts. If any challenge is found to be valid, the Answer Key will be revised accordingly, and the revised Final Answer Key will be applied to all candidates’ responses. The result will be prepared and declared based on this final key. It is worth mentioning that individual candidates will not be informed about the acceptance or non-acceptance of their challenges.

NEET UG 2023 Provisional Answer Key: How to Check 

● Visit the official website of NEET at neet.nta.nic.in.

● On the homepage, navigate to the section titled “NEET UG 2023 Answer Key – Download.”

● Click on the designated link to proceed.

● You will be directed to a login page. Enter your NEET application number and password in the respective fields.

● After entering the required details, submit the form.

● A PDF file containing the NEET UG 2023 answer key will be displayed on the screen.

● Review the answer key and compare it with your responses to evaluate your performance.

● If needed, you can download the answer key by clicking on the download button.

● It is recommended to take a printout of the answer key for future reference and further analysis.

title: NTA Releases NEET UG 2023 Exam Dates for Manipur Candidates
content:
The National Eligibility Entrance Test (NEET) is administered by the National Testing Agency. There has recently been an update on the Manipur applicants’ NEET 2023 exam date. The medical entrance exam was initially postponed exclusively for applicants whose exam locations were in Manipur, and the revised date had not yet been disclosed. The NEET 2023 exam for Manipur applicants will now take place on any date between June 3 and 5, 2023, according to the most recent NTA announcement. Candidates from Manipur will soon receive confirmation and a notification of the precise exam date.

The NEET 2023 exam date remained May 7, 2023, for applicants whose exam sites were located in states other than Manipur. Over 20 lakh applicants took the medical entrance exam using pen and paper mode. The NEET UG 2023 exam will take place between 2:00 P.M and 5:20 PM. Aspirants from Manipur must arrive at the designated NEET 2023  exam site by the time specified on their admit card.

NEET UG 2023 Exam Date Time

NTA to Announce NEET UG 2023 Exam Schedule for Manipur Candidates Soon. The exam will be held on any date between June 3 and June 5 and will be conducted using pen and paper.

Will NEET UG 2023 be conducted twice?

According to the most recent information, NTA will stop conducting NEET twice yearly as of 2023. Only candidates for Manipur whose prior tests were postponed will be allowed to take the exam. People can go to nta.nic.in or neet.nta.nic.in to get the most recent information about NEET 2023.

Highlights of NEET UG Exam timings 2023 

The NEET exam for the year 2023 is scheduled to take place on May 7. However, for candidates in Manipur, the exam date has been rescheduled. As per the latest notification from the National Testing Agency (NTA), the Manipur NEET 2023 exam will be conducted on any date between June 3 and June 5, 2023.

The duration of the NEET 2023 exam will be 3 hours and 20 minutes, starting at 2:00 PM and ending at 5:20 PM. It is important for candidates to be aware of the gate closing time, which is set at 1:30 PM. Candidates must ensure that they reach the examination centre well before the gate closing time to avoid any inconvenience.

The question paper will be distributed at 1:45 PM, and the examination will begin at 2:00 PM. Candidates are advised to be present at the examination centre on time and be prepared to start the examination promptly. The NEET examination will conclude at 5:20 PM. These timings and dates are subject to the official announcement and candidates are advised to refer to the NTA notifications for any updates or changes in the schedule

Steps to Change the Exam City

Here are the steps to change the exam city for candidates from Manipur who missed the NEET (UG)-2023  exams due to the law & order situation:

● Exam cities include Aizawl (Mizoram), Kohima/Dimapur (Nagaland), Shillong (Meghalaya), Guwahati (Assam), Jorhat (Assam), Silchar (Assam), Kolkata (West Bengal), Delhi, Bengaluru (Karnataka), and Imphal (Manipur) for candidates from Manipur.

● Through the Interactive Voice Response System (IVRS) feature offered by NTA, the exam city option can be chosen.

● From May 26, 2023, at 7:00 a.m., through May 30, 2023, at 7:00 p.m., the IVRS facility will be open.

● Candidates will receive instructions on how to use the IVRS feature via an SMS on their registered cellphone number.

● Candidates must utilise their registered mobile number in order to access the IVRS service.

● To choose the chosen exam city within the allotted time, follow the directions in the message.

● After selecting the exam city, candidates should wait for the allocation of the exam centre in their preferred city.

● Once the allocation is done, candidates can download their revised admit cards from the official exam portals of NTA for the respective exams.

title: The Ultimate Guide to Choosing the Best Medical College in 2024
content:
Choosing the right medical college after completing your 12th class in the medical stream can be a difficult task given the vast number of options available both nationally and internationally. It is crucial to exercise patience and diligence in selecting the best college, as it will shape your future career prospects. Your college education will enable you to build on your foundational knowledge and develop essential skills for your chosen profession.

To identify the ideal medical college for admission, there are several key factors to consider :

Total expenses: Choosing the best college to study MBBS abroad requires you to consider your budget first. Some colleges offer the complete course expenses, including food and hostel, at a low cost, while others can charge you more than 25-30 lakh for the same facilities.

Mode of teaching: To avoid language barriers and gain a clear understanding of the subject, it is important for students to ensure that the medium of instruction for the MBBS program they are considering abroad is English before taking admission.

Recognition: It is crucial to keep in mind that pursuing an MBBS degree from an international institution is only considered valid in India if the institution is recognized and approved by the National Medical Commission. This is especially important for students who plan to practice medicine in India after completing their degree.

Location of the university: Although it may not be crucial in terms of the quality of education, the location of the MBBS abroad college is still an important factor to consider. This is one reason why countries like the Philippines, Russia, Uzbekistan and Kyrgyzstan are becoming popular choices for MBBS studies abroad, as they offer favorable living conditions and amenities

Duration of courses: Compared to India, different countries have varying education systems that offer flexibility in the course duration. For example, medical universities in Russia, China, and Ukraine have a 6-year course duration. While, in Georgia, medical universities have both 5 and 6 years courses.. In Kyrgyzstan, some universities provide a 5-year course duration. It’s important for students to understand that saving a year in their education is significant, and course duration is a crucial factor to consider when choosing a medical college abroad.

title: MD in Obstetrics and Gynaecology : Admission, Eligibility, Fees and Top Colleges
content:
MD in Obstetrics and Gynaecology is a full-time postgraduate medical course that spans three years. It combines two medical specialities that deal with female reproductive organs, namely obstetrics (during pregnancy) and gynaecology (outside of pregnancy). These two specialities are often combined to create a single medical discipline and postgraduate training program.

The MD Obstetrics & Gynecology course focuses on various topics such as Obstetrics Pathology, Gynecological Obstetrics and Gynecology, and common diseases related to pregnancy. It also covers neonatal hygiene, infant care, and breastfeeding. The course equips students with the necessary skills to manage complicated pregnancies, perform obstetric operations, and handle common disorders related to newborn babies. After completing this course, individuals can pursue a rewarding career in the field of Obstetrics & Gynecology.

In India, there are a total of 404 colleges/hospitals offering the MD Obstetrics and Gynaecology course, with a total of 3552 seats available. The number of seats available for MD in Obstetrics and Gynaecology is not easily available as it is a different course.

Overview

Course LevelPost Graduation
Duration3 years
Examination TypeSemester System
EligibilityMBBS with minimum 50% marks in aggregate
Admission ProcessEntrance Exam + Group Discussion/Personal Interview
Average Course FeeINR 10,000 to 5 Lakhs
Average SalaryINR 3 to 20 lakhs per annum
Top Recruiting CompaniesFortis Healthcare, MAX Healthcare, Care Hospitals, Indian Council of Medical Research, Apollo Hospitals, Kailash Hospital, Adecco India Private Limited, Jaypee healthcare limited, Meditrina Hospitals Private Limited, etc.
Top Recruiting AreasColleges & Universities, Medical Colleges, Govt./ Pvt. Hospitals, Content Writing (Medical), Nursing Homes, Medical Labs

MD in Obstetrics and Gynaecology Eligibility

  • Candidates must have completed an undergraduate MBBS degree from a college or university recognized by the Medical Council of India.
  • The recognition cut-off dates for the medical college’s MBBS Degree courses and compulsory rotatory internship must be as prescribed by the MCI.
  • Candidates required to have completed a one-year compulsory rotating internship in a teaching institution or other institution recognized by the MCI.
  • Candidates must have obtained permanent registration with any State Medical Council.

Meeting these eligibility requirements is necessary to apply for and be considered for admission to an MD in Obstetrics and Gynecology program.

Read Also :

MD in Radiology 

MD in Dermatology, Leprosy, and Venereology

Admission Process

  • Applicants must clear the NEET PG medical exam conducted by NBE.
  • Aspirants must qualify NEET-PG with the required cutoff percentile in individual categories, i.e. 50th percentile for the general category, and 40th percentile for SC, ST, and OBC categories, to get admission in MD Obstetrics and Gynecology.
  • The NEET-PG cutoff for MD Obstetrics and Gynecology is relatively high due to its high demand and limited seat availability in top private and government medical colleges in India.
  • Seats for MD Obstetrics and Gynecology are available under various quotas like All India Quota seats, Management quota seats, Institutional quota seats, and NRI quota seats, etc.
  • Students can opt for these quotas during NEET-PG Counseling.

Career Prospects0

  • Gynaecologists hired in various settings, including nursing homes, government hospitals, private clinics, and pharmaceutical organizations.
  • Postgraduates in gynaecology can pursue private practice or advanced research.
  • Advanced research can be pursued through courses such as PhD in a specific branch of gynaecology.
  • There are various job positions available for individuals with MD Obstetrics & Gynecology, such as Family Physician, Gynecologist Physician, Obstetrician Physician, and Consultant.
  • The average salaries for these job positions are INR 12 LPA, INR 10 LPA, INR 9 LPA, and INR 8 LPA, respectively.

title: MD in Radiology : Admission, Eligibility, Fees and Top Colleges
content:
MD in Radiology is a postgraduate medical program with a duration of three years. This course focuses on the science of radiology, which involves the use of X-rays and other high-energy radiation to identify medical issues and determine the appropriate treatment. A radiologist, who is also a physician, uses radio technology to diagnose medical conditions and provide treatment options.

The program provides in-depth knowledge on various aspects of radiology, such as the production of X-rays, radio physics, interaction of radio with the matter, radiography, special radiography, radiation detection and measurement, among others.

MD in Radiology : Overview

Course NameDoctor of Medicine in Radiology
Course LevelPostgraduate
Duration3 years
EligibilityM.B.B.S + Internship
Entrance ExamNEET - PG
Average Course FeeINR 40,000 Per Annum to 8 Lakhs Per Annum
Job ProfileDiagnostic medical sonographer, MRI Technician, CT scan technologist etc.

Why Pursue MD in Radiology?

  • Many people choose to pursue radiology as a profession because they find the diagnostic challenges interesting.
  • Radiology offers numerous opportunities for professional development.
  • The field of radiology provides knowledge on radiography, radiation protection, image formation, radio diagnosis, CT scan, MRI, and Mammography, among others.
  • Pursuing a postgraduate degree in radiology can also be a suitable path for those interested in pursuing a PhD since it provides ample knowledge about the field.

MD in Radiology Eligibility

  • To be eligible, students must have completed 10+2 education in the science stream with biology and chemistry as major subjects and obtain a minimum of 60% aggregate from a recognized institution.
  • Students must then complete a 5-year MBBS degree or a similar degree in medical science, with at least 55% aggregate.
  • They must also complete a 1-year in-house internship during their MBBS course.
  • Candidates need to pass the NEET-PG or other designated entrance exams with the required marks.

MD in Radiology Admission Process 

  • To pursue radiology, applicants need to clear the NEET PG medical exam conducted by NBE.
  • The aspirants must qualify NEET-PG with the required cutoff percentile in individual categories i.e. for the general 50th percentile and 40th percentile for SC, ST and OBC to get admission in MD radiology.
  • The medical seats are available under different seat quotas like management quota seat, all India quota seat and institution quota seat.
  • The required percentile for the general category is a minimum cut off 50% and for SC/ST minimum cut-off is 40%.
  • Students can opt for these quotas during NEET-PG Counseling.

title: MD in Dermatology, Leprosy, and Venereology: Admission, Eligibility Criteria, and Fees
content:
The Doctor of Medicine in Dermatology, Venereology & Leprosy, also known as M.D. (Dermatology, Venereology & Leprosy), is a three-year postgraduate program that students can pursue after completing MBBS. This program deals with the study of skin, nails, hair, and related diseases. The National Medical Commission (NMC) has released guidelines for a competency-based postgraduate training program for MDs in Dermatology, Venereology & Leprosy.

This program aims to create specialists who can provide high-quality health care and contribute to advances in science through research and training. The program includes required training that helps students recognize the health needs of the community, handle medical problems effectively, and stay aware of recent advances in their specialty. The candidate should possess a broad range of skills that enable them to practice independently, teach medical/para-medical students, and understand research methodology.

In India, there are 106 government colleges that offer MD Dermatology courses. The total number of MD Dermatology seats in government colleges across different states of the country is 455. Among all states, Tamil Nadu has the highest number of MD Dermatology seats with 44 available.

Course Overview

Name of Course MD in Dermatology, Leprosy, and Venereology
Level Postgraduate
Duration of Course 3 Years
Minimum Academic Requirement MBBS degree obtained from any college/university recognized by the Medical Council of India
Admission Process Entrance Exam (NEET PG)

Counselling by DGHS/MCC/State Authorities

INI CET for various AIIMS, PGIMER Chandigarh, JIPMER Puducherry, NIMHANS Bengaluru
Course fees Rs. 40K to 10 Lakh per annum
Average Salary Rs. 5 Lakh to 15 Lakh per annum

Primary Objective of  Course

  • Diagnose and manage common skin diseases, sexually transmitted diseases, and leprosy.
  • Effectively manage medical emergencies related to skin, leprosy, and venereal disease.
  • Implement preventative measures against communicable, venereal diseases, and leprosy at both individual and community levels.
  • Teach necessary knowledge and laboratory skills to medical and paramedical team members.
  • Maintain a compassionate attitude towards patients and their families.
  • Evaluate and initiate investigations to solve problems related to skin, venereal diseases, and leprosy.

Read Also :How to Choose PG specialization after MBBS?How to Secure Admission to Top MD/MS Universities in 2023

MD Dermatology Eligibility

  • To be eligible for admission, candidates must have an undergraduate MBBS degree from a college/university recognized by the Medical Council of India (MCI).
  • The recognition cut-off dates for the medical college’s MBBS Degree courses and compulsory rotatory internship must be as prescribed by the Medical Council of India (now NMC).
  • Candidates are required to have completed a one-year compulsory rotating internship in a teaching institution or other institution recognized by the MCI.
  • The candidate must have obtained permanent registration with any State Medical Council.

MD Dermatology  Admission Process

  • To pursue MD Dermatology, applicants need to clear the NEET PG medical exam conducted by NBE.
  • The aspirants must qualify NEET-PG with the required cutoff percentile in individual categories i.e. for the general 50th percentile and 40th percentile for SC, ST and OBC to get admission in MD Dermatology.
  • Due to its high demand and less seat availability, the NEET-PG cutoff for MD Dermatology is relatively high for top private and government medical colleges in India.
  • Dermatology seats in medical colleges are available under various quotas like All India Quota seats, Management quota seats, Institutional quota seats and NRI quota seats, etc.
  • Students can opt for these quotas during NEET-PG Counseling.

MDs in Dermatology Career Opportunities

  • Career prospects for dermatologists abroad are bright due to the increasing demand.
  • Private practice or working in a clinic helps dermatologists gain confidence and revenue.
  • Experienced dermatologists can have rewarding careers abroad due to increasing demand.
  • Promotion prospects include becoming a medical director or department head.
  • MD Dermatology degree holders are in great demand and hired in private hospitals, nursing homes, or government dispensaries.
  • Dermatologists can set up their own clinics after gaining considerable experience.
  • Initial salary ranges from INR 1.5 to 3 Lakhs per year but can increase up to 5 Lakhs with experience.

title: MBBS in Georgia Eligibility, Fees, and Top Colleges
content:
Looking for a golden opportunity to pursue MBBS abroad? Look no further than Georgia! With over 5,000 Indian students applying each year, MBBS courses in Georgia offer a bright future for aspiring doctors. Universities in Georgia offer scholarships for eligible students with a minimum of 50% in PCB in ICSE/CBSE/State Board and NEET qualification. The best part? There are no capitation fees for MBBS students!

Advantages of studying MBBS in Georgia

  • Georgian medical universities follow UNESCO and WHO quality standards.
  • Georgian MBBS graduates can compete internationally.
  • Modern laboratory facilities enhance clinical skills.
  • Georgia is a safe place for MBBS students, with direct admission and no need for donations or entrance exams.
  • A medical degree from Georgia allows for the United States licensing examination.
  • NMC-approved universities in Georgia offer additional training for NMC screening tests.

Eligibility Criteria for MBBS in Georgia

  • 12th examination with 50% marks in science background
  • 50% minimum marks required for all aspirants
  • SC/ST/OBC category students need at least 45% marks
  • NEET examination is mandatory for admission to Georgian universities
  • Students must have passports

Documents required to pursue MBBS from Georgia

  • Mark sheets for the 10th and 12th examinations.
  • Valid student visas are mandatory for securing MBBS seats in Georgia.
  • Medical fitness documents and travel insurance are required.
  • Birth certificate and passport-size photographs, along with a passport copy
  • Bank statements and an authorization letter are necessary
  • NEET certificate and scorecard, as well as an invitation letter from the concerned university, must be presented.

MBBS Fees Structure in Georgia

The six-year MBBS course in G follows the syllabus pattern of American and European countries, making it globally recognized. With five out of the top 30 European medical institutions located in Georgia, universities here are recognized by UNESCO, WHO, and ECFMG.

But what sets Georgia’s medical education apart is its exceptional teacher-student ratio of 1:20. This encourages teachers to guide each student with care and patience, and enables students to pay more attention to their courses. With a total cost of Rs. 30,00,000 to Rs. 50,00,000 for the entire course, pursuing MBBS in Georgia is an investment in your future.

title: Postgraduate Specializations after MBBS
content:
As a candidate for the medical school exam, you face tough competition. But have you thought about your career options after completing your MBBS degree? Relying solely on your MBBS degree may not be enough to establish a successful practice or secure a position in a top hospital or research facility. To advance your career, you should consider pursuing postgraduate specializations offered by India’s top medical institutes. These specializations can help you stand out and excel in your field.

Why PG specialisation is important after MBBS?

Improve career prospects : After pursuing MBBS it is very important for the students to carry on studies to get specialization in a particular field. it will help to boost their career and will open the door to many opportunities.

Got your promotion and raise : When you are working somewhere without any specialization, then the higher authority doesn’t bother you. The management is always keen to draw maximum benefits from special skills that students will pursue after MBBS .so it is very important for students to pursue pg after MBBS.

Personality development : Pursuing a postgraduate Specializations degree can broaden your horizons and enhance your personal development. With exposure to a wider range of perspectives, you are better equipped to envision the bigger picture and develop more sophisticated solutions.

More financially stable : Having a postgraduate degree can significantly enhance your earning potential in the job market compared to having just a bachelor’s degree. Employers are likely to recognize the added value and depth of knowledge that comes with advanced education and may offer you more lucrative compensation packages.

PG Medical Entrance Exam :

Two types of the medical entrance after MBBS

National Eligibility Entrance Test for Post Graduate (NEET-PG):

To appear for NEET PG, the entrance exam for admission to postgraduate medical courses in India, you must:

  • Have completed MBBS from an MCI-recognized college.
  • no upper age limit.
  • Have completed one year of internship before the exam.

The exam pattern for NEET PG is as follows:

  • It is a computer-based test (CBT) with 200 multiple-choice questions (MCQs).
  • The duration of the exam is 3 hours.
  • The syllabus includes all 19 subjects studied during the MBBS course.
  • Each correct answer is worth 4 marks, while each wrong answer will result in one mark being deducted.

Institute of National Importance Combined Entrance Test (INI CET) :

To be eligible for INI-CET, the national-level entrance exam for admission to postgraduate Specializations medical courses in India, you must:

  • Have completed MBBS from an MCI-recognized institute.
  • Have completed one year of internship before the date of counselling.

The exam pattern for INI-CET is as follows:

  • It is a computer-based test (CBT) with 200 multiple-choice questions (MCQs).
  • The syllabus covers topics from all subjects taught during the MBBS course.
  • The exam duration is 3 hours.
  • There are four sections – A (Basic Sciences), B (Clinical Sciences), C (Preclinical Sciences), and D (Paraclinical Sciences).

Read Also :

How to Choose PG specialization after MBBS?

State-Wise NEET PG Medical Seats in India

Route to pursue specialization after MBBS 

To become a doctor in India, you need to:

  • Score a minimum of 50% in Class XII with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotechnology
  • Qualify for UG NEET exam .
  • Get admission to a medical college through counselling based on NEET rank
  • Complete MBBS degree, internship and housestaffship
  • Qualify for PG entrance exams
  • Secure admission to a master’s degree course based on ranking in counselling process. Specializations and institutes depend on ranking

Eligibility

To be eligible for postgraduate Specializations medical courses in India, you must have completed your MBBS/BDS/BVSc & AH degree, along with the compulsory internship and house staff ship, and hold a valid registration number. There is no age restriction to apply for these courses.

Types of specialization after MBBS

Here are the specializations after MBBS, divided into Medicine and Surgery categories, in bullet points:

Medicine specializations:

  • Radiology
  • General Medicine
  • Pediatrics
  • Pathology
  • Pharmacology
  • Psychiatry
  • Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation
  • Physiology
  • Pulmonary Medicine
  • Anatomy
  • Anesthesiology
  • Dermatology, Venereology & Leprosy
  • Respiratory Medicine
  • Sports Medicine
  • Tropical Medicine
  • Immunohematology and Blood
  • Transfusion
  • Infectious Diseases

Surgery specializations:

  • General Surgery
  • Orthopedics
  • Traumatology & Surgery
  • Ophthalmology
  • ENT (Ear, Nose, and Throat)
  • Obstetrics & Gynecology
  • Otorhinolaryngology

Medical colleges that offer both MD and MS courses in India: (Through INI CET)

The following are some of the top institutions in India that offer postgraduate medical courses:

  • All India Institute of Medical Sciences (multiple locations)
  • Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education and Research (Chandigarh)
  • National Institute of Mental Health and Neurosciences (Bangalore)
  • Jawaharlal Institute of Postgraduate Medical Education & Research (Pondicherry)
  • Sree Chitra Tirunal Institute for Medical Sciences and Technology (Trivandrum)
  • Central Institute of Psychiatry (Ranchi)

title: MBBS in Kazakhstan: Eligibility, Fees and Best Medical Colleges
content:
MBBS in Kazakhstan is a popular choice among medical aspirants due to its top-notch medical colleges recognized by major international organizations such as WHO, USMLE, IMED GMC, and NMC. Several renowned universities to Indian students, providing them with an opportunity to fulfill their medical aspirations.

Kazakhstan is home to some of the best MBBS universities, attracting over 60,000 medical students annually, including 5,000 Indian students. With its world-class medical education and facilities, Kazakhstan has emerged as the #1 national medical university.

Eligibility to study MBBS in Kazakhstan

  1. Candidates must be at least 17 years old.
  2. They must have cleared their 10+2 examination.
  3. Aspirants must have secured a minimum of 50% marks in their 12th examination.
    The subject combination required for MBBS seats is Chemistry, Physics, and Math.
  4. As per the regulations of Kazakhstan universities, candidates must qualify for NEET-UG.
  5. Aspirants must possess a valid passport to study Medicine in Kazakhstan.
    For ST/SC/OBC candidates, it is mandatory to score a minimum of 40% marks in the same.

Admission Process

Step 1: Register on the “Study in Kazakhstan” website.
Step 2: Choose a university, or programme, and apply.
Step 3: Research funding options, including scholarships.
Step 4: Collect the required documents for the university you select.
Step 5: Pass the competitive selection exams.
Step 6: Receive a positive response from the university and apply for a student visa.

MBBS Fees in Kazakhstan

The official website reports that the cost of education MBBS in Kazakhstani universities typically ranges from $800 to $5,500 per year, depending on factors such as region, university, program, and level of education.

content:
Many students are contemplating the option of pursuing MBBS education overseas. Medicine is an extensive and demanding field, and becoming a doctor requires significant dedication, perseverance in academics, and mental balance to handle any circumstance.

In India, medical courses are highly sought-after and rapidly expanding. While it is a challenging field, it is also a well-respected one. The healthcare industry is massive, with approximately millions of job opportunities around the world.

Why study medical courses abroad?

There are numerous reasons to study mbbs abroad. Many countries provide quality medical education at affordable prices. Students from India usually prefer to study abroad because, in India, there is a high demand for fees and donations in medical colleges and also because there are not so many facilities provided by medical colleges. One of the important reasons to study abroad is that, in India, there is high competition in the medical field.

Many foreign countries provide quality medical education. Some are  : 

Russia : 

Russia has emerged as a prominent destination for pursuing medical education in Europe owing to the advanced facilities and technology available in Russian universities.

The admission process for medical courses in Russian universities is relatively straightforward, as it does not require students to appear for any entrance exams.

The Philippines has become a popular choice for Indian students looking to pursuing . Studying at a medical university in the Philippines offers a high-quality education at a lower cost compared to universities in India.

The colleges in the Philippines follow the American format in teaching, which provides students with excellent communication skills, along with quality education.

Kyrgyzstan is a highly sought-after destination for studying MBBS abroad due to its affordable cost of education, with fees ranging between 12-18 lakhs. So students who want to pursue MBBS in Kyrgyzstan can go for it without any second thought.

Bangladesh :

and a significant number of medical colleges offering top-notch medical education for international students.

Kazakhstan offers an affordable MBBS programme, with low tuition fees for students. The medical universities in Kazakhstan renowned for providing high-quality education has garnered global recognition. MBBS in Kazakhstan is one of the great options for students who want to study abroad.

title: NEET UG 2023: Study MBBS in Russia-Eligibility, Fees and Colleges
content:
Study MBBS in Russia has now become the prime alternative of students seeking MBBS in abroad as russia remained a top study-abroad destination for Indian medical students, with a reported 18,039 Indian students studying in the country in the year 2022. Although the Indian Ministry of External Affairs does not specifically track the courses of study pursued by these students, data from the National Board of Examinations (NBE) indicates that in 2021, 4,515 Indian students sat for the Foreign Medical Graduate Examination (FMGE) and 1,119 of them successfully passed the screening test.

Eligibility to study MBBS in Russia

  • Must have obtained at least a 50% aggregate score in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (PCB) subjects in their Class 12 board examination.
  • Should have qualified for the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET) with a minimum score that places them in the 50th percentile or above.
  • Must have a medical clearance certifying that they are free from any contagious diseases.
  • The minimum age requirement is 17 years.

Course Structure

  • In Russia, an MBBS degree is a six-year program, with five years dedicated to academic study and one year for an internship.
  • Dentistry and pharmacy courses last for five years, and nursing courses last for four years.
  • The first three years focus on the theoretical part, while the latter three years focus on practical training.
  • There are 12 semesters in total, with each year divided into two semesters.
  • After completing four years, students receive a bachelor’s degree and a diploma in higher education.
  • Students can continue their studies and pursue a Master’s program.
  • The academic session typically begins between September and October in Russia.

The Admission Process to Study MBBS in Russia

  • Admission to MBBS courses in Russia does not require an entrance exam.
  • Interested students must visit the website of the university/institute they wish to apply to and fill out the corresponding application form.
  • Students are required to submit all necessary documents, such as proof of identity and educational qualifications.
  • University authorities will verify the submitted information and will contact selected students via email.

MBBS Fees in Russia

  • The MBBS fees in Russia for Indian students are lower compared to other countries like the US, UK.
  • The full-time MBBS degree program typically costs can range between 5000 USD to 6000 USD per academic year.
  • Other daily expenses, such as food, transportation and study materials should also be taken into account when budgeting for the study mbbs in Russia.

Best Colleges to Study MBBS in Russia

Here are the popular MBBS colleges in Russia:

  • Altai State University
  • Bashkir State Medical University
  • Volgograd State Medical University
  • Belgorod State University
  • Kazan State Medical University
  • Kursk State Medical University
  • First Moscow State Medical University
  • Pirogov Russian National University
  • Voronezh State Medical University
  • Samara State Medical University

Studying MBBS abroad opens doors to many opportunities to gain an international perspective, experience diverse cultures, and access quality education at affordable cost.

title: Madhya Pradesh NEET PG Medical Seats 2023 | Govt and Private Colleges
content:
The MP NEET PG 2023 counselling is being conducted by the department of medical education (DME) on behalf of the government of Madhya Pradesh. The MP NEET PG/NEET MDS counselling process is usually conducted in two rounds. If there are still seats left vacant after the mop-up round, DME Madhya Pradesh may conduct an additional stray vacancy round. The entire counselling process will be conducted in online mode at the official website, dme.mponline.gov.in.

In Madhya Pradesh, there are a total of 1833 NEET PG 2023 seats available in both private and government medical colleges.

title: State-Wise NEET PG Medical Seats in India
content:
Aspiring candidates who are interested in pursuing MD/MS courses via the NEET PG 2023 examination should be aware of the total number of seats in India. The national board of examinations announced the results of the NEET PG 2023 on March 14th. It is vital to understand the seat allocation process and the state-wise distribution of NEET PG seats. By doing so, candidates can make informed decisions about their choices of colleges and specializations.

The NEET PG exam is a national-level entrance exam for admission to postgraduate medical courses in various government and private medical colleges across India. The seats available in medical education are decided by the Medical Council committee ( MCC) and the respective state governments. The Medical Counselling Committee (MCC) conducts the NEET PG counselling 2023 for 50% of AIQ, Deemed/Central Universities, and ESIC/AFMS institutes, while the respective state counselling authority conducts for the rest of the 50% seats. These seats are then allocated based on the candidate’s rank, choice of college and specialization, and availability of seats.

Therefore, candidates must familiarize themselves with the seat allocation process and the state-wise distribution of NEET PG seats. This information can be found on various official websites and counselling portals. By staying up-to-date with the latest information, candidates can maximize their chances of securing their desired college and specialization.
