title: How to Choose  PG specialization after MBBS?
content:
Choosing a postgraduate ( PG ) specialization after MBBS can be a daunting decision, but here are some factors to consider that might help you to choose the perfect course for you.

  • Personal interests and career goals:  consider what area of medicine you find interesting and enjoyable to study. Just focus and think about long-term goals and which specialities will take you high in your career. 
  • Job prospect: look into the course which has high demand in your country or region. And always choose the course in which you have high interest. and it is fine to change your career path when you realize you are not passionate about it. your interest is the first priority.
  • Availability of training programs: check the availability of the programs for the specializations in which you have a high level of interest.  
  • Work-life balance: it is very important for the aspirants to choose the specialties in which they can maintain the work-life balance. Some specialities demand long hours and on-call duties, while some offer more flexible work schedules. 
  • Salary and compensation:  why do students prefer medical education ? one reason is highly reputed and the other one is it rewards high salary and compensation. so look into the salary and compensation packages for different specialities. Always keep in mind that some specialities may be more financially rewarding than others.
  • Professional development opportunities: Different specialities offer different opportunities. opportunities related to research, teaching and leadership roles.
  •  Evaluate your strengths and weaknesses: understanding your strengths and weaknesses can help in choosing the best specializations after MBBS. Consider the specializations which you explore during the MBBS course. 
  • Go for the most reputed college:  The reputation of the institution where you pursue your postgraduate specialization can also play an important role in your future career prospects. Go for the institution which has a good track of producing successful doctors in your chosen field.

Some Best PG Course after MBBS

 

  •  MD/MS in clinical medicine: it refers to the specialization of the diagnosis, treatment and prevention of diseases and involves a combination of clinical practice and research. 
  • MD/MS in Surgery: it refers to surgical procedures both major and minor, and also includes the combination of practical and research.  
  • MD in Pediatrics: it refers to a focus on the medical care of infants, children and adolescents and also includes treatment and diagnosis. 
  • MD in Obstetrics and Gynecology: it refers to the medical and surgical care of women during pregnancy, childbirth, and postpartum, and includes diagnosis and treatment of gynaecological disorders.
  • MD in Radiology: it refers to the use of imaging technology to diagnose and treat diseases, including X-rays, CT scans, MRIs, and ultrasounds.
  • MD in Anesthesiology: it refers to the administration of anesthesia to patients undergoing surgical procedures and other medical treatments.

Ultimately, the choice of PG specialization will depend on your personal interests, career goals, and the specific opportunities available to you. It may also be helpful to speak with practicing doctors in different specialities and attend medical conferences or seminars to gain a better understanding of the various fields.  

AR group of education have 10 years of experience in NEET PG counseling. Hence if you want to support yourself throughout your journey in NEET PG 2023  just contact the team of AR Group of education. They will guide you through the entire process.

Baba Farid University of Health Sciences has issued the guidelines for the admission procedure in postgraduate degrees degree/diploma MD /MS/PG diploma and six months of training in ultrasonography in the health science educational institutions in the state of Punjab of the 2023 session. Only candidates who have qualified for NEET PG are eligible to apply for admission to both government and private medical colleges in Punjab.

Punjab NEET PG 2023 admission criteria:

 Before applying for the medical courses, candidates must review the Punjab PG medical eligibility requirements. This list includes the requirements for Punjab NEET PG admissions in 2023.

  • Aspirants must have completed an MBBS degree from a recognized medical college.
  • Aspirants are required to get 12 month certificate of internship issued on or before March 31, 2023.
  • Candidates must provide the permanent registration certificate which should be issued by the medical council of India or the state medical council.
  • It is mandatory for the candidates to secure a minimum NEET PG 2023 cutoff.

Punjab NEET PG 2023  Eligibility Criteria 

  • To take admission in postgraduate courses One-year compulsory rotatory internship is required and the candidate should be registered with the state Medical Council/Dental Council.
  • Cutoff date for the completion of the internship will be fixed by the NMC/DCI. However, the condition of a one-year compulsory rotatory internship does not apply to those candidates who have been specifically exempted.
  • The candidates who, after passing the final qualifying examination (MBBS/BDS or the foreign Medical/Dental or a dental degree recognized by GOI/MCl/DCI), are undergoing a 12-month compulsory rotatory Internship/practical training and are likely to complete the same by the date as decided by NMC/DCI, will be eligible for admission provided they complete the compulsory rotatory Internship and obtain provisional or permanent registration. title: NEET PG 2023 Result: Expected to Release Soon
content:
The NEET PG exam for the year 2023 was conducted on March 5th, and it is anticipated that the results will be announced shortly on the official website, nbe.edu.in. More than 2.9 lakh students took part in the exam, which was administered across 902 examination centers in 277 cities on March 5th. The students who clear the NEET PG 2023 exam will be eligible to enroll in MD/MS/PG Diploma and Post MBBS DNB, Direct 6-Year DrNB, and NBEMS Diploma courses.

NEET  PG result 2023: How to Check 

 Step 1: Visit the official site of NBE at natboard.edu. in 

Step 2: Click on the NEET PG 2023 link 

Step 3: Type login credentials and  login 

Step 4: Download the page and keep a hard copy of the same further needed 

NEET PG 2023 Passing Marks

Candidates are required to attain 50th percentile to qualify NEET PG Exam and 40th percentile for SC/ST/OBC to qualify NEET PG exam 2023. 
 
CategoriesCutoff
General50th
SC/ST/OBC40th
General -PH45th
SC/ST/OBC-PH40th

NEET PG 2023: How to Download the Answer Key


To download the NEET PG Answer Key 2023, please follow these steps:

Step 1: Visit the official website of the National Board of Examinations (NBE) at natboard.edu.in.

Step 2: Look for the direct link to download the NEET PG Answer Key 2023 and click on it.

Step 3: A login page will appear on the screen. Enter the required login details, such as your registration number and password.

 Step 4: Click on the “Submit” button.

Step 5: The NEET PG answer key will be displayed on the screen.

Step 6: Check the answer key carefully and note down any objections you may have.

Step 7: If you find any discrepancies, you can raise objections by following the instructions given on the website.

Step 8: After verifying the answer key, download and save a copy of it for future reference.

Step 9: You can also take a printout of the answer key if required.

By following these steps, you can easily download and verify the NEET PG Answer Key 2023.

NEET PG  2023: Counseling Details 

The Medical Counseling Committee (MCC) will conduct counseling for 50% of the All India Quota (AIQ) seats, 100% of the state quota seats, as well as seats in deemed/central universities, ESIC/AFMS, and private institutes. The remaining 50% of AIQ seats will be filled through NEET PG counseling in 2023, which will be carried out by the respective state counseling authorities.

NEET PG 2023: Cut-off Details 

It is important for candidates to note that the NEET PG 2023 cut-off for general category candidates is set at the 50th percentile, while the cut-off for reserved categories is set at the 40th percentile. Last year, the cut-off for the general category was 275 marks, while it was 245 marks and 260 marks for SC, ST, and OBC candidates respectively. For candidates belonging to the general-PH category, the cut-off was 260 marks.

title: NEET PG 2023 TOMORROW: Timings, Marking Scheme and Things to Carry
content:
Tomorrow, the National Board of Examinations (NBE) will be administering the National Eligibility-cum-Entrance Test-Postgraduate (NEET PG 2023) in a single shift. The NEET PG exam comprises 200 multiple-choice questions (MCQs), and examinees are required to finish the exam in three and a half hours. All candidates are required to bring their NEET PG Admit Card 2023 to the examination centres.
The National Board of Examination in Medical Sciences (NBEMS) has notified candidates about the availability of NEET PG 2023 to admit cards through SMS and email alerts. In addition to the NEET hall ticket, aspirants must also bring a valid photo identities card, such as an Aadhaar card, PAN card, voter ID, or driving license.
Candidates must affix a recent passport-sized photograph, taken no more than three months prior, in the space provided on the NEET PG admit card 2023. The NBE has specified that the photograph should be a minimum size of 35×45 mm (and not larger than the box printed on the admit card for pasting the photograph), with at least 75% of the area occupied by the candidate’s face and head.

NEET PG 2023 Exam Timings

The NBE NEET PG exam allows candidates three and a half hours to answer 200 questions. Below are the exam timings for the NEET PG exam:

NEET PG Exam TimingTiming
Candidate Entry Time7: 00 AM
Entry Closes at Examination Center8: 30 AM
Grant Access for Candidate Login8: 45 AM
Candidates Log in to Read Instructions8: 50 AM
Exam Start Time9: 00 AM
Exam End Time12: 30 PM

Candidates are advised to arrive at the exam centre at least an hour before the exam time to complete the necessary formalities.

NEET PG 2023 Marking Scheme

During the NEET PG exam, candidates will have the option to review the questions marked for review before the end of the examination time. It is important to note that zero marks will be given for unattempted questions, and there will be a 25% negative marking for incorrect answers, as per the NEET PG marking scheme.

Here is the NEET PG 2023 marking scheme:

Correct Response: 4 marks
Incorrect Response: 1 mark will be deducted
Unattempted: Zero marks

NEET PG 2023 Exam Day Important Guidelines

  • Candidates must follow COVID-19 guidelines, including social distancing and wearing a face mask.
  • Fever screening will be conducted at the entry point.
  • Candidates with a fever will have to appear in a separate isolation lab.
  • Indian citizens/OCI with a Foreign Medical Graduate Examination Pass certificate must bring the original certificate on NBE letterhead.
  • Candidates without valid ID proof will not be allowed to enter the exam premises.
  • Latecomers will not be allowed to appear for the exam.
  • The reporting counter will close 30 minutes prior to the test start time.
  • The finger biometrics of candidates will be captured during the registration at the exam center.
  • No rough paper or pen will be provided at the exam center.
  • Any suspicious or disruptive behavior may lead to the cancellation of the candidate’s candidature.

title: Last Minute Preparation  Tips and Strategies  for NEET PG 2023
content:
NEET PG 2023 is one of the most competitive medical entrance exams in India, and with the exam approaching fast, it’s essential to make the most of your preparation time. Here are some last-minute tips to help you prepare for the exam.

Revise thoroughly: At this point, it’s important to revise all the topics you’ve covered during your NEET PG 2023 preparation. Go through your notes and revise the concepts, formulas, and theories.

Practice with mock tests: Take as many mock tests as you can. This will help you get familiar with the exam pattern, manage your time better, and identify your weak areas.

Go through the previous question paper:  Refer to the previous year’s question papers to get an idea of the exam pattern and difficulty level. Additionally, many coaching institutes and publishers offer previous question papers and study material for NEET PG exam preparation. You can try practising these question papers to assess your preparation level and identify areas that need improvement.

Focus on your weak areas: Use your mock test results to identify your weak areas and focus on improving them. Spend more time on the topics that you find difficult and try to get a good grip on them.

Manage your time: Time management is crucial during the exam. Practice solving questions quickly and accurately. Divide your time for each section of the exam and stick to it.

Stay calm and positive: It’s natural to feel nervous before the exam, but try to stay calm and positive. Trust your preparation and believe in yourself. Do some relaxation exercises and meditation to help calm your nerves.

Eat well and sleep well: Eating well and getting enough sleep is important for your overall health and well-being. Make sure you eat a healthy, balanced diet, and get at least 7-8 hours of sleep each night.

Read the question carefully: Read the questions carefully before attempting to answer them. Don’t rush through the questions and make sure you understand what is being asked.

Use shortcuts: During the exam, you can use shortcuts to save time. For example, use the elimination method to rule out options that are obviously incorrect.

Don’t get stuck: If you come across a question that you find difficult, don’t get stuck on it. Move on to the next question and come back to it later if you have time.

Stay updated with current affairs: Stay updated with current affairs related to science and technology, medical breakthroughs, and healthcare-related news. This will help you answer questions related to these topics.

 

In conclusion, preparing for NEET PG 2023 requires dedication, hard work, and smart strategies. Remember to stay focused, stay positive, and believe in yourself. Preparing for NEET PG 2023 requires a disciplined approach and consistent effort. With the exam approaching fast, it’s important to make the most of your preparation time. By revising thoroughly, practicing with mock tests, focusing on your weak areas, managing your time, staying calm and positive, eating well and sleeping well, reading the questions carefully, using shortcuts, not getting stuck, and staying updated with current affairs, you can give your best shot at the exam.

title: NEET UG 2023: Registrations likely to begin soon, check details here
content:
The National Testing Agency (NTA) has announced that the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test Undergraduate (NEET UG) 2023 will be held on May 7. However, aspiring candidates are still awaiting information about the registration date for NEET UG 2023. NEET UG is the sole examination for admission to MBBS and related courses across the nation. Registration for the NEET UG 2023 is likely to begin soon.
The NEET UG 2023 application dates will be announced on the official websites nta.ac.in and neet.nta.nic.in. Along with the registration dates, NTA will also provide information about the NEET 2023 exam pattern and syllabus.

NEET UG 2023 Application Process :

In order to register for NEET UG 2023, candidates must follow these steps:

  • Visit the official website neet.nta.nic.in
  • Click on the NEET UG 2023 registration link
  • Register for the NEET UG application by providing personal details
  • Fill in the NEET UG application with qualification details
  • Upload scanned images of your photograph and signature in the specified format
  • Pay the application fee through the online payment mode
  • Submit your application and download the NEET UG application form 2023.

What is the Eligibility Criteria for NEET 2023?

Currently, the eligibility criteria for NEET UG 2023 have not been released. However, according to the NEET UG 2022 Information Bulletin, the candidate must have completed 17 years of age at the time of admission or will complete that age on or before 31 December of the year of their admission to the first year of the Undergraduate Medical Course. To be eligible for NEET UG 2023, candidates must have passed the 10+2 or an equivalent level exam with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Bio-technology, and English as subjects. Additionally, they must have obtained a minimum of 50% marks in the aggregate in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology/Bio-technology in the qualifying examination.

What is the Paper Pattern for NEET 2023?

According to the NEET UG 2022 Information Bulletin, the test pattern of NEET (UG) – 2022 included four subjects, each consisting of two sections. Section A had 35 questions, while section B had 15 questions, out of which candidates were required to attempt any 10 questions. NEET (UG) – 2022 was conducted as a pen and paper-based test.

NEET 2023 Exam Important Dates :

  • NEET exam date for 2023 has been announced by the NTA and it is scheduled to be held on May 7, 2023. Candidates who wish to appear for the exam can refer to the official website, neet.nta.nic.in, for further details. The information brochure will be released soon and will provide all the necessary information regarding the exam, including the application form date, correction window date, admit card date, and result date. It is important for candidates to stay updated about these events in order to avoid missing any major announcements.
  • NEET application form for 2023 will be released along with the information brochure, but the exact date for the same is yet to be confirmed. Candidates will have to follow a five-step process to register for the exam, which includes registration, filling out the application form, uploading images, paying the application fee, and taking a printout of the application form.
  • NEET correction window for 2023 will be available for candidates to rectify any errors in their application form after the registration process is completed. It is important for candidates to keep themselves updated about the specific time period during which the correction window will be available.
  • NEET 2023 admit card will be issued by the NTA at neet.nta.nic.in after the completion of the correction window facility. The admit card will contain important information such as the candidate’s name and details, the allotted exam centre, reporting time, exam day schedule, and important instructions for the exam.
  • NEET UG 2023 exam will be conducted in offline mode on May 7, 2023, and the admit card will be a major requirement for the same. title: Top Medical Colleges In West Bengal
content:
West Bengal, the solitary state with numerous rivers and a diverse amount of history, has always been a topic of discussion for us. Also known as one of mediaeval India’s richest regions. West Bengal has never left any stone unturned in terms of leadership and field. Today, we will be discussing one of India’s most prestigious courses, MBBS. It is a 5-year, 6-month undergraduate medical programme that requires a lot of determination and hard work.

In West Bengal, there are a total of 23 MBBS colleges. There are 16 government and 7 private MBBS colleges among these. To help you avoid confusion and dilemma, the A R Group Of Education is here to assist you in selecting the best college for yourself. In addition, to make your process more convenient.

 

Eligibility Criteria for MBBS in West Bengal

To pursue an MBBS degree, applicants must pass the National Eligibility Cumulative Entrance Test, also known as NEET. Before applying for the entrance exam, make sure you meet the eligibility requirements set by the authorities. The basic eligibility criteria for candidates seeking admission to the list of MBBS colleges in West Bengal are listed below:

 

  • Candidates must be at least 17 years old to be eligible for the NEET-UG.
  • 10+ 2 must have passed/appeared
  • Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotechnology, and English are required subjects in 10+2.
  • Class 12 or equivalent examination with 50% in general, 40% in SC/ST/OBC, and 45% in general-PH.
  • For state quota seats, students must meet the West Bengal NEET domicile criteria.

 

Top MBBS Colleges in West Bengal

  1. KPC Medical College and Hospital, Jadavpur

 

  1. Shri Ramkrishna Institute of Medical Sciences and Sanaka Hospitals, Durgapur

 

  1. IQ City Medical College and Narayana Multispeciality Hospital, Durgapur

 

  1. Jagannath Gupta Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital, Kolkata

 

  1. ICARE Institute of Medical Science and Research and Dr Bidhan Chandra Roy Hospital, Haldia

 

 

West Bengal MBBS Colleges Admission Procedure

To secure a seat in one of West Bengal’s top MBBS colleges, candidates must be familiar with the admission process. The NEET UG scores are used to determine West Bengal MBBS admission. The counselling will be conducted online by the Department of Health and Family Welfare (DHFW) in Kolkata.

 

Candidates should be aware that the DHFW will be in charge of admission to MBBS colleges in West Bengal for 85% of the seats under state quota and 100% of the seats in private medical colleges. MCC will conduct the remaining 15% All India Quota seats from government colleges. The detailed admission procedure is available to students below.

 

  • For admission to AIQ seats in the MBBS course, students must register at mcc.nic.in.
  • To apply for state quota admission to MBBS colleges in West Bengal, students must fill out an online application form at wbmcc.nic.in/ug.
  • The authorities will then release the merit list, which will include the names of students who are eligible for admission to top MBBS colleges in West Bengal.
  • During online counselling, shortlisted candidates must submit their course and college preferences.
  • Authorities admit shortlisted candidates based on their choices submitted by applicants, NEET rank, available seats, reservation, and other criteria.
  • Selected students must report to the medical college of their choice in West Bengal for document verification and the completion of the admission process.

title: MBBS IN BANGLADESH
content:
For aspiring doctors from India, MBBS in Bangladesh has always been the most well-organized and cost-effective option. Every year, nearly 5,000 Indian students travel to Bangladesh to study medicine. The excellent quality medical education, affordable MBBS fees, advanced infrastructure and cutting-edge technology, highly qualified teachers, affiliations with top Bangladesh universities, and comfortable hostel and dining facilities all combine to make MBBS in Bangladesh one of the top choices for Indian students looking to begin their medical careers in Bangladesh. With 25 public universities and 50 private medical colleges in Bangladesh, MBBS in Bangladesh is a good option, particularly for Indian students. It is one of the most affordable MBBS programs in the world for Indian citizens. The NMC passing percentage of MBBS here, which is 27-30%, reflects the quality of teaching at Bangladeshi Medical Universities.

 

Seat reservations for international students, including Indian students, range from 25% to 40% at Bangladesh’s top medical schools. With such a high percentage of MBBS seats reserved, Indian students have more opportunities for excellent medical education. For Indian students, admission to MBBS programs in Bangladesh for the 2022-23 academic year is still open.

 

The Advantages of Studying Medicine in Bangladesh

 

  • Opportunities for Indian Students: Because 25% of seats in Bangladesh’s top private and medical colleges are reserved for Indian students, there are more opportunities for them in Bangladesh.
  • English as a Medium of Instruction: Because the medium of instruction for MBBS in Bangladesh is entirely English, no language training is required.
  • Universities Recognized: Every top medical university in Bangladesh is recognized by the NMC and WHO, making them globally recognized.
  • No Donation Required: There are no donation or capitation fees required for direct admission to the MBBS course in Bangladesh.
  • Admission Entrance Exam: Most medical universities do not require an entrance exam for MBBS admission.
  • Internship Opportunities: Students have the opportunity to complete a one-year internship in some of the best private and public hospitals in the country.

 

How to Select the Best MBBS College in Bangladesh?

Choosing the best MBBS college among the thousands available becomes a difficult task. Let us look at some specific factors to consider when selecting medical schools in Bangladesh:

 

  • Total number of MBBS seats available at the college
  • How old is the medical school
  • What PG options are provided
  • The flow of patients in affiliated hospitals
  • Allied hospital infrastructure
  • Number of required rural service years and bond penalty amount
  • Postgraduate studies have an internal quota
  • Stipend for clinical rotation internship
  • The college’s location

title: Top Medical Universities In Philippines
content:
Choosing which medical school to attend is one of the most important decisions. There are numerous factors that influence our school selection decisions. There are over 60 medical schools in the Philippines to choose from, each with its own set of advantages and disadvantages.

 

English is the medium of instruction in the Philippines. The majority of patients, however, speak both English and Filipino. While the bilingual culture can be intimidating at first, particularly if you are not from here, the language is relatively simple to learn.

 

  1. University of Santo Tomas – Faculty of Medicine and Surgery

For over a century, UST has produced some of the country’s best doctors as the oldest university in Asia. As a result, the college has a well-established system for teaching and managing students. For years, they have produced the greatest number of students with one of the highest PLE passing rates in the country. Since 2003, they’ve used a combination of lecture-based and problem-based teaching methods in their curriculum. Because they are subject-based, you can expect quizzes to be heavily influenced by the professors. The organ systems may also differ depending on the subject. Many professors, however, try to match them to different subjects.

 

The university is located in the heart of Manila, surrounded by affordable living areas and easy access to cheap yet delicious eateries and restaurants. When you want to go out with your friends, you’ll never run out of places to eat. The college also provides numerous opportunities for students to participate in medical missions in impoverished areas in order to make medical care more accessible to these communities.

2.  University of the Philippines – College of Medicine

In the Philippines, the name UP carries a lot of weight. As a result, they have high expectations of their students while also providing special programs for future doctors. They are the first and only school in the country to offer an INTARMED program, which allows high school graduates to begin a 7-year medicine course right away. They also offer a PhD (Molecular Medicine program) for those interested in research and other related fields.

 

Their classes incorporate an organ system curriculum. To put it another way, each system will be discussed in terms of its anatomy, physiology, pathology, and other topics. The Philippine General Hospital (PGH), located in the capital city of Manila, has a diverse patient population. This will better prepare you to recognize and manage the most common and rare illnesses in the country in the future.

 

  1. Cebu Institute of Medicine

CIM is one of the schools with the highest PLE passing rates on a consistent basis. It is also the most important medical institution located outside of Metro Manila. This school is well-known for its emphasis on independent learning. Rather than being constantly guided by a professor, you will spend a significant amount of time pacing yourself and studying on your own.

 

Being in Cebu allows you to spend time outside, away from the bustling cities of the Metro. While Cebu has urban areas, there are also rural areas where the mountains and the beach are only a short drive away if you need a break from your studies.

 

  1. Far Eastern University – Nicanor Reyes Medical Foundation

FEU, which is located in Novaliches, has been producing excellent doctors since 1952. Academically, FEU follows a subject-based flow, as do other schools. Major exams are typically based on the topics covered. These subject exams can occur as frequently as once a month. They usually try to give their students a day to study independently so that they can direct their own learning at their own pace.

 

Like other schools, student life is important in this one. Their student council created a working “trading system” (transcripts of lectures with notes) to aid learning in the classroom and at home.

1) Scholarship advice – We have a thoroughly up-to-date staff to help you choose among the available scholarship program. We will even assist you in obtaining them.

title: HOW IS MBBS IN PHILIPPINES FOR INDIAN STUDENTS?
content:
Regarding medical studies in foreign countries, Indian students have a few options. Among those few countries, the Philippines is one of the safest and most preferable for Indian medical students. In terms of MBBS study, the Medical Council of India has ranked the Philippines alongside China and other European countries.

 

When choosing a foreign country for their MBBS program, the Philippines has become popular among Indian medical students. The Philippines is an English-speaking country, with more than 95% of the population speaking English. Most Indian medical students seek admission to the Philippines after China because most seats in Chinese medical universities were filled by the middle of June, leaving the Philippines as the only option for studying medicine in English at an affordable cost.

 

How is student life in the Philippines’ medical schools?

The Philippines remains one of the most appealing locations for foreign studies, not only because it is an English-speaking country, but also because the country offers a perfect student life.

Aside from affordable medical universities and English language acceptance, a diverse range of natural resources has become a magnet for international students. Young students are always interested in experiencing the country’s natural diversity. The friendly nature of the natives never makes foreign aspirants feel as if they are far from home. The Philipinos are naturally enthusiastic and cheerful people. They are very amusing and enjoy having fun. They are always welcoming to international students. And the country has been named one of the happiest in the world. As a result, pursuing medical studies in the Philippines is a world-class opportunity.

 

Security

Every time, the country provides students with a perfect home away from home feeling. The Philippines are responsible for the familiar culture, awareness, assurance, and security. The country is regarded as one of the safest in the world.

 

Eligibility

After finishing college, a medical student can apply to its MBBS programs. MD programs are available at universities in the Philippines. Furthermore, Indian students who have completed the 10+2 examination system must enrol in the BS course before enrolling in the MD course.

 

Universities

The Philippines is one of the world’s best medical research centres. Some of the country’s colleges outperform universities and colleges in the United Kingdom and the United States.

 

Educational Quality

The Philippines follows the American model of education, and all of them (Filipinos) are very much inspired by methodical studies that encourage extracurricular activities, as opposed to the Indian study technique, which almost entirely revolves around classroom study.

 

Another notable feature of the country’s education system is no capitation fee for admission to study programs. Candidates must, however, pass exams such as the NMAT. This is required as part of core medical study programs such as MD, Pharmacy, and so on.

 

Convenient Fee Structure

The MBBS fee structure in the Philippines is quite reasonable. Another advantage of attending any of the Philippines’ universities is that students are not required to make any kind of donation at the time of admission. They only have to pay the previously announced admission fee.

 

Cost-Effective Living Hood

Every MBBS university in the Philippines offers affordable housing in university hostels. Furthermore, there are many affordable options near the university. title: HOW TO PREPARE FOR THE NEET PG?
content:
NEET-PG is a qualifying and ranking examination held by the National Board of Examinations (NBE) for aspirants wishing to pursue postgraduate Doctor of Medicine (MD), Master of Surgery (MS), and diploma courses in India’s government and private medical colleges. It is a computer-based examination consisting of 200 multiple-choice questions that candidates must answer in two hours and thirty minutes. According to the marking scheme, the candidate receives four points for each correct answer, and one point is deducted for each incorrect answer.

 

Given the examination’s difficulty and competitiveness, now is the ideal time for all candidates to begin their preparation.

 

  1. Regular study: All aspirants must ensure that they can devote at least six to eight hours of their day to their studies. They must divide their daily preparation into four one-and-a-half to two-hour study sessions. This will help to reduce the extra burden while also ensuring that the candidates’ attention span is not compromised. Taking regular breaks between sessions allows aspirants to relax and prepare more effectively.

 

  1. Focus on key topics: Aspirants should concentrate on topics such as Medicine, Surgery, Obstetrics and Gynecology, Preventive and Social Medicine, Pathology, Pharma, and Microbiology during their preparations because these subjects account for approximately 70 to 75% of the questions in the examination.

 

 

  1. Strategic Revision: Aspirants should devote at least one to two hours per day to revising key topics and weak areas to strengthen their concepts. To improve preparation efficiency, aspirants should use a single source of revision for each subject to add all important pointers and notes. Online platforms can assist aspirants in clarifying concepts and providing a brief and concise explanation of the same.

 

  1. Practise papers and mock tests: Because NEET PG is one of the most competitive exams, aspirants frequently experience anxiety and tenseness as the exam approaches. To help alleviate this anxiety, candidates must complete multiple practice tests and answer approximately 100 MCQs per day.

 

They must also concentrate on answering question papers from previous editions of the exam. This will allow them to gain a better understanding of the pattern and topics that will be covered in the exam, allowing them to feel more confident about their preparation.

 

  1. Prioritized Self-Care: Throughout their preparation, all aspirants must prioritize self-care. It is critical to stay healthy to effectively prepare for the exam. Aspirants should not jeopardize their mental or physical health. Exam-related discussions, for example, must take place in groups of no more than three to five people. Late-night studies frequently disrupt aspirants’ sleep schedules and should thus be avoided. Prioritizing self-care boosts motivation and confidence, making it an essential component of any aspirant’s preparation strategy.

 

For more counseling and advice on cracking your NEET PG examination, the A R Group Of Education is here to help you out. We are equipped with highly trained professionals who can guide you through every step. From exam preparation to MBBS Admissions, we have a team of experienced and skilled preceptors who will train you and stand with you in your journey.

title: Explore Jalalabad State Medical University, Kyrgyzstan!
content:
The Jalalabad State Medical University was established in 1992. It is located in Jalal-Abad, Kyrgyzstan. More than 4500 students are served by the university’s excellence in medical education. Professionally trained teachers and professors guide the students. The university has over 250 faculty members, including some well-known doctors. The university offers bachelor’s, master’s, doctorate, diploma, and associate degree programs. Every year, thousands of students from various nationalities come here to pursue a quality education. The university provides students with a safe and welcoming environment. Accommodation for international students is provided in the university hostel. The hostel is well-equipped with modern amenities.

Students can use the hostel’s amenities such as hot water, free internet access, laundry, and so on. The campus has an Indian restaurant that serves delicious Indian cuisine. The college admissions process is simple and convenient. It does not hold a separate entrance exam. Students can obtain a high-quality education at a low cost. The comprehensive infrastructure is well-equipped with cutting-edge technology. The library is a haven for intellectuals and bookworms. Thousands of books are housed in the library, where each student at this medical school can gain wisdom and knowledge. The university’s goal is to provide high-quality education to medical students all over the world. This university was created to educate students in fields such as medicine, electronics, energy, construction, and agro-industrial complex.

Jalalabad State Medical University is quickly becoming one of the best institutions in the country for medical sciences and MBBS studies. Many young students are constantly battling for admission to Jalalabad’s state medical university.

University Highlights
● Jalalabad State Medical University has a long history of educating and preparing students to become highly qualified specialists in their respective fields.
● Currently, 13000 students from various countries study at (JASU), including India, Iran, Israel, Kazakhstan, Nepal, Pakistan, Palestine, Syria, Turkey, Turkmenistan, Turkey, the United States, and Uzbekistan.
● More than 700 teachers are employed, including 15 doctors of sciences and professors, as well as 116 science candidates and others.
● The university has ten science faculties, including pedagogical, medical, and natural studies, among others. Every year, it graduates highly qualified doctors in 54 specialized fields.

Duration of the MBBS program at Jalalabad State Medical University
Medicine studies at Jalalabad State University last six years. These six years are divided into two parts: five years of full-time classroom instruction and one year of an internship following the completion of the course.
Classroom students at Jalalabad State Medical University receive both theoretical and practical training to help them become better doctors.
Students receive assistance from their senior doctors during the one-year internship so that they can gain experience in operations, surgeries, and other important things.
Many Indians are already studying at Jalalabad State Medical University, and many batches have already completed their curriculum at the Kyrgyzstan college.

University MBBS Admissions
Jalalabad State Medical University is one of the most prestigious medical schools in Kyrgyzstan, with students from all over the world enrolling in the MBBS program.
This university accepts international students for MBBS, MD, and BDS programs.
MBBS/MD/BDS students arrive at the university in October and November, and classes begin shortly after the registration deadline.

Ranking of the university:
JALAL-ABAD state university Kyrgyzstan is ranked 29th in the country.
In comparison to an MB or MBBS degree, a degree in medicine is awarded; the university provides a widely recognized and accepted degree all over the world.

Medium of Teaching

The University has an excellent educational standard and low tuition fees, as well as English mediums for international students and highly qualified professors. It is approved by (ECFMG), which stands for Educational Commission for Medical Graduate Students, through which many students from various countries are admitted, and the medium of instruction for foreign students is basic and simple English.

Why Choose Medicine at Jalalabad State Medical University?
The following benefits make the university the best option for everyone:

● Applicants are not required to take any entrance exams prior to being admitted to the MBBS program3 at Jalalabad State Medical University.
● Apart from the tuition fee, the university does not request capitation or donation funds.
● Both boys and girls feel safe on the university campus.
● The low tuition structure in medical universities is a major draw.
● Not only is the tuition fee for the MBBS course low, but so are the living expenses.
● International medical students have access to world-class educational facilities.
● Very safe and interesting study environment
● Senior students welcome new students with open arms and respect their peers.
● Infrastructure is modern and cutting-edge.
● There is no entrance exam to seek admission to the MBBS programme.
● The University provides Indian medical candidates with excellent career opportunities.
● Successful medical graduates can also pursue postgraduate studies.

Eligibility for Admission to the MBBS Program
The University accepts international students twice a year for the MBBS/MD/BDS degree programme. The following individuals are eligible for admission to MBBS/BDS/MD programmes:

● Students with a minimum of 60% in their (10+2) science subjects.
● Should have turned 17 years old.
● Must have passed the NEET exam with a minimum passing percentage.
● HSSC (pre-medical) students can reserve their seats at Jalalabad State University from the start, rather than waiting until the last minute.

Admission Documents to Jalalabad State Medical University for MBBS:
The university admissions process begins in September-October, and nearly 80% of the seats are filled every year in September-October. You only need to pay the application fees and submit the following documents to gain admission to Jalalabad State University:

● Transcript or certificate for SSC
● HSSC (pre-medical) (pre-medical) Certificates and transcripts
● Copy of the first page of a passport
● A new photograph with a white background
● Medical examination results
● Police clearance certificate/report
● After sending these scanned documents to the university’s e-mail address, we will send you an admission letter in 5-7 working days, and your admission process will begin.

MBBS Admission Deadlines
To study medicine in Kyrgyzstan at the Jalalabad State University, there is a deadline that all medical students must meet. Because medical studies begin in September, admission must be completed by the end of July. This gives you plenty of time to apply for a visa and begin studying for your MBBS in Kyrgyzstan.

Hostel Services
Jalal-abad State University provides hostel facilities, including a mess, to international students at a reasonable cost. The hostel rooms are fully air-conditioned and include a bed, study table and chair, lamp, personal bathrooms in each room, and complimentary laundry service at reasonable rates.

Indian food is available
At Jalalabad State Medical University, 80% of students are from other countries, and we take care of each and every one of them. For Indian students, the university has an Indian mess. We understand that the majority of Indians are vegetarians and prefer to eat vegetarian food. Our mess includes an Indian chef who prepares Indian vegetarian food to Indian tastes.

Indian Medical University Students
Jalalabad State Medical University offers Indian students the opportunity to study MBBS in Kygrststan’s Pearl City. Many Indian students have graduated from our institute and gone on to become excellent doctors, serving in a variety of countries. Indian students on campus receive Indian facilities friendly behaviour from other students.

title: EVERYTHING YOU SHOULD KNOW ABOUT NEET PG COUNSELING?
content:
NEET PG is a ranking-based admission exam for MD/MS and PG diploma programs. It is a single-window admissions exam for postgraduate courses offered by the government, private, and deemed universities throughout the country. Every year, a large number of candidates take the national entrance exam. It is managed by the National Board of Education.

 

The Medical Counselling Committee (MCC) has declared the dates for students who have passed the National Eligibility Cumulative Entrance Test-Postgraduate (NEET-PG) exams to be counseled. On August 20th, the counseling process will begin. NEET PG Counselling 2021 is a statewide counseling effort that includes all states, territories, and universities. Counseling for all India 50% quota seats is organized by the MCC on an all-India basis. Admission will be granted only after all eligibility requirements have been met, following which counseling will be offered.

 

Round 1 of NEET PG Counselling 2023

 

Following the release of the NEET PG 2023 seat allotment for counseling round one, candidates must take the steps outlined below based on their status. If an applicant fails to report to the identified institute within the specified time frame, the seat allotted in the initial round of MCC NEET PG counseling will be deleted. This is referred to as a “free exit.”

Accepting the seat allotted in the first round of NEET PG 2023 counseling: If a candidate is delighted with the seat assigned in the first round of NEET PG 2023 counseling, they can report to the assigned institute and secure their place by offering to pay the education money.

 

Round 2 of NEET PG Counselling 2023

 

Candidates in the NEET PG Counselling 2023 Round 2 must meet the following eligibility criteria:

  • Candidates who were not offered a seat in the first round of NEET PG counseling in 2023.
  • Candidates who received seats in the first round of NEET PG admission were denied during physical document verification.
  • Candidates who wish to advance to the second round of NEET PG 2023 counseling.
  • Candidates who chose a free exit or did not attend NEET PG round one counseling

 

NEET PG Counselling Mop Round 2023

  • The MCC NEET PG mop-up round will only be held for admission to deemed, central, and ESIC colleges.
  • Candidates who did not report to their assigned college in round one or round two of NEET PG 2023 counseling will be required to reapply for the mop-up round.
  • Candidates who did not receive a seat in round one or round two of NEET PG counseling will not be required to reapply for the mop-up round. All candidates will have to change their courses and colleges.
  • The official website will announce the mop-up round seat allotment for NEET PG 2023.

 

NEET PG Counselling Seats 2023 State Quota

 

For the surviving 50% state quota seats and 100% seats in private institutes, NEET PG 2023 counselling will be initiated by respective state agencies. After the NBE makes an announcement about the NEET PG result 2023, the state quota counselling process will begin. State quota NEET PG counselling would fill 50% of seats in government colleges and 100% of seats in private colleges in the state. Each state will provide an application form for NEET PG 2023 state admission counselling.

 

Candidates must meet the necessary eligibility criteria, which include a minimum NEET PG cut-off, educational qualifications, domicile, and other factors, in order to participate in state NEET PG counselling 2023. The counselling authority will release the NEET PG state merit list of eligible candidates. Candidates will be expected to fill out their preferences for colleges and coursework, as well as provide necessary documents, during NEET PG counselling for state quota seats. Based on their preferences, NEET PG 2023 state rank, booking requirements, and other variables, candidates will be allotted MD, MS, and PG diploma seats in the state.

 

Seat Allocation for NEET PG Counselling in 2023

 

When the registration process for round one of NEET PG counseling is completed, the DGHS will handle seat allocation. MCC will allocate the NEET PG 2021 seat allotment messages for shortlisted candidates on the official website. NEET PG seat allocation for 2021 will be determined by NEET PG rank, college and course preferences, available seats, reservation criteria, and other factors. Based on these considerations, the NEET PG 2021 counseling outcome will be announced on the MCC website following seat allotment processing. The PDF of the NEET PG seat allocation result for 2021 will be made available. It will include information about the candidate, such as rank, institute, and course.

 

NEET PG Counselling 2023 Documents

The following NEET PG 2023 documents are required:

  • NEET PG admit card – revised date
  • Result
  • Admit card – revised date
  • Result Mark Sheets of MBBS/BDS professional examinations
  • MBBS/BDS Degree Certificate
  • Internship Completion Certificate
  • MCI issued a registration certificate
  • Date of birth
  • proof Valid ID proof (Aadhar; Passport; Voter’s ID; Pan Card; Driver’s License) (if applicable)

 

Please contact the A R Group Of Education for more information and guidance on the subject. We have highly qualified professionals to help you with every detail about NEET PG and the application process.

 

The A R Group Of Education streamlines the application process for students aspiring to be doctors. The initial counselling sessions provided by in-house counsellors address all types of student concerns and questions, clearing the fog that has settled over their minds. Furthermore, our faculty will assist you in deciding which medical speciality you want to pursue in your studies in order to have a successful career in the medical field.

title: Advantages Of Studying MBBS in Philippines
content:
The Philippines is a sovereign state located in Southeast Asia’s western Pacific Ocean. It is a lovely country with beautiful beaches and a tropical climate. Manila is the country’s capital. The country has breathtaking natural beauty and a diverse mix of cultures and communities. Previously, the Philippines was ruled by Spain and America. Filipinos have a strong affinity for fast food, movies, and music.

 

The country with a rich mix of cultural context and breathtaking elegance attracts tourists from all over the world, but it also attracts students from 65 countries for the reasons listed below. There are many excellent universities in the Philippines.

 

The Philippines’ literacy rate

 

The Philippines has the highest literacy rate in Asia, at 94%! That means that the majority of people in the Philippines are educated and of a certain social class. That makes the country remarkable and safe for medical students to study in.

 

The Philippines is an English-speaking country, making it ideal for MBBS in Philippines!

 

English is an international language that is spoken as either the first or second language in the majority of countries around the world. It is also used as a second language in our country, following our native language Hindi, in accordance with international standards. The Philippines, the world’s third-largest economy, is not far behind.

 

The Philippines, the world’s third-largest English-speaking country, is not far behind. English is the medium of instruction at all levels of education, including MBBS in the Philippines. English is the medium of instruction at all levels of education, including MBBS in the Philippines.

 

Students are very safe in the Philippines

 

The Philippines is a female-dominated country, with females predominating in all workplaces and accounting for more than 60% of the population. It is appropriate for both boys and girls. The Philippines is a melting pot of cultures, which makes it a welcoming country, giving students an advantage in not being treated as outsiders or foreigners.

Students who study here feel more at ease in the welcoming environment of the country and are able to concentrate better on their studies.

 

After 10+2, the Philippines offers a DUAL Degree programme for MBBS

 

A dual degree programme is possible with the medicine study programme in the Philippines. Before receiving the degree of MD, students in the BS-MD programme earn a Bachelor of Science (BS) ( Doctor of medicine). BS programmes last between one and a half and two years, depending on the university.

 

Opportunities for advancement are provided by an MBBS from the Philippines

 

With many top universities, the Philippines has become a medical education hub. Students studying here would have a broader perspective. Students who study medicine in the Philippines after graduation have a better chance of finding work in other countries. This is an opportunity to study at a good university while also travelling and learning about a new country. There are also opportunities to travel around the world.

 

Climate

 

The Philippines is known as a Tropical Weather Country, with temperatures ranging from 24 to 34 degrees Celsius all year. It is very easy for Indian students to adjust to the weather there.

 

MBBS in the Philippines is Reasonably Priced

 

The Philippines is a very affordable destination. Low-cost lodging and living expenses. The course fees are significantly lower than in India. There is no donation system.

 

Excellent transportation and air connectivity

 

The Philippines is well connected to the rest of the world, with three international airports in Manila, Cebu, and Davao connecting to all major cities worldwide. Buses, taxis, metros, and other modes of public transportation are available in Manila, allowing students to easily travel throughout the Philippines. India has five countries that connect it to the Philippines by air.

 

Education in the United States

 

The Philippines has a PBL curriculum in the American style. The education system in the Philippines is based on that of the United States. It is simple to pass the USMLE. Many toppers from the Philippines go to the United States for the Residency Program.

 

Religion

 

The Philippines is a predominantly Christian country. Over 90% of the population is Christian. The Philippines is a friendly country because it is a melting pot of different cultures.

 

Indian Cuisine

 

South Indian and North Indian cuisine are both available in the Philippines. In the Philippines, there is no shortage of Indian cuisine. Some medical schools operate an Indian Mess on the university campus. There is also a private hostel with Indian cuisine.

title: Let’s Know About Lyceum Northwestern University, Philippines
content:
Lyceum Northwestern University in the Philippines has a more than 50-year history of offering top-notch MBBS education and academic achievement in South Asia.

 

Every year, students from all over the world come here to pursue their dreams in fields such as medicine, arts and sciences, business education, criminology, architecture, law, engineering, and other vocational studies. University’s MBBS Education is world-renowned for its comprehensive curriculum and research centers. There is a huge group of Indian students who study at the Lyceum Northwestern University campus to pursue their long-held dreams of becoming doctors.

 

The university is dedicated to developing compassionate, knowledgeable, and highly competent physicians by providing early hands-on clinical training and opportunities to hone professional skills. Moreover, they are also well-known for building a community of scholars and professionals dedicated to serving society, as well as assisting students in reaching their full potential as competent, ethical, caring, and inquiring citizens and visionary leaders.

 

Eligibility requirements for admission to the University:

Students in the General Category with a minimum of 50% in physics, chemistry, and biology are eligible to enroll.

Students from the SC and SEBC categories with a 40% aggregate in physics, chemistry, and biology are eligible to enroll in the AMA School of Medicine. In addition to the science marks, students must have a passing grade in English.

 

Weather conditions:

The Philippines has five climates: tropical rainforest, tropical savannah, tropical monsoon, humid subtropical, and oceanic (both in higher-altitude areas), all of which are characterized by relatively high temperatures, oppressive humidity, and abundant rainfall. Based on the amount of rainfall, the country has two seasons: a wet season and a dry season. This is also dependent on where you live in the country, as some areas get rain all year (see Climate types). The warmest months of the year are determined by temperature. The warmest months of the year are March through October, with the winter monsoon bringing cooler air from November to February. The warmest month is May, and the coolest is January.

 

Duration of the course:

For students who have completed class 12 with Science, the total duration of the Medical, MD, or MBBS course is 5 and a half years. The first 16 months are devoted to a pre-medical course. It focuses on laying the groundwork for a solid medical education. The next four years are dedicated to core medicine and clinical rotations.

 

The advantages of Lyceum North Western University are as follows:

  1. Admission does not require a donation.
  2. Admission does not require an entrance exam.
  3. Low college tuition
  4. Fully furnished hostel facility
  5. Canteens serving Gujarati and other Indian cuisines
  6. The library is open 24 hours a day, seven days a week for reading.
  7. cutting-edge technology in a campus hospital for best practices
  8. Wi-Fi is available around the clock.
  9. A secure environment and well-mannered people
  10. The University provides excellent boarding and lodging.

 

Practical Experience:

Medical Courses in the Philippines are generally Practical Oriented, following the pattern of the United States, where the LNU Medical Program is more practical-oriented and provides practical exposure to its medical students through an affiliation with government hospitals and medical centers. LNU students receive training at Region 1 Medical Centre and other Dagupan government hospitals.

It is one of Kazakhstan’s most prestigious top medical universities, with a long list of medical courses available to medical students.

 

Why should you attend the Kazakh National Medical University?

Kazakh National Medical University Courses:

 

Courses include medical science bachelor’s and master’s degrees. Aside from MBBS, you can study nursing, dentistry, and other medical fields.

The Philippines has a literacy rate of 90%. It demonstrates that education is the country’s top priority.

 

The Philippines is the most populous English-speaking country in Southeast Asia. English was made available as a medium of higher education instruction. Furthermore, Filipinos are regarded as some of the world’s most proficient English speakers. It is well-known for its high-quality educational institutions and high-standard education. It grants Doctor of Medicine (MD) and Master of Surgery (MS) degrees, for gaining MD MS admission in Philippines which is equivalent to MBBS in the Indian educational system.

 

 

Why Study Medicine in the Philippines?

title: The Best Guidance for MBBS Admission 2022
content:
Doctors are unquestionably the envy of others, which is why MBBS is one of the most sought-after higher studies worldwide. In the Indian context, MBBS In India is popular not only for the number of universities it has, but also for the quality of education it provides.

It goes without saying that studying medicine in India is a difficult endeavour. First and foremost, let us review the fundamentals of admission to MBBS programmes in India.

Eligibility for Indian MBBS Admission:

 

  • Students must have passed the 10+2 exam with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (Zoology & Botany).
  • For admission through the All India Entrance Exam, the student must be an Indian national (NEET).
  • Students must be 17 or older as of December 31st of the year of admission.
  • After graduating from high school, it is critical to begin preparing for three competitive entrance exams: the National Eligibility Cumulative Entrance Test (NEET), the All India Institute of Medical Sciences for MBBS (AIIMS MBBS), and the Jawaharlal Institute of Postgraduate Medical Education and Research (JIPMER MBBS).

The following factors must be considered in order to secure a medical seat at a reputable government college:

Senior Secondary Examination or Central Board of Secondary Education or its equivalent with PCB marks.

NEET examination results were obtained at the central level.

Aspiring students for AIIMS and JIPMER medical colleges must take a separate entrance examination administered by the respective authority.

It is worth noting that almost every MBBS aspirant in India takes the NEET UG or PG exam in order to be admitted to an MBBS programme. This significant medical entrance examination is held in 154 test centres across India. In 2021, over 16.1 lakh candidates registered for the NEET.

Recognized Medical Colleges in India provide Medical Education in India. The Medical Council of India Act requires all institutions to be recognised. Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery are abbreviated as MBBS. Medical Colleges are educational institutions that offer the MBBS course as part of their curriculum.

 

This course includes basic and pre-clinical subjects such as Anatomy, Biochemistry, Pathology, Physiology, Medicine, and Pharmacology. According to WHO guidelines, it is a four-and-a-half-year programme with a one-year internship for students.

MBBS Exams in India

AIIMS Entrance Exams – All INDIA Medical Science Entrance Exam NEET – National Eligibility Cumulative Entrance Test JIPMER – Jawaharlal Institute of Postgraduate Medical Education and Research

 

  • Candidates seeking admission to MBBS programmes in India must have completed their Class 12th examination with a PCB of 50% for the general category and 40% for the backward category.

title: Admission To MBBS In India
content:
Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) is one of India’s most prestigious courses. Candidates who complete an MBBS degree are eligible to become qualified doctors. Pursuing MBBS is a difficult task because students are tested at each stage of becoming a doctor. Candidates who want to become doctors should be aware of the MBBS application process in India.

Every year, millions of students dream to be doctors. However, only a few are admitted to MBBS programs in India. Obtaining an MBBS seat is a highly competitive process. Every year, over 18 lakh aspirants compete for a seat at India’s prestigious medical college. The first hurdle for candidates seeking MBBS admission is passing the NEET.

 

The NEET exam is given once a year by the National Testing Agency (NTA). According to the Supreme Court of India, NEET-UG is the single national-level entrance examination for MBBS admission in India. The NEET exam also grants admission to 1,899 MBBS seats at AIIMS institutions and 249 seats at JIPMER. Based on NEET scores, 612 medical colleges, 15 AIIMS, and 2 JIPMER institutions offer MBBS admission. When MBBS seats are compared to the number of aspirants who take the exam, the competition for MBBS admission in India is rising exponentially. Many students who score low on the NEET exam struggle to gain admission to government medical schools. Private MBBS colleges, on the other hand, charge exorbitant fees.

 

Due to the obvious high tuition fees in India, many students from the middle class struggle to gain admission to the MBBS programs. These students’ only hope is to qualify for MBBS admission in India if they pass the NEET exam. The NTA screening process for MBBS admission is very rigorous and entirely based on NEET exam merit. Following the announcement of the NEET result, the Medical Counselling Committee (MCC) oversees the MBBS admission process. MCC offers MBBS admission to 15% All India Quota, deemed/central universities, AFMC, and Employee State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) institutes. MBBS admission to the remaining 85% of state quota seats is bestowed with good grades by the respective state counselling authorities.

 

The NTA screening process for MBBS admission is very stringent and entirely based on NEET exam merit. Following the announcement of the NEET result, the Medical Counselling Committee (MCC) oversees the MBBS admission process. MCC offers MBBS admission to 15% All India Quota, deemed/central universities, AFMC, and Employee State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) institutes. The counselling authorities of the respective state grant MBBS admission to the remaining 85% of state quota seats.

 

 

Criteria for MBBS Eligibility

 

In India, the National Medical Commission (NMC) establishes the eligibility criteria for MBBS admission. Only candidates who meet the eligibility criteria are considered for MBBS admission. Candidates who are found to be ineligible at any stage of the MBBS admission process will have their candidature canceled. Examine the MBBS admission eligibility requirements listed below:

 

  1. Academic requirements: To be eligible for MBBS admission in India, applicants must have completed class 12 from a recognized board.

 

  1. Subjects studied: In order to pursue an MBBS program, students must have completed Physics, Chemistry, and Biology in grades 11 and 12.

 

  1. Minimum marks required: Candidates for MBBS admission in India must obtain at least 50% in the qualifying examination. The minimum marks required for MBBS admission from the SC, ST, and OBC categories are 40%.

 

  1. Age: To be eligible for admission to MBBS in India, applicants must be at least 17 years old. The maximum age of a candidate for MBBS admission is 25 years.

B) College selection – We have an updated list of colleges that are perfect for students to study MBBS in India. From suggesting the best college, we provide you with the “done for you type of services” which include-

 

1) Scholarship guidance – We have a completely up-to-date team to guide you to opt for the ongoing scholarship programs. We even help you in the process of availing them.

 

2) Seat locking – We have tie-ups with the top Medical colleges of India through which we ensure that you don’t miss a chance to secure admission to your dream college.

title: What you should know about MCC NEET PG Counseling?
content:
The National Eligibility Cumulative Entrance Test Post Graduate, abbreviated as NEET PG, is India’s primary qualifying entrance and rank-determining exam. Every year, it is held by the National Board of Examinations, which is part of the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India. It is the successor to the All India Post Graduate Medical Entrance Examination (AIPGMEE).

Guidelines:

If a student chooses to pursue the following courses, he or she must take the examination for the same.

  1. Master of Surgery (MS)
  2. Doctor Of Medicine (MD)
  3. Diploma courses from any government or private college in India.

 

 

NEET PG is a computer-based examination, and the subjects tested follow the Graduate Medical Education Regulations syllabus. The exam lasts 210 minutes and was first administered in 2013. It is held once a year, and the student’s score is valid for one year after the results are published. To take the exam, you must have an MBBS degree as defined by the Indian Medical Council Act.

 

There are currently 114 medical colleges and institutes (both government and private) to which you can gain admission through the NEET-PG examination.

 

Criteria for Eligibility

 

Any student pursuing an academic career in India holds a Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) degree (if the degree has not yet been awarded, a provisional pass certificate is required) according to the Indian Act of Medical Council can apply for the exam.

 

A valid Indian or State Medical Council-authorised MBBS registration certificate is also required. Only candidates who have completed a minimum of one year of internship are eligible to apply for the same Examination. For candidates who have not yet finished their training, proof of an upcoming internship program that is nearing completion will suffice.

 

If a candidate has a bachelor’s degree from a foreign medical college, he or she must take the Medical Council of India Screening Test / Foreign Medical Graduate Examination. Only after passing this exam will the student be eligible to take the Entrance Exam for the same.

 

There are two additional primary requirements for students who have graduated from Foreign Medical Colleges in order to apply for PG of NEET. To begin, the candidate must register with the Medical Council of India (Central or State) and complete a full year of internship.

 

Eligibility for NEET PG Counseling

 

MCC will establish a specific cut-off for the year. Candidates who achieve the qualifying cut-off marks set by them will be eligible to attend the NEET Counselling. Though the changes regarding cut-off on an annual basis, it remains close to 275-320 for general candidates and around 250-300 for reserved quota candidates.

 

Procedure for NEET PG Counseling

 

The entire counselling process takes place online. It goes through the following steps:

 

Registration for NEET

 

Candidates who wish to enrol for the NEET PG Counseling process must first register on the MCC’s official website and will be required to register using their official phone number/ office phone number and a valid email address.

Candidates will be given login credentials, which they must use later to complete the registration application form and upload all academic information. Candidates must pay a one-time counselling fee via Net banking after completing the registration application (online payment). The fee is usually divided into two parts: refundable and non-refundable.

 

The counselling fees for a candidate’s deemed universities are higher than the counselling fees for the AQI or Central Universities. More information is available on the website.

 

Selection Filling

 

Following registration, candidates must select their preferred colleges in order of preference. There will already be a list of colleges; you must choose which ones you want and arrange them in the order you prefer. After you’ve finished filling out the choices, save a copy of the list for future reference.

 

 Seat Allocation

 

Following that, the council will undergo the first round of seat allocation. If the candidate is offered a seat at their preferred institute, they may continue with the admission process; otherwise, the candidate may choose a free exit. Following that, MCI begins enrollment for the second round of counselling. In the second round of registration, candidates can create a new preference list in the same way they did in the first. The prior preference list will be considered void for candidates who chose a free exit after the first round.

 

Documents required for the process are the following:

 

  1. Admit Card
  2. Rank Card
  3. Marksheet Of Class X
  4. MBBS Degree Certificate
  5. MBBS Marksheet
  6. Certificate of EWS
  7. Caste certificate
  8. Registration Certificate issued by MCI/SMC
  9. Internship Completion Certificate
  10. Any disability certificate
  11. A valid photo id proof like a Voter id card/ pan card/ Aadhar card/ Passport/ Driving License

 

Process of Document Verification

If the candidate chooses admission, he or she must be available at the respective institute on a specific day for document verification, as announced by the MCI. The personnel of the assigned institutes will verify the documents on this day. Candidates must bring the original documents and self-attested photocopies of the required documents.

 

Seat Allocation Results

Typically, the total number of available seats is divided in half. This division applies to MD, MS, and Diploma programs. One-half of the seats are reserved for Central Government institutes (with a 50% quota on a pan-India basis), while the other half is reserved for state medical council institutes. A centralized counselling process, similar to the NEET UG exam, ensures seat allocation. The merit list is compiled using Score. Reservations are available for the SC, ST, OBC, PWD, and Economically Weaker Section (EWS). There are no reservations in Armed Forces Medical Service Institutions.

The A R Group of Education offers promising MCC NEET PG Counseling services to prospective medical students. We assist you in selecting the best career options by providing you with a personalized report that includes information about your competitive exam scores. We also offer one-on-one sessions in which our expert counsellors assist you in making informed decisions. We will assist you in obtaining admission to the appropriate university.

title: Programs for MD MS Admission in India
content:
MD/MS in India is one of the most popular career options for students interested in pursuing a Post-Graduation Medical course in INDIA. MD stands for Doctor of Medicine, and it entails three years of clinical education in the field of medicine while pursuing an MD. Students receive specialized training to treat patients/diseases using non-surgical procedures, i.e. only medicines.

MS stands for master of surgery, and it entails three years of clinical training in the field of surgery. MS students receive speciality training to treat patients/diseases using various surgical procedures.

 

INDIA MD/MS ADMISSION

 

To be admitted to MD/MS programs in INDIA, candidates must pass the NEET-PG entrance exam, which is administered by NBE each year. To apply for NEET-PG, candidates must have stable or provisional enrollment with MCI and have completed an MBBS degree from an MCI-recognized medical college/institute with a one-year mandatory internship. After passing the NEET-PG entrance exam, students must go through NEET-PG counselling at the Central and State levels.

MCC (Medical Counseling Committee) provides NEET-PG counselling on behalf of DGHS (Director General Health Secretary). Counseling for the All India Quota is done by the MCC.

 

  • With 50% of all government college seats.
  • With 100% of seats available at central universities such as AMU and BHU,
  • With AFMC, DU (Delhi universities), and ESIC hospitals all full,
  • With 100% of seats in prestigious private universities.

 

Process of applying for MD MS in India: 

Candidates must have passed the NEET -PG (National Eligibility Cumulative Entrance Test) for Medical Postgraduate Courses (MD/MS/PG -Diploma). NBE administers the NEET-PG exam online throughout India.

 

Concerning the NBE (National Board of Examinations):

The Government of India established the NBE in 1975 and provides quality education in modern medicine. The primary goal of the NBE is to conduct Postgraduate Medical Examinations across India. It is a completely online mode for the NBE exams. In India, NBE is overseen by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare. According to recent changes in medical entrance exams, NBE is now conducting a single entrance exam for admissions to MD/MS/DNB programmes throughout the country.

title: EVERYTHING YOU NEED TO LEARN ABOUT NEET PG
content:
NEET PG is a computer-based entrance exam for admission to MD/MS and PG diploma courses at various government, private, Deemed/Central, ESIC, and AFMS medical institutions across India. NEET allows students to gain admission to medical schools. The National Board of Education (NBE) administers this exam once a year in an online format across the country for admission to various medical colleges. This exam should be known to all medical graduates who wish to pursue postgraduate studies at the best medical institutions. The exam is not about how many details you consume, how much knowledge you can memorise, and how many facts you can reproduce.

It is more about how picky you are in terms of the information you consume, how much of that information can you revise and re-revise, and how you can wisely reproduce the information on demand. Only about 10-12 thousand of the 1.5 lakh applicants each year are able to secure their desired seat.

However, it is important to note that everyone has worked extremely hard to get to where they are, and yet only 10% of all aspirants make it through.

 

Candidates who meet the eligibility requirements can download the online application form nbe.edu.in. For the registration of the same, candidates must enter their personal, educational, contact, internship, MBBS degree, and other requested information. The NBE will also announce the NEET PG 2023 information bulletin, which will include important details such as exam eligibility, process, pattern, centre, number of seats, and more. Graduates who submit their application forms within the deadline will be issued an admit card for the same.

 

Documents Required for the Exam:

  3. Photocopy of provisional/permanent registration with the State Medical Council/Medical Council of India.

  5. Candidates who have E-Aadhaar cards must ensure that their photograph is clear and fits their face at the time of the exam.
